data.title: Executive Director, Institute for Agriculture & AI
data.company: MBZUAI (Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
MBZUAI is seeking an Executive Director to provide overall vision, leadership, and strategic direction for the Institute for Agriculture & AI (IAAI). The Executive Director is responsible for shaping and executing the Institute’s mission at the intersection of artificial intelligence, agriculture, and sustainability. 
The role oversees strategy development, institutional management, partnership building, and resource mobilization, while representing the Institute to funders, government entities, industry partners, and the global research community. The Executive Director ensures the Institute’s long-term impact, relevance, and financial sustainability. 
Key Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership and VisionDefine and lead the Institute’s long-term vision, strategy, and priorities at the intersection of artificial intelligence, agriculture, and sustainability, in alignment with MBZUAI’s mission and global agendas.Translate complex agricultural and food-system challenges into AI-driven research, innovation programs, and scalable solutions with measurable impact.Position the Institute as a globally recognized center of excellence for responsible and impactful AI applications in agriculture.
Institutional Management and GovernanceProvide executive oversight of the Institute’s research, infrastructure, partnerships, and operational functions, ensuring effective governance, accountability, and performance management.Establish and oversee planning, budgeting, and reporting frameworks to ensure financial sustainability and institutional resilience.Ensure alignment with policies, governance structures, and strategic priorities.
Research Enablement and Cross-Functional CoordinationWork closely with research leadership, data engineers, technical teams, and operations functions to align infrastructure capabilities with scientific objectives and emerging research needs.Provide strategic input into infrastructure roadmaps, technology investments, and capacity planning to support the Institute’s long-term growth and impact.
Partnerships, Fundraising, and External EngagementAct as the primary representative of the Institute to governments, donors, industry partners, and international organizations.Lead resource mobilization efforts, including fundraising, grants, and strategic partnerships that support research, infrastructure (e.g. HPC), and impact objectives
Academic Qualifications RequiredA minimum of advanced degree in technology, development, agriculture, science, economics, public policy, or related fields.PhD is desirable but not mandatory where equivalent senior leadership experience is demonstrated
Professional Experience RequiredEssential:Proven senior leadership experience at the intersection of technology, agriculture, and development, ideally within regional or global, multi-country environments.Significant experience engaging at senior levels with governments, ministers, regulators, private sector leaders, donors, and international organizations.Strong understanding of cultural, political, and socio-economic contexts in low and middle-income countries (LMICs) and their implications for agricultural innovation and technology adoption.Excellent communication, negotiation, representation, and public speakingskills, with the ability to influence across sectors and disciplines.
Preferred:Proven track record in building, leading, and sustaining multi-stakeholder partnerships involving governments, donors, research institutions, industry, and civil society.Experience managing research-to-impact pipelines, including the translation of research outputs into field deployment, policy uptake, or scalable solutions.Familiarity with cross-cutting themes such as gender inclusion, climate resilience, and sustainability within agricultural innovation and development programs.

data.title: AVP, Client Service Engineering - AI Automation
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
The AVP is an execution-focused role, working under the guidance of the VP. The role requires strong leadership, technical acumen, and collaboration skills to drive cross-functional AI initiatives and coordinate with stakeholders across business lines, technology, and support functions.

The AVP will often operate as project lead for specific initiatives or workstreams with stakeholders across Business Lines, Group Technology, DAAI, Risk, Finance, Legal, Compliance, Audit, GCOO and vendors to drive AI automation priorities, intelligent process redesign, and governance. This is an execution-focused role; tangible results in adoption, ROI, scalability, and risk mitigation will be key in defining success.

data.title: Director of Fintech
data.jd_text:
HungerStation is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in over 70+ countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

To lead and oversee HS’s efforts to use technology, in order to drive the development and implementation of financial technology solutions to enhance financial processes, customer transactions, and overall business efficiency.

Fintech

Develop and execute Hungerstation’s financial technology strategy in alignment with business growth, digital transformation, and customer experience objectives.Lead the design and implementation of scalable fintech solutions to support high-volume transactions across customers, merchants, riders, and internal finance operations.Drive innovation in payment systems, digital wallets, refunds, settlements, and reconciliation processes to ensure speed, accuracy, and reliability.Partner closely with Product, Engineering, Data, Operations, and Finance teams to embed fintech solutions seamlessly into Hungerstation’s platform and operational workflows.Ensure full compliance with Saudi regulatory requirements (SAMA, ZATCA, VAT, data protection) and Delivery Hero group policies, while proactively managing fintech-related risks and controls.Oversee fraud detection, transaction monitoring, and cybersecurity measures related to financial systems and digital payments.Own and manage the fintech budget, ensuring cost efficiency, ROI optimization, and sustainable investment in new technologies and partnerships.Analyze financial and transaction data to identify trends, revenue opportunities, cost optimization areas, and system performance improvements.Prepare executive-level financial technology reports and dashboards, providing insights on transaction performance, risk exposure, and innovation impact.Lead and develop a high-performing fintech team, fostering a culture of ownership, innovation, and continuous improvement in line with Hungerstation’s leadership principles.Stay ahead of emerging fintech trends (open banking, embedded finance, BNPL, digital wallets, AI-driven fraud prevention) and assess their relevance to Hungerstation’s ecosystem.Build and manage strategic relationships with banks, payment providers, fintech startups, and regulatory bodies to strengthen Hungerstation’s financial infrastructure.Support business expansion initiatives by enabling new financial products and services for merchants and customers (e.g., faster settlements, flexible payment options, and value-added financial tools).

Strategy & Planning

Contribute to the development and execution of the Division’s ABCs and OKRs, while ensuring alignment with other Functions' heads.Make critical decisions that support the Division's strategic and tactical direction.Establish the plans for the respective functions/teams and provide inputs that support the Division’s goals and objectives. Distribute tactical responsibilities effectively to potential leaders in the Division, to improve the succession readiness of the organization over time. Measure and track relevant OKRs and metrics to ensure the achievement of goals and take corrective actions when necessary.

Governance and Resilience

Follow all relevant policies, procedures, and processes for the daily work to be carried out in a controlled and consistent manner. Contribute to the identification of opportunities for continuous improvement of processes and practices to enhance effectiveness. Uphold a high-performance working environment and promote HS’s Values.

People Management

Set performance objectives, provide necessary support, evaluate direct reports, and provide regular feedback on performance. Participate in the identification and recruitment of key talents for the function. Provide clear direction, prioritize tasks, assign and delegate responsibility, and monitor the workflow. Provide opportunities for team members to suggest, participate in, and contribute to improvement, innovation, and knowledge-sharing initiatives.

Budget Management

Manage and own the budgeting cycle and provide input to the budgeting process. Ensure effective utilization of the function's budget, and report accurately on progress and challenges. Investigate and propose implementing strategies that result in a positive financial impact for HS.Manage the reporting departments effectively within budget, and report accurately on progress made and challenges encountered. Formulate strategies that result in a positive financial impact for HS and aim to mitigate financial and operational risks.

Qualifications

Knowledge and Experience

10 - 12 years of relevant experience. 

Education and Certifications

Bachelor’s degree in finance, Fintech, or a related field.Master’s degree is Preferred.

data.title: Head of KSA, Business Development Climate & Sustainability Services
data.jd_text:
About The Role

Grade Level (for internal use):

12

The Role: Head of KSA, Business Development Climate & Sustainability Services

The Team: The candidate will be responsible for leading S&P Global’s Climate and Sustainability Services market expansion throughout the Kingdom, focusing on three primary client pillars: Financial Institutions, Non-Financial Corporates in hard-to-abate sectors, and Government Related Entities (GREs). This role demands deep client engagement and high-level strategic conversations, requiring the ability to connect across ministries, national champions, regulators, corporates, and financial institutions. Key expectations include building a robust on-the-ground network, meeting with senior executives and project owners, pitching tailored sustainability solutions, and designing offerings that leverage the full range of S&P Global’s data, analytics, platforms, and advisory services. The candidate will play a pivotal role in positioning S&P Global as a trusted partner in climate and sustainability, ensuring that client needs are met with innovative and impactful solutions.

Responsibilities And Impact

Market Expansion & Positioning - Establish S&P Global’s sustainability and climate services in KSA and boost local market presence through ongoing engagement with ministries, corporates, and financial institutions. Build a strong network to enhance visibility and leadership.Client Engagement & Relationship Building - Cultivate lasting relationships with senior decision-makers and engage in informed discussions on climate, transition planning, ESG, and sustainability. Act as a strategic partner for continuous client engagement.Business Development & Growth - Pursue new opportunities with financial institutions, corporates, and government entities. Lead pitches and design tailored solutions utilizing S&P’s offerings. Lead internal teams in preparing proposals aligned with regional priorities.Solution Integration & Delivery Coordination - Turn client needs into actionable projects using data, analytics, and advisory platforms. Coordinate globally for seamless delivery and contribute to RHQ’s balanced onshore/offshore delivery model.

What We’re Looking For

Basic Qualifications: 

Exclusive for Saudi Nationals and be based in Riyadh to ensure essential on-the-ground presence10+ years of experience in a consulting role, ideally covering banking, investment, risk, financing, or sustainability-related areasDemonstrate fluency in Arabic and English, with exceptional business writing and executive-level communication skillsProven experience building or scaling a business, team, or regional market presenceStrong, hands-on experience in sustainability and climate topics, including advisory, technical analysis, or climate-related riskDeep technical knowledge of ISSB, PCAF, GRI, and decarbonization standardsExperience with quantification of financed emissions, such as PCAF methodologiesFamiliarity with local industry dynamics in Saudi Arabia, especially hard-to-abate sectorsExperience in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions quantificationExperience working in international organizations with exposure to global teams and matrix structuresDemonstrated leadership experience, including managing teams, influencing stakeholders, and delivering cross-functional outcomesStrong project management capabilities, able to lead complex sustainability engagements end to end

Preferred Qualifications

Deliver high-quality executive presentations and develop compelling materials using advanced PowerPoint skillsClearly and persuasively articulate complex sustainability concepts to diverse audiencesEngage confidently with high-level stakeholders in both technical and strategic discussionsReport directly to the Global Head of Climate and Sustainability Services.Contribute to the development of a hybrid delivery model by integrating onshore Riyadh-based staff with offshore and nearshore teams to support project executionCommit to regular travel within Riyadh; travel outside the Kingdom is limited and not a priorityDrive business retention and expansion by strengthening client relationships and integrating data, analytics, services, and platforms to support account growth through upselling and cross-selling initiativesLead the transition to recurring and subscription-based models by converting one-off projects into ongoing subscriptions and facilitating client adoption of S&P toolsIdentify and develop new key client opportunities by targeting financial institutions and government entities, expanding market presence in Saudi Arabia, and building a robust pipeline for sustainable growthThis role is exclusively for Saudi nationals. Please note that we do not offer sponsorship or relocation for this position.

About S&P Global Energy

At S&P Global Energy, our comprehensive view of global energy and commodities markets enables our customers to make superior decisions and create long-term, sustainable value. Our four core capabili

With active development across multiple high yield geographies, the firm focuses on the deployment of complex, capital-intensive assets. Operating in a lean, executive-led environment, this role offers the autonomy to build financial structures for 1B+ USD projects and direct accountability to the C-suite. Key Responsibilities Project Finance Architecture: Lead the end-to-end financial structuring for large-scale energy projects. Design and execute SPV/Project Company setups, including the ringfencing of assets to manage risk and optimize capital allocation. Capital Structuring & Debt Management: Manage the debt-equity mix for major developments. Engage with lenders, structure non-recourse project finance, and oversee the financial close of multi-hundred-million-dollar deals. Operational Financial Leadership: Act as the "Project CFO" for designated project entities. Oversee the full financial lifecycle from the development/construction phase through to long-term operational and revenue-generating stability. Risk & Compliance: Implement rigorous financial governance frameworks across multi-jurisdictional environments (specifically focusing on emerging markets). Ensure compliance with international reporting standards and lender requirements. Cross-Functional Coordination: Serve as the primary finance liaison between technical, legal, and operational project teams. Work closely with external financial/tax advisors to manage complex joint venture agreements. Reporting & Strategy: Deliver high-level financial analysis and performance metrics to the board and internal stakeholders, providing the narrative that drives future project investment. Candidate Requirements Experience: 15+ years of progressive finance experience in the energy, infrastructure, or large-scale EPC sectors. Proven track record in managing project-level financials for assets exceeding 200M+ USD. Technical Competence: Expert-level mastery of project finance, financial modeling, treasury management, and corporate structuring. Deep understanding of multi-currency, multi-jurisdiction project accounting. Project Execution: Direct experience in taking large-scale energy or infrastructure projects from Financial Close through to the operational phase. Commercial Mindset: Ability to balance complex financial structuring with the operational realities of working in challenging, emerging-market environments. Communication Skills: Exceptional verbal and written communication. Must be capable of representing the firm in high-stakes negotiations with external lenders, equity partners, and government stakeholders. Personality & Mindset: Entrepreneurial and "hands-on." Comfortable working in a lean environment where taking direct ownership is expected. Culturally adaptable and resilient. Mobility: Willingness to be based in Dubai or Europe, with an appetite for significant travel and potential relocation to project sites in Africa to ensure on-the-ground financial oversight. Education: Strong academic background in Finance, Economics, or Engineering. Professional certifications (CFA, ACCA, CPA) are highly desirable. Please apply by submitting your CV. data.title: Head of Asset Management & AI Transformation
data.jd_text:
Remote | Global Role | Multi-Industry

This is not a traditional asset management role

This role is designed for someone who understands that the future of maintenance and asset management is driven by data, reliability engineering, and intelligent systems.

If your experience is rooted in Reliability-Centred Maintenance (RCM), preventive and predictive strategies, labour optimisation, and cost control, and you are interested in applying this knowledge to AI-driven solutions, this is a unique opportunity to operate at the forefront of the industry.

About MaxAccelerate Technology Group

MaxAccelerate Technology Group is a global technology and transformation business focused on helping asset-intensive organisations improve performance, reduce cost, and scale operations through data, systems, and AI.

Through our ecosystem, including DataMax AI, we work with organisations across Facilities Management, Transportation, Pharma, Manufacturing, Energy, and Infrastructure to transform how assets are managed.

Our approach is grounded in real operational understanding:

 Maintenance strategies must be structured, measurable, and optimised  Systems such as IBM Maximo and other EAM platforms must deliver actionable insight  AI must be applied in a way that drives tangible outcomes across cost, labour, and asset performance 

The Role

We are seeking a Head of Asset Management & AI Transformation to lead the definition and delivery of AI-driven asset management solutions.

The focus of the role is to take deep operational knowledge — including:

 RCM frameworks  Preventive and condition-based maintenance strategies  Workforce planning and labour models  Spare parts, inventory, and asset lifecycle considerations 

and translate these into structured AI use cases and scalable solutions.

Key Responsibilities

 Work closely with Heads of Maintenance, Engineering Leaders, and Operations teams to understand:  Maintenance strategies and maturity  Preventive vs reactive ratios  Labour utilisation and workforce efficiency  Spare parts management and inventory control  Define and structure AI use cases that address:  Reduction in reactive maintenance  Optimisation of preventive maintenance schedules  Labour efficiency and workforce planning  Spare parts forecasting and cost reduction  Asset lifecycle optimisation  Develop and lead Proof of Concepts (PoCs) that validate:  Operational improvements  Cost savings  Performance uplift  Translate business requirements into clear scope of works for DataMax AI R&D teams  Ensure AI solutions integrate effectively with EAM/CMMS platforms (Maximo preferred) and align with real operational workflows  Build clear ROI models, ensuring every solution delivers measurable impact across:  Maintenance costs  Asset uptime  Labour efficiency  Inventory and working capital  Support clients in adopting solutions that improve their infrastructure, operations, and long-term asset strategy 

Why This Role Matters

Most organisations have:

 Maintenance strategies defined on paper  Systems implemented but underutilised  Data available but not fully leveraged 

This role exists to bridge that gap.

You will ensure that:

 Maintenance frameworks such as RCM are not just theoretical, but digitised and optimised  Preventive strategies are continuously refined based on real performance data  Labour and cost structures are aligned with actual operational demand  AI is applied in a way that improves real infrastructure outcomes, not just reporting 

What We Are Looking For

 Strong background in Asset Management, Maintenance, or Engineering leadership  Deep understanding of:  RCM and reliability frameworks  Preventive and predictive maintenance strategies  Workforce and labour optimisation  Spare parts and inventory management  Experience working with EAM/CMMS platforms, ideally IBM Maximo  Ability to translate operational challenges into structured, solution-driven approaches  Commercial awareness and the ability to link technical improvements to cost and performance outcomes 

The Opportunity

This role offers the opportunity to work across industries and influence how organisations manage and optimise critical assets.

You will play a key role in:

 Defining how AI is applied to asset management in real environments  Delivering measurable improvements in cost, performance, and efficiency  Shaping solutions that can be scaled across multiple industries 

Apply

If you are interested in moving beyond traditional asset management roles and want to be involved in shaping the next generation of maintenance and asset optimisation, we would welcome a conversation.

data.title: Executive Director, Structured Credit Investment
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Job Purpose

As part of GM Investments, the Portfolio Manager will drive the build-out and growth of Private ABS investments, with a focus on SME, consumer, fintech-originated receivables, and forward-flow structures.

The role will expand the structured credit mandate, developing scalable exposure to private ABS, warehouse facilities, forward-flow agreements, and securitization take-outs, primarily targeting senior and super-senior risk (AAA/AA/A) within the Bank’s risk appetite.

Working in close partnership with the Head of Investments and Head of Fixed Income Investments, the Portfolio Manager will originate, structure, and manage proprietary investment frameworks, contributing to diversification, recurring income generation, and long-term growth of structured credit portfolios.

Key Accountabilities

 Construct and actively manage portfolios of private ABS, with exposure to SME lending, consumer finance, fintech-originated receivables, and specialty asset classes. Originate and execute forward-flow transactions with fintech platforms, non-bank lenders, and specialty finance companies. Develop and oversee warehouse, prefunding, and term securitization structures, including transition from private ABS to rated public take-outs where relevant. Design robust investment frameworks for private ABS, ensuring compliance with internal risk appetite, regulatory constraints, and capital treatment. Maintain active dialogue with fintech platforms, originators, servicers, arrangers, and rating agencies to source opportunities and manage asset performance. Implement strong risk monitoring, performance analytics, and P&L attribution frameworks for private and illiquid structured credit portfolios. Integrate macroeconomic, relative value, and structural analysis into investment decision-making. Apply advanced modelling and structuring expertise, including cash-flow modelling, stress testing, and scenario analysis for private ABS. Identify new asset classes (e.g. BNPL, merchant cash advance, trade receivables, digital SME lending) consistent with the Bank’s risk appetite

Specific Accountability

As this mandate will lead to new portfolios and asset classes, the Portfolio Manager will:

 Build the end-to-end investment and risk infrastructure (credit, legal, operational, servicing). Establish private ABS and forward-flow governance frameworks within the Bank. Act as a center of expertise on private structured credit, supporting Risk, Legal, Compliance, Treasury, and Finance teams. Ensure scalability from bilateral / private transactions to securitized formats. Maintain strong market visibility and represent the Bank across global private ABS and fintech credit ecosystems.Collaborate with internal teams on: Capital efficiency and regulatory treatment, Liquidity management, Accounting and valuation of private assets

Qualifications & Experience

Minimum Qualification

Minimum 10 years of experience in structured credit, private ABS, or specialty finance

Proven track record in managing portfolios and launching new mandates across structured credit products.

Advanced structuring and modelling skills (cash-flow models, stress tests, waterfall analysis).

Experience Gained At

Alternative Asset Managers, Investment Banks, or Specialty Finance Platforms.

Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills for global market engagement

data.title: Executive Director- Privileged Client Banking
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Company Description

First Abu Dhabi Bank is an inclusive environment where each person values the experiences, perspectives, ideas and beliefs of others. We’re in a unique position to learn from all our colleagues, combining international experience with deep cultural knowledge and local expertise. At FAB, you’ll have the support of your team and a strong relationship with your line manager, who will trust you with responsibility and recognize your good performance. As we embrace different ways of seeing the world, listening to each other and respecting different viewpoints, we grow stronger – together.

Job Description

We are seeking an experienced and visionary Executive Director for our Privileged Clients Group managing Corporate & Investment Banking portfolio in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates. This role will drive strategic growth, oversee complex financial transactions, and maintain strong relationships with high-profile clients in the region.

Develop and execute strategic plans to expand the bank's corporate and investment banking business in the UAE and broader Middle East regionMotivate a team of senior bankers, ensuring high performance and professional developmentOversee complex financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, capital raising, and structured finance dealsBuild and maintain strong relationships with key clients, including major corporations, government entities, and high-net-worth individualsCollaborate with other business units to identify cross-selling opportunities and maximize revenueEnsure compliance with local and international banking regulations and internal risk management policiesRepresent the bank at high-level industry events and forumsProvide strategic advice to the board of directors on market trends, opportunities, and challenges in the corporate and investment banking sector

Qualifications

Master's degree in Finance, Business Administration, or a related field; MBA or CFA certification preferredMinimum of 15 years of experience in corporate and investment banking, with a significant portion in senior leadership rolesProven track record of driving business growth and managing high-performing teams in the banking sectorIn-depth knowledge of corporate and investment banking products, services, and best practicesStrong understanding of global financial markets, economic trends, and their impact on banking strategiesExtensive experience in the Middle East financial markets, with a focus on the UAEExceptional leadership, communication, and interpersonal skillsDemonstrated ability to build and maintain relationships with C-level executives and high-profile clientsStrong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to make strategic decisions in a fast-paced environmentThorough understanding of UAE and regional financial regulations and compliance requirementsFluency in English; Arabic language skills highly desirable

data.title: Head of Client Growth & Relationships
data.location: Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Job Description 
Job Title: Head of Client Growth & Relationship ManagementReports to: Chief Business Officer Role: Full timeLocation: Abu Dhabi 
Company Details 
Our client, a bank – Category 1, recently received its IPA from the Financial Services Regulatory Authority (FSRA) and as part of the preparation for its Financial Services Permission is seeking to recruit a proactive, hands-on and diligent Head of Client Growth & Relationship Management to support the Chief Business Officer to build a scalable, data-driven client growth engine aligned with FSRA regulatory standards and the bank’s digital-first operating model.
Job Purpose 
The Head of Client Growth & Relationship Management will lead the bank’s end-to-end customer growth, acquisition, onboarding, segmentation, engagement, retention, and relationship strategy. The successful candidate will combine deep expertise in digital acquisition, digital onboarding, client segmentation, relationship management, and commercial strategy with strong regulatory awareness in a prudentially regulated (Category 1) banking environment.
This is a senior leadership role requiring commercial acumen, regulatory discipline, strong vendor management capabilities, and hands-on digital execution experience.
Duties and Responsibilities Key Duties & Responsibilities
1. Client Growth & Digital AcquisitionDevelop and execute the bank’s digital customer acquisition strategy across all relevant channels.Lead performance marketing initiatives across all major social media platforms (LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, X, TikTok, YouTube, etc.) and digital advertising channels.Optimize acquisition funnels using data analytics, A/B testing, and performance metrics (CAC, LTV, conversion rates, retention rates).Manage digital campaigns in compliance with FSRA marketing and financial promotion regulations.Drive scalable and cost-efficient growth aligned with the bank’s risk appetite and capital strategy.
2. Digital Customer OnboardingEnsure successful customer onboarding with improvement of onboarding conversion rates .Collaborate with Compliance, Risk, and Operations to ensure onboarding frameworks meet FSRA and ADGM requirements.Continuously enhance user experience (UX/UI) to reduce friction and improve activation rates.
3. Client Segmentation & Value PropositionDefine and continuously refine customer segmentation strategies (e.g., retail, SME, affluent, corporate, sector-based segments).Design tailored value propositions, product bundles, and service models for each segment.Develop differentiated pricing, fee structures, and promotional strategies per segment.Use behavioural, transactional, and demographic data to personalize offerings and improve engagement.Monitor segment profitability and adjust strategies to optimize revenue and risk-adjusted returns.
4. Relationship Management StrategyBuild and lead a client relationship management framework appropriate for a digital bank.Develop retention, upsell, and cross-sell strategies.Establish high-touch relationship models for priority segments.Ensure consistent client communication and experience across all digital and human touchpoints.
5. Third-Party Vendor & Partner ManagementManage relationships with digital marketing agencies, onboarding technology providers, KYC vendors, CRM providers, analytics firms, and other third-party vendors.Negotiate commercial agreements and ensure performance SLAs are met.Ensure third-party arrangements comply with FSRA outsourcing and operational risk requirements.Continuously evaluate vendor performance and optimize cost structures.
6. Commercial Strategy & PricingContribute to defining pricing strategies for all client segments.Design and evaluate promotional campaigns and incentive structures.Support product teams in refining the bank’s overall value proposition.Conduct competitive benchmarking and market analysis.Align pricing strategies with profitability targets and regulatory constraints.
7. Data, Analytics & Performance ManagementEstablish KPIs for acquisition, onboarding, retention, and client engagement.Develop dashboards and reporting frameworks for executive management and the Board.Leverage analytics to drive decision-making and continuous improvement.Ensure accurate reporting in line with internal governance and regulatory expectations.
8. Regulatory & Governance AlignmentEnsure all growth, marketing, onboarding, and relationship activities comply with FSRA regulations.Collaborate with Compliance and Risk to manage conduct risk and customer protection obligations.Embed fair treatment of customers principles in segmentation, pricing, and promotions.Support regulatory reviews, audits, and reporting requirements.
Core Competencies
Strong leadership and strategic thinking skills.Deep expertise in digital acquisition and performance marketing.Proven experience in digital customer onboarding in a regulated financial institution.Advanced knowledge of customer segmentation and lifecycle management.Str

data.title: Enterprise Programs Director
data.jd_text:
The driving force behind our success has always been the people of AspenTech. What drives us, is our aspiration, our desire and ambition to keep pushing the envelope, overcoming any hurdle, challenging the status quo to continually find a better way. You will experience these qualities of passion, pride and aspiration in many ways — from a rich set of career development programs to support of community service projects to social events that foster fun and relationship building across our global community.

The Role

The Enterprise Programs Director will find, land, and execute global enterprise deals. The ideal candidate thrives at the intersection of business transformation and technology enablement—able to engage confidently with C-level executives, guide large delivery teams, and drive high-impact outcomes across global enterprises. This role requires someone who can translate business objectives into structured, executable programs while fostering strong client relationships and leading with influence across matrixed environments.

Your Impact

Strategic Leadership: Direct complex customer engagements, both during the assessment (pre-deal) and implementation phase, providing continuity for our customers and ensuring alignment with corporate objectives and client needs.Stakeholder Engagement: Build and maintain strong relationships with C-level executives and key stakeholders across multiple customer levels.Team Management: Lead large teams of consultants, partners and professional services colleagues, fostering a collaborative and high-performing environment.Program Execution: Ensure the successful delivery of enterprise assessments and deployment programs, managing scope, budget, timelines, and resources effectively. Continuously identify opportunities to enhance program delivery and drive operational excellence.Operating in an advisory capacity to identify key problems, develop and evaluate alternative solutions and make recommendations to key customers and stakeholders.

What You'll Need

The successful candidate will have a minimum of twelve (12) years’ experience with Information Technology initiatives, including formal project leadership experience.At least 4 years of Management Consulting experience at a large firm, with the corresponding skillset: business acumen, structured communication style, ability to synthesize complex information, concisely communicate with executive level presentation skillsExperience with data integration and management platformsOutstanding problem solving and analytical skills to align with the customer on the desired outcomes and be able to convert a solution vision into a tangible execution planStrong analytical skills and ability to translate business requirements into a set of project deliverables and associated tasks.Experience in managing large scale projects impacting multiple business functions at multiple locations, including international.Strong communications skills (written and oral), with an ability to listen and to articulate project status and issues. Communication experience should include presentations at all organizational levels.Team building and management skills, to bring a diverse group together to deliver the required business results through influence and execution.Technical understanding to enable effective delivery of technology projects including new systems implementations and upgrades to existing systems.Good working understanding of the project management lifecycle and the key knowledge areas of project management: integration, scope, time, cost, quality, human resource, communications, risk, procurement, stakeholder.Customer focus - proven track record of customer satisfaction and delivery success and ability to establish and maintain appropriate relationships with business and IT stakeholders.Industry recognized Project Management certification desirable.Familiarity with AspenTech's tools and solution is a plusFluent in English, Secondary language preferred in supporting region

data.title: Senior Program Director (Infrastructure and Transportation)
data.jd_text:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!

At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Guided by our leadership vision of valuing people, embracing agility, and fostering growth, we cultivate an innovative culture that empowers you to achieve your full potential. Unleash your talent and redefine what’s possible.

Job Description

Parsons is looking for an amazingly talented Senior Program Director to join our team! In this role you will provide leadership and direction for every phase of significant infrastructure and transportation programs to assure on-schedule completion within or below budget and in accordance with contractual obligations. The Senior Program Director will plan and define goals and devise methods to accomplish them; develop in-depth knowledge of client objectives, contract terms, and corporate policies.

What You'll Be Doing

Leads, plans, directs, supervises, and controls the execution of all business, technical, fiscal, and administrative functions of the assigned program, project, or subtask.Leads and manages the execution of multiple projects in a mega program.Acts as the company representative with the client and selected suppliers during the program execution. Negotiates changes to the scope of work with the client. Assists business development in understanding client issues and business plans to market and secure new/additional work within the context of role.Leads technical excellence on the project, including furtherance of the Quality improvement processes of the client and Parsons.Mobilizes company resources, through effective liaison with support departments, other offices, or subsidiaries, to create project teams capable of completing effective, quality work.Assigns responsibility for executing project plans to key subordinates after careful assessment of how to utilize their qualifications and strengths. Provides input to performance reviews and development plans for subordinates.Monitors and reports to management on the progress of all project activity within the program, including significant milestones, and any conditions which would affect project cost or schedule.Participate in negotiations with regulatory agencies and in public meetings in support of clients.Oversees establishment of all required project and program Project Management Plans, Health and Safety Plans, Quality Assurance/Quality Control Plans, and other documents as required.Works with the key project staff, consultants, contractors, and subcontractors to devise and execute action plans to rectify potential cost overruns or delays, or to accommodate significant changes to the scope of work.Completes other responsibilities associated with the position as may be appropriate.


What Required Skills You'll Bring

Bachelor's degree in engineering or equivalent from a recognized university essential.Minimum 25 years of relevant experience of significant large-scale projects, in leadership roles.Previous heavy civil engineering project development and construction delivery within infrastructure and transport environment, essential.Considerable experience at senior management/director level in client facing roles is essential.Effective oral and written skills for client presentations, technical reporting, and internal / external correspondence; ability to effectively present information to key stakeholders as required by the clientUnderstanding of the construction management at risk alternative delivery process and the functions inherent to construction management of large-scaled projectsUnderstanding of budget development and cost accounting


What Desired Skills You'll Bring

Aviation experience.Master’s Degree.Chartered Engineer status, PE, PMP/ChPP certification or equivalent.Major earthworks and/or tunneling experience.GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) experience preferred, KSA (Kingdom of Saudi Arabia)Ability to manage and encourage a team of construction management professionalsAbility to interface with all levels with project team, client, and othersKnowledge of construction contract negotiation and administrationAbility to read, analyze and interpret complex documentsAbility to respond effectively to the most sensitive inquiries or complaintsAbility to apply principles of logical or scientific thinking to a wide range of support function problemsAbility to function in a multi-organizational environment by developing relationships that comply with the client’s proactive, collaborative culture.


Parsons equally employs representation at all job levels no matter the race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, disability or genetic information.

We truly invest and care about our employee’s wellbeing and provide endless growth opportunities as the sky is the limit, so aim for the stars! Imagine next and join the Parsons quest—APPLY TODAY!

data.title: Director, Government Affairs & Policy
data.jd_text:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Director, Government Affairs & Policy

Overview

The Director of Government Affairs & Policy (GAP)– Cybersecurity for the Eastern Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EEMEA) region will champion cyber diplomacy and execute both regional and global engagement strategies to foster a secure, inclusive, and innovative digital economy. This pivotal role will engage with governments and establish public-private partnerships across EEMEA, while also advancing Mastercard’s cybersecurity policy priorities on a global scale. The position requires a deep understanding of regional legislative processes, as well as the intricacies of geopolitics and international relations in one of the world’s most dynamic and diverse regions.

The Director will be responsible for coordinating internal efforts across various regions and business functions and liaising externally with industry stakeholders, trade organizations, governments, and multilateral entities, in alignment with the EEMEA GAP SVP and other GAP Leads within EEMEA. This leader will work closely and coordinate with GAP Cluster Leads to represent Mastercard in bilateral and multilateral cyber dialogues, international forums, and strategic partnerships, ensuring that regional insights from EEMEA contribute to and align with the company’s global cybersecurity strategies.

  Advance Mastercard’s global cybersecurity policy objectives with EEMEA governments and stakeholders, reporting to the EEMEA Regional Lead for Government Affairs & Policy. Contribute to the development and execution of global and region-specific cyber policy strategies that are fully aligned with national and institutional objectives of EEMEA countries. Represent Mastercard in regional and international cyber policy fora and carry out multi-country advocacy campaigns to engage key stakeholders and government officials across EEMEA. Monitor political, legislative, and decision-making processes of countries and multilateral organizations in the EEMEA region, providing timely assessments of implications for policy or business operations. Prepare and deliver briefing documents, messaging points, and background materials for advocacy engagements and policymaker meetings relevant to the EEMEA context. Build and maintain trusted relationships with senior government officials, foreign counterparts, and multilateral organizations operating in EEMEA and beyond. Engage with industry partners, governments, and other stakeholders to build coalitions and strategic public-private partnerships that support Mastercard’s cybersecurity policy goals throughout EEMEA. Collaborate internally at the global, regional, and local levels and across business functions (e.g., Government Affairs & Policy, Regulatory Affairs, Security Solutions, Corporate Security, Privacy, AI & Data Responsibility, Communications, Strategic Growth) to ensure unified messaging and alignment between EEMEA and global strategies.


All About You

The ideal candidate for this position should embody a global mindset and thrive in a multicultural environment, adept at building consensus across borders and cultures. Based in Dubai or Riyadh, the Director must demonstrate flexibility, creativity, and resilience as they navigate the rapid transformation and regulatory complexity characteristic of EEMEA.

 A global team member who can work across multiple time zones and cultures, operating independently and constructively as a collaborative team player. High-energy, self-motivated. Strong analytical approach to problem solving, with an ability to develop creative, actionable solutions to complex challenges, adapt to short deadlines, and work productively under pressure in dynamic commercial and policy environments. Willingness to travel extensively within the EEMEA region and globally as necessary to advance initiatives and represent Mastercard at relevant events. A distinguished track record of achievement in public policy, government relations, and/or related government experience, particularly within EEMEA government institutions or relevant international organizations. Thorough understanding of the political, legislative, and decision-making processes of EEMEA countries and multilateral organizations, with the ability to anticipate regulatory trends and assess their impact on cyber policy. A proven record of accomplishment in developing and advancing effective cybersecurity policy strategies, particularly in emerging markets and fast-chang

data.title: FRANCHISEE, Fractional C-Suite and Part-Time Director service
data.company: Boardroom Advisors: Part-Time CEOs MDs NEDs for Scale-Ups and SME's.
data.jd_text:
Background Information
Board Advisors provide an excellent solution for ambitious businesses looking for growth, profitability and stability.
Increasing numbers of senior level people are turning to a portfolio career, seeking to have more control over their lives and freedom from life within a large corporate environment.
Expansion
Our aim is to continue increasing our market penetration throughout the world and therefore, we seek ambitious individuals capable of growing with us as Master Licensees in different countries.
Our Business
We operate within the Scale-Up marketplace servicing companies that ideally have between 10 and 1000 staff and likely $2m - $100m turnover although we do mentor some smaller businesses.
We work directly with the business owners as part of the Board team on a long-term, on-going basis. We also work with VCs, PEs and Funds with their portfolio companies as well as family businesses. Typically, this takes the form of working within each business between 1-10 days a month.
Our approach is to provide the highest quality Board Advisor skill set, with a strong emphasis on strategic and commercial skills, and to pro-actively engage with business owners to deliver terrific value.
We are sector agnostic and provide Director-level expert skills in finance, marketing, sales, IT, HR and Managing. We also supply Non-Executive Directors, Chairs, Mentors and Coaches.
Tasks
What is the arrangement for Master Licensees?
Master Licensees have exclusive territorial rights to using the Boardroom Advisors brand, web site, case studies, business model, marketing systems, processes etc for a period of years. We provide initial induction, training and ongoing support starting with weekly meetings and phasing over to monthly Board Meetings over time where we provide strategic help and advice.
In exchange for helping to get the business up and running, we ask for a small initial commitment fee, a 10% royalty and a 10% shareholding in the licensee company. This ensures long-term commitment from the “parent” company in the success of the licensee.
How do you deliver your services to a business?
Following the initial meeting with a prospect business by a Regional Director,
Boardroom Advisors introduces the appropriate Advisor to the business owner and ascertains the objectives and anticipated outcomes.
Work is on an agreed package or number of days per month; or occasionally the delivery is an initial project with the opportunity to develop into a package or on-going days per month engagement.
For each business there is a lead Advisor and a Regional Director. This team approach helps foster a stronger B2B relationship and business loyalty.
What type of work does Boardroom Advisors undertake?
Our work covers all aspects of a Board Advisor role in a successful business. Our aim is to
establish long-term involvement with a business, generating regular income streams, rather
than work on projects. Engagements are extremely varied and dependent upon the business
needs and the stage of development the company has reached. Some examples of work the
client has as a “trigger” to dealing with us and we might undertake as part of the
appointment as Advisor include:
➢ Strategy development
➢ Performance review
➢ Systems development
➢ Funding analysis
➢ Outsourcing and supplier analysis
➢ Pricing review
➢ Growth planning
➢ Staffing structure analysis
➢ Board salary reviews
Requirements
Who joins Boardroom Advisors?
All our Advisors “have been there, seen it and done it”. They are predominantly well
qualified, all have scaled businesses, been real life Managing Directors or CEOs in business, and all our Advisors get a real buzz out of our clients’ success.
To join Boardroom Advisors, you will need to:
I. Share our values. We believe that successful businesses not only need outstanding commercial skills but require a strong and aligned culture in which to thrive. We are proud of our culture. Prospects, businesses owners, suppliers and our team tell us that we are refreshingly different. Our three core values “transparency, energy and teamwork” provide the foundation on which the culture is built. We seek individuals that are aligned with our culture and values.
II. Demonstrate that you have a strong empathy towards entrepreneurs in the Scale-Up
marketplace as well as VCs, PEs and Fund portfolio companies as well as family businesses. And a strong desire to engage with entrepreneurs and investors to create a position as the “trusted advisor of choice”. We require team members who can work with businesses over the long term with a laser focus on excellent customer service.
III. Have the ability to fund yourself outside of joining our team. We are non-exclusive, no cost and no obligation. It will take time to build your Regional Director and Advisor business base, this could take months before earnings start to flow.
IV. Team energy. We require individuals who can put all their energies into being part of our team. A key as

WE LOVE AVIATION RH Aero Systems is setting the standard for aviation support equipment and services. Through our industry-leading businesses – Rhinestahl and HYDRO – we deliver capability across custom-designed Ground Support Equipment, OEM-licensed engine and airframe tooling, 26 global service centers, and innovative engineered solutions for OEMs, MROs, and Operators worldwide. 
Director Operations Middle East (m/f/d)
YOUR TASKSDevelop deep operational capabilities in the Middle East to support and grow the company’s expertise in the region and globally.Provide leadership to the teams to ensure product quality, reliability, and delivery of commitments on time and on budget.Oversee and expand service business in the region through operational efficiencies, on-time delivery, and customer experience.Drive team performance and collaboration to strengthen relationships with both internal and external stakeholders.Lead and inspire team leads to create and execute strategic plans aligned with their respective functions and targets.Provide regular reports and strategic recommendations to the leadership team.Identify and implement business improvements that enhance cost-efficiency and operational effectiveness.Clearly communicate the company’s value propositions and ensure alignment with organizational processes.Proactively assess and address challenges, ensuring timely resolution of issues impacting operations or strategy.Lead and support change initiatives that foster innovation and continuous improvement across the organization.Promote a culture of compliance, accountability, and ongoing process enhancement.Align with the Director Sales on all customer-related topics and ensure stable load from the market.
YOUR PROFILEDegree in Engineering, Manufacturing, Service Operations, Business, or a related field; Master’s preferred.Strong background in operations management within manufacturing, ideally in the aviation industry.Demonstrated success in managing cross-functional teams with strong leadership and mentoring skills.Strong understanding of engineering and industrial production processes.Experience working in SMEs and matrix organizations.International business exposure with cultural awareness and adaptability.Expertise in project management, productivity improvement, and strategic planning.Exceptional communication, negotiation, and presentation abilities; confident at executive level.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; thrives under pressure.High-level organizational and decision-making capabilities.Represents the company at events and maintains a strong public profile.

data.title: Commercial Director, Middle East & North Africa, Cardiometabolic
data.jd_text:
Working at Abbott

At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and your family, be your true self, and live a full life. You’ll also have access to :

Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of.A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries worldwide and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune.A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as the best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists.

The Opportunity

This position works out of our Dubai location in the Cardiometabolic & Rapid Diagnostics division.

About Rapid Diagnostics Division

Our rapid diagnostic solutions are used in hospitals, laboratories, and clinics around the globe. The crucial information derived from our tests, instruments, and informatics systems is often the first step in patient care decision-making for hundreds of health conditions from heart attacks to blood disorders to infectious diseases and cancers.

The Commercial Director, Middle East and North Africa - CM is accountable for defining and executing the commercial strategy for Abbott Cardiometabolic Diagnostics division across 19 diverse markets within the region. This leadership role is responsible for driving sustainable revenue growth, achieving profitability objectives, and expanding market share across multiple product portfolios.

The incumbent will lead the development and implementation of comprehensive sales strategies, leveraging deep market intelligence and competitive insights to identify growth opportunities and mitigate risks. This position requires a strong ability to translate strategic vision into actionable plans that deliver measurable business outcomes aligned with Abbott’s global objectives

The incumbent leads and mentors a team of Country Leads, Key Account Managers, Distributor partners and technical field personnel, cultivating a high-performance culture focused on flawless commercial execution. The position orchestrates contract negotiations with external customers, ensuring excellence in tendering and contracting processes. Additionally, the Commercial Director fosters an insights-driven culture, informing cross-functional stakeholders of market dynamics and competitive activities to enable strategic decision-making. The role also oversees the implementation of company policies, ensuring operational excellence and compliance across all markets.

What You'll Do-

Commercial Strategy & Execution

Develop and implement regional sales strategies aligned with short- and long-term business objectives.Establish annual sales quotas, forecasts, and pricing strategies, ensuring accurate revenue and profit projections.Drive business growth through acquisition of new customers and expansion of existing accounts.Lead contract negotiations and tendering processes, ensuring competitive and compliant submissions.Monitor market dynamics, competitive activities, and regulatory changes to inform strategic decisions.

Sales Management & Performance Optimization

Plan, direct, and monitor sales activities, providing monthly performance feedback against forecasts.Manage sales administration, customer service, credit collections, invoicing, and ensure timely order processing.Coach Country Leads, Key Account Managers, and field teams on sales techniques, promotions, and execution excellence.Maintain high-level relationships with clients, consultants, and external organizations to strengthen Abbott’s market position.

Leadership & Organizational Development

Foster a diverse and inclusive workplace that empowers all team members to contribute fully toward organizational objectives.Create an entrepreneurial environment, recruiting, coaching, and developing top talent across the region.Keep Abbott’s vision, mission, and values at the forefront of decision-making and daily operations.Demonstrate effective change leadership, ensuring smooth transitions during organizational or market shifts.Build strategic partnerships with internal and external stakeholders to advance departmental and organizational goals.

Operational Excellence & Compliance

Oversee customer service and supply chain operations to ensure efficient product availability and stock flow.Ensure strict compliance with company policies, regulations, and ethical standards.Make operational decisions regarding work processes, resource allocation, and scheduling to achieve departmental objectives.Maintain company standards and procedures across all commercial activities.

Market Development & Insights

Market Development & InsightsGenerate opportunities with existing and new customers, concluding sales calls independently and collaboratively with Marketing.Provide field-based support to commercial partners to drive product sales growth.Assess current and future product opportunities and pursue strategic initiatives to max

Our purpose defines why we exist: to protect, heal and nurture in the relentless pursuit of a cleaner, healthier world. We are a global team united by this purpose. Join us in our fight to make access to the highest quality hygiene, wellness, and nourishment a right and not a privilege. Marketing Marketing is at the heart of Reckitt. With extensive media and digital teams embedded throughout the organisation, we measure both our business and our societal impact. Our global teams lead on our overarching brand strategy, brand guardianship and innovation pipeline in close partnership with R&D. Leaving our local marketing teams to deliver on our regional goals in partnership with our Sales teams. With the scale of our 2500 marketing community - alongside our leading-edge Marketing Academy - your opportunities to grow and upskill will be extensive. Manage the media team ensuring clear direction of work, priorities of tasks, capability development, work life balance and mental wellbeing. data.title: Head of Capital Markets Academy
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Freedom Broker Global Markets is a subsidiary of Freedom Holding Corp. (FRHC), a NASDAQ-listed diversified financial services group.
The company operates under a financial services permission granted by the Financial Services Regulatory Authority (FSRA) of Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM), specialising in brokerage and investment services.
About the Role
The Head of Academy | Senior Lecturer will own the end-to-end learning strategy for finance and financial markets education, including course architecture, content governance, faculty/instructor management, assessments, and certification pathways. The individual will act as the primary business counterpart for the regulator and other external stakeholders to ensure alignment on learning outcomes, standards, and quality assurance.
Key Responsibilities
• Define Academy strategy, operating model, and annual roadmap aligned with regulatory priorities and business needs• Lead development of courses and learning pathways (beginner to advanced) across capital markets, trading, market structure, risk, compliance, and related topics• Establish academic governance: curriculum standards, review cycles, assessment methodology, and quality control• Recruit, onboard, and manage instructors/content developers; oversee delivery formats (in-person, virtual, blended)• Implement measurement: learner outcomes, completion rates, satisfaction, exam performance, and continuous improvement• Ensure professional presentation and learner experience (English/Arabic where applicable) and alignment with relevant regulations and industry best practices
Candidate Requirements
• Extensive academic and/or training leadership experience in finance and financial markets (e.g., capital markets, market microstructure, derivatives, fixed income, portfolio/risk, regulatory frameworks)• Demonstrated experience developing courses/curricula (including learning outcomes, assessments, and instructional design)• Strong stakeholder management, particularly with regulators or standards-setting bodies• Excellent communication and executive-level writing skills; ability to represent the firm externally• Proven ability to lead cross-functional projects and manage multiple deliverables under timelines
Preferred Qualifications
• Native or near-native English and Arabic (strong advantage)• Background from top-tier universities or recognized financial education institutions (preferred but not required)• Relevant credentials (e.g., CFA/FRM or equivalent) are a plus• Experience launching an academy, certification program, or professional training function is a plus
Why Join the Freedom Family?
Be part of a growing, international financial services groupDynamic work culture with strong executive exposureOpportunities to join an expanding team across a developing regional network

Responsibilities About the team: The Cloud Security team is responsible for the security assurance of ByteDance enterprise businesses and the underlying cloud platform, covering areas such as security architecture, SDLC, vulnerability management, security incident response, and security compliance. The team aim to ensure the security and stability of the ByteDance cloud platforms while enabling the success of the cloud business. The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) is responsible for the overall cybersecurity posture of the organization and for ensuring compliance with applicable cybersecurity laws, regulations, and regulatory frameworks, including the National Cybersecurity Authority (NCA) Essential Cybersecurity Controls (ECC) within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (KSA). The CISO will provide independent executive leadership and oversight across cybersecurity governance, risk management, and security operations. The role requires a pragmatic, business-oriented leader with strong expertise in cybersecurity, cloud security, and modern technology environments, capable of ensuring that cybersecurity risks are identified, managed, and reported in alignment with the organization's risk appetite and regulatory expectations. You’ll collaborate closely with senior executives. This role offers the opportunity to shape strategic outcomes, reduce risk exposure, and drive secure innovation across complex, interconnected ecosystems. Cybersecurity Risk Management & Compliance: - Own and oversee the cybersecurity risk management framework. - Ensure continuous compliance with applicable regulations and industry good practices. - Ensure timely remediation of audit findings and regulatory observations. - Act as the primary point of accountability for cybersecurity matters with regulators. Security Operations & Technology Oversight: - Oversee cybersecurity operations including monitoring, detection, vulnerability management, IAM, and incident response. - Ensure effective prevention, detection, response, and recovery capabilities. - Govern the selection

data.title: Director of Business Development, Middle East
data.jd_text:
RETHINK MANUFACTURING 

The only way to ignite change is to build the best team. At Bright Machines®, we’re innovators and experts in our craft who have joined together to create a new category of manufacturing that will help transform the industry. We believe software and data are the answer, thoughtfully applied to solve our customers’ unique challenges. Through intelligent automation, we give factories newfound flexibility, scalability, and resilience. We deliver products to meet the demands of today while building a platform to take advantage of what comes next.

Working with us means you’ll have the opportunity to make lasting, impactful changes for our company and our customers. If you’re ready to apply your exceptional skills to create the factory of the future, we’d love to speak with you.

About The Role

We’re looking for a results-oriented, experienced, and regionally located Account Executive/Director to win and nurture new business for Bright Machines in KSA, UAE and other GCC countries.

Bright Machines is a next-generation technology manufacturer bringing AI and data center infrastructure manufacturing to the edge. Through an integrated platform spanning Virtual Product Development, Software-Defined Automation, and a Unified Data Platform, Bright Machines transforms how complex infrastructure systems are designed, built, and scaled. By connecting design intelligence, programmable automation, and real-time production data, the company shortens time from silicon to revenue, lowers total cost of ownership, and increases the lifetime value of deployed systems.

What You Will Be Doing

Source, shape, and drive new AI and data center infrastructure manufacturing opportunities across KSA, UAE, and the broader GCCLead complex, high-value ($20M+) infrastructure sales cycles from origination through negotiation and closeWin net-new logos and expand strategic accounts through executive engagement, proposal development, and cross-functional coordinationDefine and execute the Middle East go-to-market strategy, prioritizing key segments, partners, and programsBuild executive relationships with regional cloud providers, sovereign entities, telecom operators, and government-backed initiativesDevelop and manage the regional partner and channel ecosystem to accelerate scaleLeverage demand generation and structured sales processes to penetrate and grow target accountsEnsure compliance with local procurement, regulatory, and sovereign program requirementsRepresent Bright Machines at senior industry and government forums across the regionTravel up to 50% as required

What You Will Bring

10+ years of enterprise business development experience in the Middle East with strong knowledge of regional buying dynamicsBachelor’s degree required with advanced degree preferredProven record closing $10M–$100M+ complex infrastructure deals in multi-stakeholder environmentsEstablished C-level relationships across KSA and UAE with the ability to drive executive alignmentExperience selling to Saudi ministries, PIF-backed entities, and leading regional operators including G42, Khazna, Mobily, STC, e&, Equinix, and Moro HubStrong understanding of KSA Vision 2030 priorities, sovereign digital transformation programs, hyperscale and sovereign cloud markets, and AI compute infrastructureDemonstrated success winning net-new strategic accounts and expanding long-term platform engagementsBackground in datacenter, automation, advanced manufacturing, or AI infrastructure environmentsCollaborative, self-directed operator who delivers results in a distributed

IT WOULD BE GREAT IF YOU HAD

Arabic language fluency (not mandatory but strong plus)Prior experience selling Datacenter services, AI infrastructure, automation and/or manufacturing Direct experience with Datacenter expansion and colocation services Familiarity with sovereign cloud requirements and data regulations Experience scaling early-stage technology into GCC Experience at a high-growth technology, robotics, automation, or deep-tech company 

BE EMPOWERED TO CHANGE AN INDUSTRY

Bright Machines is a next-generation, AI-enabled manufacturer focused on data center infrastructure assembly operations. Bright Machines uses its proprietary AI-based robotics and software to assemble AI infrastructure hardware products (i.e., data center servers) for hyperscalers and leading Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs). With its new AI factory, Bright Machines addresses increasing market demands for computing power due to the surge of AI and the U.S. national mandate to reshore manufacturing by building data center infrastructure at scale with higher quality and shorter time-to-market.

Bright Machines is headquartered in San Francisco, California, with an integration center in Guadalajara, Mexico. The company has been recognized as one of Forbes’ AI 50, awarded “Best AI-based Solution for Manufacturing” by AI Breakthrough, named a “Technology Pioneer” by the World Economic Forum, and highlig

data.title: Project Director. LSTK EPC Power. KSA
data.jd_text:
Técnicas Reunidas group (TR) is a leading Oil & Gas International Engineering and Construction Company specialized in the design, construction and management of execution of Industrial plants worldwide. We participate in projects related to hydrogen, biomass, biofuels, decarbonisation, artificial intelligence, digitization.The sustainable growth, decarbonization and energy transition are the concepts that represent our culture.Our high level of professionalism and commitment drive us to a permanent research to improve efficiency and sustainability.At Tecnicas Reunidas, we have a strong commitment to equal opportunities between men and women, as well as diversity. To achieve this, we carry out rigorous selection processes that ensure it.
Description:We are looking for a Project Director, to be part of our Project Management Team and for our Power EPC Projects, being responsible for:
Contractor representative and ultimate project responsible to the Client and Company.Main contact person with official Bodies, Entities, Partners and other Stakeholders involved in the Project.To define the extent and nature of the services to be covered according to the contract scope of work.To manage and control the project execution in all the relevant aspects and different stages to ensure that the project targets related to cost, schedule, quality and HSE are reached.To define the project organization chart and, supported by the different departments of the Company, mobilize to the Task Force the necessary resources to cope with the project scope, including the Client teamTo lead, coordinate and motivate multicultural and multidisciplinary work teams to achieve the partial / final project milestones.To ensure the early submission of the different project plans and procedures in accordance with Client specifications and requirements.To implement the communication plan and meetings schedule, either with the Client or internal, to revise the project status on weekly/monthly basis.To promote Value Improvement and Constructability Methodologies during detailed Engineering.To approve the Procurement and Subcontracting strategy, based on potential risk analysis, capacity, experience and workload of vendors and subcontractors.To monitor the schedule and cost control to avoid project delays and unexpected impacts in the cashflow.To identify and guarantee that QA/QC and HSE requirements are implemented and periodically get feedback from Client about performance on these subjects.To report to the Client and Company about the project progress, alerting on potential risks and implementing corrective plans/actions to mitigate them.To establish the Management of Change in the project and to identify potential Change Orders / Contract Variations to negotiate with Client.To manage the close-out of the Contract, either in the technical or financial terms.
Requirements for the position:Project Director with more than 15 years of working experience, at least 5 of them in Power Plants project execution.BSc in Industrial Engineering or equivalent.Relevant Experience in EPC LSTK projects developed on EPC Main Contractors.Willingness to travel. Candidates shall be ready to perform part of the work in other countries as required (worldwide mobility applies to both short and long-term assignments 1-2 years).

Our mission is to ring every doorbell, everyday. We want to redefine what technology can do in this region, and we’re looking for a Head of Creative Strategy who can help us move even faster. What you’ll do: The Creative Strategy function sits at the core of how noon shows up to customers — driving high-impact campaigns, creative execution, and brand storytelling across all markets and platforms. This role requires someone who can own end-to-end creative output, from ideation through to execution, and translate business priorities into campaigns that drive growth. You’ll work closely with the CEO, acting as a creative operator, strategist, and force multiplier. You will lead campaign development, shape creative direction, and ensure execution across digital, media, and on-ground channels is best-in-class. This role is built for someone with real creative leadership instinct, sharp thinking, and the ability to operate in fast-paced, high-pressure environments. You should be able to develop strong campaign ideas independently and drive them through to execution with high standards. Key responsibilities include: Lead the development of breakthrough campaign ideas-from concept through to execution Own end-to-end campaign delivery across digital ads, media, social, and key commercial moments Translate business priorities into high-impact creative platforms that drive measurable outcomes Drive hero campaigns across key moments (Ramadan, sale events, growth pushes) Oversee creative production including ad films, digital content, and campaign assets Bring strong filmmaking sensibility — storytelling, pacing, and visual direction Ensure high-quality, fast-moving, performance-driven execution across all channels Shape and evolve noon’s creative direction and tone of voice Drive execution across ATL, BTL, digital, and on-ground activations Work closely with internal teams, agencies, and production partners to deliver at scale Partner with CEO / GrowCo and business leaders on high-priority growth initiatives Build and lead a culture of creative excellence, ownership, and high standards What We’re Looking For: 10–15+ years of experience in creative strategy, advertising, or campaign leadership roles Proven track record of delivering high-impact, full-funnel campaigns Strong creative instinct and independent ideation ability Deep understanding of campaign development across digital, performance, and brand marketing Strong production and filmmaking sensibility Comfortable operating both strategically and hands-on Experience working in fast-paced, high-growth environments Strong interpersonal skills — able to work across creative, marketing, product, and leadership teams High ownership — able to drive ideas from concept to execution without drop-off Comfortable working under pressure with tight timelines and high expectations Detail-oriented, with a strong focus on quality and output Commercially aware — understands how creativity drives performance and growth Bonus Points If You Have: Experience in e-commerce, tech, or digital-first businesses Strong portfolio of ad films, campaigns, or large-scale creative work Experience working across multiple markets or culturally diverse audiences Prior experience working closely with founders or CEO offices Who will excel? We’re looking for people with high creative standards who understand that great work requires both ideas and execution.
