data.title: Finance Director
data.jd_text:
About Us:Maritime Mercantile International (MMI) is a leading distributor and retailer of premium wines, spirits, champagne, beer, and non‑alcoholic beverages across three continents. Emirates Leisure Retail (ELR) operates more than 300 restaurants, cafés, and bars across six countries, representing global brands such as Costa Coffee and Pret A Manger, alongside distinctive homegrown concepts including Mr. Toad’s, Saddle, and the award‑winning ZOMA in Zanzibar.As proud subsidiaries of the Emirates Group, MMI & ELR share one mission: to bring more to life. Guided by our HERO values - Honesty, Excellence, Respect, and Opportunity - we create memorable experiences for our customers, our people, and the communities we serve.
About the roleWe are seeking an executive‑level Finance Director to lead the global financial, procurement, and governance agenda across the MMI & ELR Group. Reporting directly to the CEO, this role is pivotal in shaping long‑term value creation, driving financial transformation, and enabling sustainable growth across multi‑market, multi‑currency, and omnichannel operations.You will bring strategic vision, commercial depth, and operational excellence to a business with ambitious global growth plans.
Responsibilites:Set and lead the global finance and procurement strategy, partnering with the CEO and Executive team to drive growth, profitability, and long‑term organisational and shareholder value.Act as the Finance lead for strategic initiatives, including M&A, market entry, and capital restructuring.Oversee financial operations across all markets, including reporting, budgeting, forecasting, pricing, and margin optimisation.Strengthen procurement excellence through strategic sourcing, vendor management, and contract governance.Drive digital transformation, embedding AI, automation, and advanced analytics to enhance decision‑making and operational efficiency.Identify, assess, and mitigate financial, operational, and strategic risks to protect assets and ensure long‑term sustainability.Guide Group treasury and cash flow planning, ensuring financial stability, optimised capital structure, and effective FX risk management.Lead governance, risk, and compliance, ensuring adherence to international accounting standards, tax regulations, and ESG reporting requirements.Manage capital structure and cash flow, including treasury, liquidity, and FX risk.Build and lead high‑performing global teams, fostering a culture of accountability, agility, and continuous improvement.Act as a trusted advisor to the CEO, Executive team, and Boards on financial, commercial, and strategic matters.
What You Bring15+ years of senior finance leadership in global, multi‑channel organisations.Experience in distribution, retail, e‑commerce, hospitality, and/or F&B is highly valued.Deep expertise in international finance, procurement, cross‑border operations, and digital transformation.Strong understanding of compliance, risk frameworks, and ESG reporting.Professional qualifications such as CPA, CA, CFA, or MBA preferred.A visionary mindset with the ability to balance strategic thinking and operational execution.
What we offer:Join a team of experienced professionals who are experts in the industry. We want to create memories and provide an exceptional experience to every customer on every visit. That's why we're passionate about offering a wide variety of delicious and high-quality products. We’re committed to sustainable practices, reducing our environmental impact, and giving back to the communities that we operate in. We're committed to putting our people first by creating a positive and supportive work environment where everyone feels valued and respected.
How to applyClick "Apply' to join our team.

data.title: Application Director, AMET. Taste Texture & Health
data.jd_text:
Dubai, UAE

We are dsm-firmenich – innovators in nutrition, health, and beauty. We bring progress to life by combining the essential, the desirable, and the sustainable.

From our master perfumers and flavorists to our expert nutritionists and scientists, our trailblazing teams work closely with customers, supporting their ambitions with 150+ years of R&D expertise.

With a passionate and talented team of close to 30,000 employees, we are determined to be a force for good. And to work together to positively impact people and planet.

At dsm-firmenich people are at the heart of the company. We are committed to equal employment opportunities and value diversity in the workplace.

As our successful Application Director, you will lead and manage a team of Application Managers, Flavour & Food Technologists, Technicians and Assistants to develop and nurture a best-in-class application team delivering outstanding solutions for clients.

In this role you will be part of the AMET (Africa, Middle East & Turkiye) region and report to VP Creation & Application, Principal Flavorist.

Your Key Responsibilities

Develop, nurture, and maintain a working environment that allows the application team to optimal answer to client briefs.Lead the application team of Middle East & North Africa and Sub Sahara African. Ensuring individual coaching and support to the team. Establishing training & development programs.Setting objectives, conducting feedback and progress reviews; ensuring proper skill set and training with the use of equipment and develop industry conform top quality applications.Facilitate team approach and secure implementation of tools (systems, equipment’s…)Build strategic partnership with Creation, Regional Segment team, Marketing, Collection Management, Sales, and other dsm-firmenich groups, promote collaborative team work to meet needs and deliver on the strategy and client requirements.Manage application teamwork load, project follow-up, challenge submissions and quality.Drive collection usage in briefs and foster team collection knowledge via regular tastings and gap feedback.Lead key strategic and technical projects like Tenders, rationalizations…Provide technical support through markets and category teams, ensures right value proposition is put behind defined opportunities through effective project management & vitality, in line with regional Strategy, Operational Rules, Client Segmentation and Category Focus.Actively participate in Customer Reviews and Account plans for key clients from the technical angle. Regular Internal cluster visit (SSA) and client visits on need basis.Assures laboratory and its equipment’s are regulatory serviced and maintained according to local regulations and dsm-firmenich SHE policies; Support audits and SHE initiatives.Provides technical support /enquiries to internal and external customers.

We Offer

A rich history and a promising future of bold scientific innovation and passionate creation with our customers.A space to grow by encouraging and supporting curiosity and an open mindset.A culture that prioritizes safety and well-being, both physically and mentally.The opportunity to work for a company where sustainability is much more than a claim and is core to our strategy and purpose.An eagerness to be one team and learn from each other to bring progress to life and create a better future.We offer competitive pay, career growth opportunities, and outstanding benefit programs.

You Bring

MSc. in Food Science or equivalent + 10 years of experience in the Food/Flavour industry (preferably in technical field) with strong technical skillset.Experience in Beverage, Sweet Goods and Savory segments.Experience and knowledge of food ingredients (Hydrocolloids, Enzymes, Culture, Functional System)Proven Leadership abilities and demonstrated experience in managing a team.Strong project management skillsExcellent interpersonal and communication skills, including sound influencing skills, and at ease working cross-functionally.Customer Service mindset; Senior level with technical credibility and gravitas facing customers.Open-minded; flexible and organized.English language is mandatory.

About Dsm-firmenich

At dsm-firmenich, we don’t just meet expectations – we go beyond them.

Join our global team powered by science, creativity, and a shared purpose: to bring progress to life.

From elevating health to making fortified food and sustainable skincare, the impact of your work here will be felt by millions – every single day. Whether it’s fragrance that helps you focus, alternative meat that’s better for the planet, or reducing sugar without losing flavor, this is where you help shape the future of nutrition, health, and beauty for everyone, everywhere.

And while you’re making a difference, we’ll make sure you’re growing too. With learning that never stops, a culture that lifts you up and the freedom to move across businesses, teams, and borders. Your voice matters here. And your ideas? Th

The Business Development Director will play a critical role in shaping strategies and portfolio growth of the company by leading the analysis, feasibility and market analysis to drive informed, data-backed decision-making in line with shareholder expectations and corporate goals. The successful candidate will possess a robust background in real estate investment and sales, coupled with the ability to translate market insights into actionable strategies for built to sell business. Key Responsibilities: Built-to-Sell Strategy: Strategy Development: Develop and implement comprehensive real estate strategies for both new and existing built-to-sell projects, ensuring alignment with company goals and market opportunities. Market Insight: Identify emerging market trends, growth opportunities, and innovative strategies to enhance project viability and maximize shareholder returns. Data-Driven Recommendations: Provide actionable recommendations based on data-driven insights, ensuring projects meet both financial and operational benchmarks. Business Plans: Define, prepare, and oversee detailed business plans for real estate projects, including financial projections, funding strategies, timelines, and resource allocation. Portfolio & Value Creation: Lead discussions on portfolio performance, explore new opportunities, and recommend strategic shifts to drive value creation for the company. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate with Development, Sales, Finance, and Marketing teams to ensure that business plans are aligned with both internal and external stakeholder expectations. Pricing Strategy & Analysis: Pricing Strategy Development: Lead the development and implementation of pricing strategies that align with company objectives and market conditions. Market & Competitor Analysis: Continuously monitor and analyze competitors’ pricing and market dynamics, adjusting strategies accordingly to maintain competitiveness. Data Analytics: Utilize data analytics and market research to support pricing decisions and forecast future pricing trends. Cross-Department Collaboration: Work closely with Sales, Marketing, and Finance teams to establish effective pricing models that support product positioning and profitability. Feasibility Studies: Conduct detailed feasibility studies, including market research, financial modeling, and risk assessments, to evaluate new development opportunities. Partnership Development: Strategic Partnerships: Identify and establish strategic partnerships with landlords, co-investors, and other potential partners to maximize project returns. Negotiation & Integration: Lead negotiation

data.title: Head of Business Development
data.jd_text:
About Us:KPay Group (“KPay”) is a leading fintech company committed to empowering businesses of all sizes through simple, smart, seamless, and secure technology solutions. Serving over 72,000 merchants across Australia, Hong Kong, Japan, and Singapore, KPay helps unlock growth potential by providing a one-stop platform for financial management, business operations, and digital transformation. In 2024, KPay raised a record-breaking USD 55 million in its Series A funding round—the largest globally in the payments sector that year. The company has since been recognised as PayTech of the Year at the Asia FinTech Awards and named to Forbes Asia’s 100 to Watch list in 2025.
About the Role:As the first business leader on the ground in the UAE, you will architect and execute our go-to-market strategy, build and lead the early BD/sales team, and establish KPay as a trusted partner for UAE merchants. This is a true zero-to-one role: you will shape our market entry, define our sales playbook, cultivate key partnerships, and directly drive early revenue—laying the foundation for scalable growth. If you thrive in building from scratch, excel in high-ambiguous environments, and are motivated by creating something impactful, this is your opportunity.
Job Description:Build & own the UAE GTM strategy – develop and execute the market entry plan, including target segments, pricing approach, partnership roadmap, and sales model tailored to the UAE ecosystem.Lead from the front – personally drive early enterprise and key merchant deals while building, mentoring, and scaling a high-performing BD team.Design & implement sales operations – establish CRM processes, pipeline governance, performance metrics, and incentive frameworks to ensure transparency, velocity, and predictability.Forge strategic partnerships – identify, negotiate, and manage alliances with banks, fintechs, ISVs, and channel partners to accelerate market penetration.Own the revenue number – achieve and exceed quarterly targets for merchant acquisition, transaction volume, and revenue growth.
Job Requirements:12+ years in business development, sales, or GTM roles, with a proven track record of building and scaling revenue in early-stage or high-growth B2B SME environments (fintech, payments, SaaS preferred).Deep understanding of the UAE business landscape – existing network, cultural fluency, and knowledge of local regulations, competitive dynamics, and customer behavior.A builder’s mindset – experience designing sales processes, team structures, and partnership frameworks from scratch, not just managing established pipelines.Strong leadership presence – ability to inspire, coach, and grow a team while remaining hands-on in key deals and strategic conversations.Excellent communication and relationship skills – persuasive in both one-on-one and group settings, able to engage C-level stakeholders and partners with credibility.Resilient and entrepreneurial – thrives in ambiguity, learns quickly, and operates with urgency, integrity, and a merchant-first attitude.

growing our team at Saatchi & Saatchi. We are on the lookout for an Associate Business Director to join the team. The Associate Business Director is a senior leadership role responsible for end to end stewardship of client relationships within the Publicis Groupe ecosystem. The role requires deep understanding of how a modern holding company operates across multiple agencies, verticals, and specialist capabilities while ensuring seamless, integrated delivery to clients. This role acts as a champion of integration, translating the Groupe’s structure, processes, and Power of One approach into practical, day to day ways of working that drive better outcomes for clients, teams, and the business. The role carries responsibility for strategic client partnership, business growth, creative excellence, team leadership, commercial performance, operational governance, and contribution to both business and non business agency objectives. Responsibilities: Client Leadership and Account Ownership Serve as the primary senior client partner, owning relationships at a strategic, operational, and commercial level Co lead annual and quarterly business planning, ensuring client objectives are met through the right mix of Groupe capabilities Maintain strong senior level client trust while navigating complex stakeholder environments on both client and agency sides Proactively identify opportunities for growth, innovation, and integration across the client’s business Groupe Integration and Power of One Leadership Demonstrate a strong understanding of Publicis Groupe verticals and specialist capabilities including creative, media, PR, data, technology, CX, production, and commerce Act as an integrator, connecting the right teams, agencies, and expertise to solve client problems holistically Champion the Power of One model in day to day operations as a working reality Ensure smooth collaboration across agencies by aligning on roles, responsibilities, governance, and decision making Navigate and uphold Groupe processes, systems, and ways of working, ensuring compliance without compromising speed or creativity Business Growth and New Business Co drive organic growth across assigned accounts through strategic thinking, strong delivery, and integrated solutions Co lead and support new business pitches within the Groupe, coordinating multiple agencies and disciplines into a single coherent response Work close

data.title: Senior Director Business Development
data.jd_text:
Role Overview We are working with a prominent and fast semi government group to appoint a Senior Director of Business Development. This is a high-impact leadership role responsible for driving commercial strategy, unlocking new revenue streams, and maximizing the value of a diverse asset portfolio across the UAE.
This position goes beyond traditional business development — it requires a commercially astute leader who can identify market opportunities, structure complex deals, and build strategic partnerships that deliver sustainable, long-term growth.
Key Responsibilities :• Lead the development and execution of a forward-thinking commercial and business development strategy aligned with overall organizational objectives• Identify, evaluate, and execute new business opportunities, including acquisitions, joint ventures, strategic partnerships, and innovative revenue models• Conduct in-depth market research and competitive analysis, translating insights into actionable strategies that enhance asset performance• Develop robust feasibility studies and business cases, ensuring all opportunities meet financial and strategic return thresholds• Drive revenue optimization initiatives, including tenant mix strategy, leasing enhancements, and new monetization channels across assets• Play a key role in investor engagement, delivering clear performance updates and building confidence in the portfolio’s growth trajectory• Collaborate closely with internal leadership teams to ensure alignment across commercial, financial, and operational priorities• Represent the organization at industry events, forums, and key stakeholder meetings, strengthening market presence and partnerships
Skills, Experience & Competencies : • 7–10+ years of experience in business development, investments, or commercial real estate, with a strong track record in deal origination and execution• Demonstrated success in leading complex negotiations and closing high-value transactions• Strong expertise in real estate markets, investment analysis, and financial modelling• Ability to translate market intelligence into strategic growth initiatives• Highly developed stakeholder management skills, with experience engaging investors, developers, and government entities• A strategic mindset combined with a hands-on, execution-focused approach• Excellent communication, presentation, and influencing capabilities
Availability:Preference will be given to candidates available immediately or a maximum 30 days after accepting the offer.
We are looking for a candidate who will be able to work in United Arab Emirates. Please apply with your resume and remember to provide us with your contact details.
We will review your job application within 7 working days. Should your profile fit the requirements of the role a consultant from Edari will be in touch with you to get a deeper understanding of your profile, to discuss the role in more detail and potential next steps.

data.title: Director of Business Solutions
data.jd_text:
At Workplace Options (WPO), we deliver human-centric solutions that empower people to thrive - personally and professionally. With a global team of more than 4,800 professionals, we provide real-time emotional support, therapy, coaching, mindfulness, and family services to millions worldwide. Since 1982, we’ve partnered with employers, health plans, and insurers to design flexible, forward-thinking programs that evolve alongside today’s changing workforce.

Today, we support 160 million people across 150,000 organizations, including over half of Fortune 500 companies. Now part of the TELUS Health family, we are expanding our global impact by combining decades of wellbeing expertise. Together, we’re building healthier, more resilient organizations—one person at a time.

Current Opportunity: Director of Business Solutions, Middle East

Location: Dubai

Required Languages: Fluent English and Arabic

Hybrid role - 3 days from office and 2 days remote

What you will do:

This is a high-impact, growth-focused role for a strategic sales leader who thrives in outbound business development. You will lead efforts to build and scale a strong reseller ecosystem, while also engaging directly in complex enterprise B2B sales cycles with strategic partners and corporate clients.

Your mission: identify high-potential reseller opportunities, open new markets, shape go-to-market strategies, and position WPO’s wellbeing solutions as a critical component of partner and enterprise offerings.

Responsibilities:

Reseller & Market Growth 

Identify, qualify, and acquire new reseller partners across existing and emerging segmentsBuild long-term, mutually beneficial partnerships that drive sustainable growthShape scalable reseller programs in collaboration with cross-functional teams


Strategic B2B Sales

Lead consultative, insight-driven sales conversations with enterprise and institutional buyersNavigate complex, multi-stakeholder decision-making and longer sales cyclesArticulate compelling, ROI-focused value propositions tailored to each partner


Collaboration & Enablement

Partner closely with Account Managers to ensure seamless handoff and long-term successWork with Product, Marketing, and Enablement to refine GTM strategiesBring market intelligence back to the business to influence strategy, programs, and KPIs


What Success Looks Like: 

A strong, qualified pipeline of high-potential reseller and enterprise opportunitiesTrusted advisor relationships with senior decision-makersEffective cross-functional collaboration and smooth post-sale transitionsConsistent contribution to program evolution and market expansion


Required Qualifications/Skills:

To excel in this role, you will need the following qualifications and skills:

5–7 years of sales experience (wholesale, channel, reseller, healthcare, insurance, or tech preferred)Proven success in B2B and enterprise sales with complex deal cyclesStrong prospecting, consultative selling, and executive-level communication skillsExperience in partner-led go-to-market environments (preferred)Fluent in English and ArabicWillingness to travel regionally


Why join us:

Make a direct impact on regional growth and strategic directionBuild and scale reseller programs from the ground upCollaborative, fast-paced environment with room to growCompetitive compensation with a transparent, performance-driven commission structure


What we offer:

At Workplace Options, we don’t just deliver well-being services to our clients, we champion wellbeing for our own employees as well. Examples of our benefits and commitment to employee wellbeing include:

Medical insurance, various leave plans, Training/tuition reimbursement, Gym Reimbursement, Wellness rewards, Access to EAP benefits and all WPO services, Mentorship programme

At Workplace Options, we are committed to and are accountable for building a workplace where individuals feel empowered to bring their whole selves to work, free from judgment or fear of discrimination. We understand that having a diverse organization is only the beginning and it will require nurturing and care to thrive. We will continue to take action to ensure we achieve equitable and measurable outcomes.

We strive to cultivate a space where diverse voices are not only heard but actively sought out and valued for the unique insights they bring. By embracing and promoting authenticity, we aim to build a vibrant and inclusive community that fosters collaboration, innovation, belonging and personal growth.

For further details about WPO please check out our website www.workplaceoptions.com and these short videos give a great overview of what we do Human-Powered Care and The WPO Global Experience

Workplace Options collects and processes personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws. If you are a European job applicant, refer to our Privacy Notice for further details (https://www.workplaceoptions.com/privacy-notice-for-recruitment/).

data.title: Business Development Director
data.jd_text:
Thunes is scaling fast rapidly across the Middle East and we’re looking for elite hunters to open new markets and close transformational deals. If you love building, thrive on delivering big deals, and want to sell a product that actually moves money globally in real-time, this is your opportunity!

What You’ll Do

Own the end-to-end sales cycle - prospect, pitch, negotiate, close, and activateBuild and convert a high-quality pipeline of fintechs, banks, PSPs, and digital platformsOpen new markets, landing strategic logos and unlocking revenue fastDrive senior-level conversations with founders, C-suite, and product teamsCollaborate with internal teams to execute seamless go-lives and expand accounts


What You’ll Bring

8+ years in B2B payment sales, proven hunter DNAStrong network across PSPs, fintechs, banks, wallets, e-commerce playersTrack record of exceeding targets and closing complex commercial dealsExecution-first mindset - proactive, resilient, self-driven, hands-onFluent English; other regional languages welcome


Why join Thunes

Uncapped commission with real earning powerHigh-growth fintech backed by global investorsSell a mission-critical payments network used by world-class brands driving financial inclusionHuge white-space markets - build and own your territoryCulture of ownership, ambition, and entrepreneurial execution


About Thunes

Thunes is the Smart Superhighway to move money around the world. Thunes’ proprietary Direct Global Network allows Members to make payments in real-time in over 130 countries and more than 80 currencies.

Thunes’ network connects directly to over 7 billion mobile wallets and bank accounts worldwide, as well as 15 billion cards via more than 320 different payment methods, such as GCash, M-Pesa, Airtel, MTN, Orange, JazzCash, Easypaisa, AliPay, WeChat Pay and many more.

Thunes’ Direct Global Network differentiates itself through its worldwide reach, in-house SmartX Treasury System and Fortress Compliance Platform, ensuring Members of the network receive unrivaled speed, control, visibility, protection and cost efficiencies when making real-time payments, globally.

Members of Thunes’ Direct Global Network include gig economy giants like Uber and Deliveroo, super-apps like Grab and WeChat, MTOs, fintechs, PSPs and banks.

Headquartered in Singapore, Thunes has offices in 14 locations, including Abidjan, Barcelona, Beijing, Dubai, Hong Kong, Johannesburg, London, Manila, Nairobi, Paris, Riyadh, San Francisco and Shanghai.

For more information, visit: https://www.thunes.com/

data.title: Head of Compensation and Benefits
data.jd_text:
About the RoleWe are seeking a strategic and experienced Head of Compensation & Benefits to lead the design, implementation, and governance of our client's total rewards strategy across the organization. Based in Dubai, this role will play a critical part in attracting, motivating, and retaining top talent within a fast-paced luxury retail environment.
The successful candidate will partner closely with senior leadership and HR business partners to ensure our compensation and benefits programs remain competitive, compliant, and aligned with the company’s business objectives and luxury brand positioning.
Key ResponsibilitiesDevelop and execute a comprehensive total rewards strategy aligned with organizational goals and growth plans.Design and manage compensation structures, salary benchmarking, and incentive frameworks across retail, corporate, and regional teams.Lead the annual salary review, bonus, and long-term incentive planning cycles.Oversee benefits programs including health insurance, wellness initiatives, allowances, and other employee benefits across the UAE and potentially broader regional markets.Conduct market analysis and benchmarking to ensure competitiveness within the luxury retail and premium consumer sectors.Ensure compliance with UAE labor laws, tax regulations, and corporate governance standards.Partner with finance and leadership teams on reward budgeting, forecasting, and cost management.Drive improvements in pay transparency, reward communication, and employee value proposition.Lead and mentor the compensation & benefits team while building scalable processes and systems.Support organizational transformation, job architecture, grading frameworks, and pay equity initiatives.
RequirementsBachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Finance, or a related field (Master’s degree or HR certification preferred).10–15+ years of progressive experience in compensation & benefits or total rewards, with at least 5 years in a leadership role.Strong experience within luxury retail, premium retail, or consumer-driven industries.Deep knowledge of job evaluation methodologies, compensation benchmarking, and incentive design.Experience managing regional or multi-country benefits programs is highly desirable.Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate complex data into strategic insights.Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.Familiarity with HRIS systems and compensation tools.
What our Client Offers:Opportunity to shape the total rewards strategy of a leading luxury retail organization.A dynamic, multicultural working environment in Dubai.Competitive executive compensation package and performance incentives.Comprehensive benefits and career growth opportunities.

data.title: Director of Franchise Development
data.jd_text:
We are seeking Disruptive Entrepreneurs with Great Networking, Strategic, Execution & Business Advisory Skills and NOT Pure Job Seekers.
About the CompanyFranchiseBazar is expanding across the UAE and searching for passionate, high-caliber individuals ready to establish and scale their own franchise consultancy. Become the Co-founder /Co-Owner/CEO/MD of your business, backed by the global expertise of FranchiseBazar, and play a pivotal role in accelerating franchising and new business models in the UAE market.
About the RoleAs a Franchise Development Director reporting to the Principal Chief Executive Officer / Managing Partner, Co-Founder & Country Master Franchisee Owner, you’ll lead a dynamic business in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Sharjah, Al Ain, or Ajman, representing FranchiseBazar’s portfolio of 4000+ global and regional brands.
ResponsibilitiesConnect entrepreneurs, business buyers, investors, and SMEs with innovative franchise business opportunities every day.Advise SMEs, family offices, and local businesses to become franchise-ready and drive scalable growth.Enable UAE-based brands to expand across the Middle East, Indian Subcontinent, and beyond — while bringing international brands to the UAE.Offer A-Z franchise development solutions and be integral to the evolution of business models in one of the world’s hottest commercial hubs.
QualificationsSenior Investors / Management professionals (CXO, Director, Partner, VP, Head of Business, Consultants) looking for high-growth business and franchise opportunities.Minimum investment of 150,000 AED and a 300 sq ft dedicated office in Dubai (or similar commitment in other major UAE cities).If you are already operating a consultancy / advisory / brokerage business - franchise consultancy could be a natural diversification + addition and you could start getting great results and profitability, instantly.Option to start as a unit franchisee in major cities for 100,000 AED in Abu Dhabi, Sharjah, Al Ain, Ajman.
Required SkillsWell-networked individuals in UAE’s business, investment, real estate, government, and corporate sectors.English fluency is a must; knowledge of Arabic or other languages is a strong plus.Ability to recruit, manage, and inspire a branch network, as well as collaborate with other consultants, brokers, real estate professionals, and financial advisors.Proven business development skills and daily access to entrepreneurs, investors, and new clients from your existing network.
Preferred SkillsExperience in business advisory, consulting, investment deals, franchise or business brokerage, or management roles or real estate or franchise sales or franchise consulting.Strong track record in new business development, franchising, sales strategies, retail, construction, banking, financial advisory, or event management.Working successfully with entrepreneurs, start-ups, business buyers, and investors.Experience in corporate leadership, B2B consulting, strategy, or building high-level client relationships.
Pay range and compensation packageOperate as a business owner under an exclusive country license — not as an employee.Potential to earn a 70% share of revenues from your deals closed, with no upper limit.Pure performance-based remuneration: the more you scale your consulting and franchise sales business, the more you earn.Access to world-class training to become a certified franchise consultant in UAE.
Equal Opportunity StatementNo fixed monthly packages – this is an entrepreneurial, commission-only growth model built for leaders ready to make their mark in the booming franchise and consulting sector of the UAE.
Ready to Own Your Success? If you’re hungry to launch a franchise consulting business in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Sharjah or any major UAE city, have the required investment, and possess a network you believe in, this is your platform!
We are also seeking Exclusive Master Franchise Partners for Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, Egypt, Turkey & Jordan. Ask for a Detailed Presentation Should you be interested in any of the above country.
Email your CV with the subject “YES I Am Interested to Explore A Country Master Franchisee” to MENA@FranchiseBazar.com.
Let’s connect and write your next success story together!

data.title: Head of Compliance, EMEA
data.jd_text:
Who We Are:

At OKX, we believe that the future will be reshaped by Crypto, ultimately contributing to every individual's freedom. OKX began as a crypto exchange giving millions of people access to crypto trading and over time becoming among the largest platforms in the world. In recent years, we have developed one of the most connected Web3 wallets used by millions to access decentralized crypto applications (dApps). OKX is a trusted brand by hundreds of large institutions seeking access to crypto markets on a reliable platform that seamlessly connects with global banking and payments.

We are deeply committed to shaping a fairer, more transparent and accessible society through blockchain technology. This is why we publish proof of reserves monthly, and continue to ship new innovative security features.

About The Team:

The Compliance function at OKX is evolving. We are not just a control function; we are a strategic partner to the business. We are a team of risk-minded problem solvers and operational executors who ensure the business runs safely at speed. We advise on regulatory obligations while relentlessly optimizing our processes to allow OKX to scale efficiently.

What You'll Be Doing:

As the Head of Compliance, Europe, you will be the operational engine behind our European compliance strategy.You will move beyond high-level advisory to become a true executor, responsible for bringing the compliance team to its next level of maturity.

Your mandate is clear: make OKX's European compliance run faster and better. You will lead a diverse team of country heads and MLROs, transforming them into a cohesive, high-performance unit. You will drive operational excellence, reduce friction in our controls, and support strong compliance controls without slowing down commercial growth.

What You'll Be Doing:

Drive the strategic direction and the execution of the Compliance program across all OKX entities within Europe. Manage and elevate the performance of Country Heads of Compliance and MLROs, moving the team to a more unified, fast-moving regional force. Audit current workflows and ruthlessly eliminate inefficiencies. Implement robust, scalable frameworks that reduce manual workload and utilize data/tech to speed up decision-making. Liaise with first, second, and third-line teams to build a control environment that exceeds regulatory standards while maintaining commercial agility. Oversee compliance reviews and investigations. Produce data-driven compliance reports for management and the Board. You will focus on metrics, SLAs, and KPIs that demonstrate the speed and effectiveness of the compliance function. Participate in Board committee and sub-committee meetings, whether as a member or on an as-needed basis. Act as the primary escalation point for complex issues, providing decisive, commercially aware guidance to business stakeholders. 


What We Look For In You:

8+ years of experience in compliance within financial services, FinTech or cryptocurrency industries, with a specific track record of operationalizing compliance strategies. You don't just identify problems, you fix them. You have a proven ability to work fast, meet tight deadlines, and drive projects to completion in a high-pressure environment. Experience reporting into or working closely with Operations teams. You understand how to balance regulatory safety with operational speed. Deep understanding of EU regulations (MiCA, GDPR, AMLD5/6) and UK regimes. You know how to navigate the fragmented European regulatory landscape. Strong people management skills with experience leading dispersed teams. You know how to rally a team around a common goal and drive performance. You are comfortable using data to measure team performance, identify bottlenecks, and justify resource allocation. Crisp, high-impact communication skills. You can explain complex regulatory concepts to product teams and operational challenges to regulators. 


Nice To Haves:

Direct experience scaling a crypto or high-growth fintech company in Europe. Prior experience working in the crypto industry. CAMS, ICA, or equivalent certification. 


Perks & Benefits

Competitive total compensation package L&D programs and Education subsidy for employees' growth and development Various team building programs and company events Wellness and meal allowances Comprehensive healthcare schemes for employees and dependants More that we love to tell you along the process! 


Notice:

All official OKX vacancies are published on this website. While roles may appear on selected third-party platforms from time to time, information on other sites may be inaccurate or outdated. If in doubt, please apply directly through our official careers website.

Information collected and processed as part of the recruitment process of any job application you choose to submit is subject to OKX's Candidate Privacy Notice.

Whether you're seeking excitement, relaxation, or cultural enrichment, we offer something for everyone.

Shape the future of digital innovation at Miral Experience by leading as a visionary Senior Software Architect, where your expertise will transform complex business challenges into scalable, high-impact solutions. You’ll design and mentor cutting-edge architectures—spanning cloud-native systems, Martech stacks, and data-driven platforms—while aligning technology with strategic business goals, ensuring performance, security, and seamless digital experiences. With deep hands-on experience in Azure, microservices, and modern frameworks like React and Sitecore, you’ll collaborate across teams to define roadmaps, enforce best practices, and drive adoption of emerging technologies. data.title: Head of Chemistry - Yasmina British Academy - AY2026/27
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Job Description

Aldar Education are currently seeking an outstanding Head of Chemistry for Yasmina British Academy in Abu Dhabi, commencing next academic year 2026-2027.

Yasmina British Academy is an outstanding English Curriculum school in the beautiful city of Abu Dhabi. With our globally recognised and admired curriculum, dedicated and passionate practitioners and outstanding teaching and learning, Yasmina British Academy is an exceptional place to work. It has an exciting, diverse and progressive learning community that annually produces some of the very best I/GCSE and A Level result in the UAE. Our learning culture is under pinned by our core values of excellence, respect, passion, collaboration and creativity allowing all students to thrive. Furthermore, the Academy is extremely proud of its recent ADEK inspection, which rated Yasmina British Academy as 'Outstanding' in all six performance standards, putting it amongst the top schools in the UAE and Middle East.

Responsibilities

 RESPONSIBILITIES 

Development of the department

Develop and implement departmental policies aligned with the school’s vision, high achievement standards, and effective teaching and learning.Establish, monitor, and evaluate short-, medium-, and long-term departmental development plans.Use data to identify underachieving students and implement targeted support strategies.Analyse school data, research, and inspection findings to inform teaching practices, expectations, and improvement targets.Ensure schemes of work are relevant, regularly reviewed, and inclusive of SEN, G&T, and EAL provision across all year groups.


Teaching and learning

Ensure curriculum coverage, continuity, and progression for all students, including high achievers and those with special educational needs.Establish clear policies for assessment, recording, and reporting of student achievement.Set and maintain high expectations for teaching quality, student achievement, and behaviour.Monitor and evaluate teaching and learning to identify strengths and areas for improvement, and implement actions accordingly.Ensure regular departmental contribution to the school’s CAS programme and after-school booster/revision sessions for I/GCSE and IB DP students.


Leading and managing staff

Performance manage team in accordance with school performance management policy;Establish clear expectations and constructive working relationships with team, ensuring collaboration, support, accountability and high professional standards;Hold regular department meetings with minutes and agendas; agenda should include focus on improving standards relating to student achievement and teaching and learning;


Managing resources

Efficient and effective deployment and control of staff, resources and budgets;Effective use accommodation to create an effective and stimulating environment for teaching and learning in accordance with Learning Environment Policy;Ensure a safe working and learning environment in which risks are properly assessed and Health and Safety policy and regulations are observed at all times.


Other Duties And Responsibilities

Contribute to the school's CAS Programme by leading/supporting one after-school activity each term (CAS includes I/GCSE and IB DP Revision/Booster Sessions);To be an Academic Mentor and Extended Essay Supervisor to Year 12/13 students;Cover for absent colleagues and invigilate examinations, as required, including a minimum of one evening and one weekend external examination session, to support the Examinations Officer and Summer Examination Schedule;Any other duties as assigned by the Principal.


Qualifications

 QUALIFICATIONS 

Bachelor's degree in Education or other relevant field with a teaching qualification.Minimum of 6 years' experience in teaching specialist subject up to GCSE and A Level desirable but not essential.Previous experience working with students whom English is not their first language.A high level of professionalism and consideration of the well-being of children.Respect for all members of a school community, irrespective of position, gender, age and ethnic background.A clean enhanced Disclosure and Barring Services check or police check.

data.title: Facilities Asset Management Director (FM Operations)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Join Us At Miral Experiences

Miral Experiences is a region’s leader in the management and operation of world-class, award-winning immersive attractions and experiences across Abu Dhabi. Our diverse portfolio includes some of the most iconic entertainment and leisure destinations, including Ferrari World Yas Island Abu Dhabi, Yas Waterworld Yas Island Abu Dhabi, Warner Bros. World™ Yas Island Abu Dhabi, SeaWorld Yas Island Abu Dhabi, CLYMB™ Yas Island Abu Dhabi, and TeamLab. Phenomena Abu Dhabi, and CLYMB™ Abu Dhabi, as well as cultural treasures like Qasr Al Watan. We specialize in crafting unique experiences that cater to all tastes and ages, delivering a seamless blend of thrills, joy, and discovery. Our attractions are designed to ignite the imagination and create unforgettable memories for visitors from around the world. Whether you're seeking excitement, relaxation, or cultural enrichment, we offer something for everyone.

About The Role

As the Facilities Asset Management Director, you will drive the strategic development and execution of facility management policies to ensure the seamless operation and maintenance of all physical assets. You will oversee comprehensive CAPEX and OPEX budgets, direct major upgrades and modifications, and guarantee compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations. Leading the Hard and Soft Services Divisions—including HVAC, plumbing, irrigation, and civil works—you will analyze equipment failures, optimize cost-effective practices, and manage a high-performing facilities team. Your role demands a blend of technical expertise, leadership, and operational acumen to maintain peak efficiency, minimize downtime, and uphold the highest standards of safety and performance across our parks.

What You'll Do

Facility Operations & Maintenance

Supervise and manage all facilities operations, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and operational excellence.Plan, direct, and control all aspects of leisure facility maintenance to maintain optimal functionality and guest satisfaction.

Financial & Budgetary Oversight

Manage Division Profit & Loss (P&L), collaborating with Finance and Senior Leadership to track expenditures against budgets.

Team Leadership & Development

Conduct performance reviews and disciplinary actions to foster a high-performing and motivated team.Inspire colleagues to achieve success and progress within the organization.Ensure training programs are delivered to meet annual and quarterly training hour requirements.

Contractor & Vendor Management

Supervise contractors and vendors working within the parks to ensure quality and compliance.Approve third-party contractor schedules for monthly manpower allocation.

Project & Asset Management

Lead project management for new construction and facility upgrades aligned with park strategies.Oversee facility and attraction installation processes to meet contractual, technical, and operational requirements.Participate in design reviews to communicate operational improvements to designers.Develop scope, budget, and schedule requirements for refurbishment programs.Ensure third-party inspections are completed and facilities/attractions are accepted per regulatory standards.

Preventive Maintenance & Compliance

Design and implement preventive maintenance (PM) programs and standard operating procedures (SOPs) for integration into the CMMS tool.Monitor PM tasks to optimize equipment efficiency and minimize downtime/breakdowns.Ensure accurate and timely data entry into the CMMS system.Maintain proper inventory levels for spare parts across all facilities equipment.Review Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and contracts in alignment with procurement processes.Ensure all Fire Life Safety Systems (FLS) are fully operational and compliant.Communicate scheduled interruptions to facilities equipment to relevant stakeholders.

Safety & Regulatory Compliance

Prioritize the safety of colleagues and guests by ensuring adherence to government health and environmental regulations (e.g., HACCP, firefighting systems).Address and rectify items listed in the Safety Log from HSE in a timely manner.Ensure all colleagues are properly trained and utilize safety equipment to reduce workplace injuries.

Strategic & Operational Oversight

Provide input for setting facilities budgets and actively seek cost-saving programs.Monitor departmental KPIs and strategic initiatives to drive continuous improvement.Conduct technical reviews of documentation and collaborate with maintenance teams for operational efficiency.Oversee asset management and inventory to maintain optimal performance and compliance.Implement comprehensive preventive maintenance programs and develop departmental policies and procedures.Ensure parks’ assets are maintained to the highest quality and performance standards.Oversee maintenance operations to achieve objectives for costs, guest service satisfaction, cleanliness, and sanitation.

data.title: Project Director (Residential)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Overview

At AtkinsRéalis, we are seeking an experienced Project Director with a strong background in complex building projects. This role requires a forward-thinking leader with over 20 years of experience managing large, multi-disciplinary teams on complex construction projects.

Your role

Lead, plan, and direct multidisciplinary teams in the successful delivery of residential projects.Manage all aspects of project execution, including safety, quality, schedule, and cost control, ensuring alignment with client objectives.Build and maintain long-term relationships with key stakeholders and clients, enhancing AtkinsRéalis' reputation and identifying new business opportunities.Monitor contractor performance to ensure compliance with contractual obligations.Oversee the coordination and management of large-scale building projects, focusing on publicrealm, enabling works, and building assets.Ensure resource allocation meets current and future project demands, maximizing operational efficiency.Ensure the highest standards of Health & Safety on all construction activities.

About You

Degree-qualified in engineering or a related field (BSc, BEng, BA or equivalent); Chartered status preferred.20+ years of experience in construction, with at least 7 years in roles similar to this, focused on large building projects.Expertise in managing large-scale, complex construction operations, particularly within the rail sector.Strong communication and reporting skills, capable of engaging with senior executives and managing client expectations.Demonstrated ability to deliver high-quality projects on time and within budget.Experience in managing multidisciplinary teams and complex stakeholder relationships.A deep understanding of construction techniques, project controls, and safety management.A strategic thinker with the ability to mobilize teams, plan effectively, and manage both technical and commercial aspects of projects.Strong interpersonal skills and ability to build and maintain effective working relationships.A track record of delivering high-profile, large-scale residential projects.

Rewards & benefits

Explore the rewards and benefits that help you thrive – at every stage of your life and your career.

This Includes

Tax-free salary.Comprehensive life insurance coverage.Premium medical insurance for you and your dependents.Generous annual leave balance.Flexible and hybrid work solutions.Remote work opportunities outside of country.Company gratuity scheme.Discretionary bonus program.Annual flight contribution.Relocation assistance.Transportation & housing allowances (available for remote work locations).Employee Wellbeing Program: 24/7 access to specialists in finance, legal matters, family care, personal health, fitness, and nutrition.

Seize every opportunity to sharpen your skills, expand your expertise, and be recognized for the impact you make.

About AtkinsRéalis

We're AtkinsRéalis, a world-class engineering services and nuclear organization. We connect people, data and technology to transform the world's'infrastructure and energy systems. Together, with our industry partners and clients, and our global team of consultants, designers, engineers and project managers, we can change the world. We're committed to leading our clients across our various end markets to engineer a better future for our planet and its people.

Find out more.

Cross-Domain Collaboration: Strong engagement skills across Business, EA, and Transformation teams. This is a chance to lead the architectural evolution of one of the region's most influential financial institutions. data.title: Head of Credit Decisioning
data.location: Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Summary
The Head of Credit Decisioning has end-to-end accountability for the design, governance, and performance of automated credit decision frameworks across consumer and business lending products. The role leads the strategy, development, deployment, and continuous optimization of credit decision engines spanning origination, underwriting, limit management, pricing, and early warning systems, leveraging strong AI driven solutions.
This role is highly strategic and technical, combining advanced analytics, AI and machine learning, decision science, and strong regulatory governance to enable scalable, fast, fair, and profitable credit decisions. The incumbent will ensure that credit decisioning capabilities support business growth, digital transformation, regulatory compliance, and superior customer experience.
Major Responsibilities 
Credit Decisioning Strategy & Leadership
Define and own the enterprise-wide credit decisioning strategy aligned with business growth, digital channel expansion, and risk appetite.Lead the transformation from manual and rules heavy credit assessment to scalable, automated, and AI enabled decision frameworks.Establish a clear vision for real time, straight through, and omnichannel credit decisioning across products and customer segments.Build and lead a high-performing Credit Decisioning function, ensuring strong capability development in analytics, decision science, and risk governance.Act as the central authority on all credit decision logic, policies, and automation across the retail credit lifecycle.
Decision Engine Design & Architecture
Lead the design, development, and deployment of enterprise credit decision engines covering:Origination and underwritingCredit limit assignment and dynamic limit managementRisk-based pricing and offer optimizationEarly warning and pre-delinquency triggersArchitect hybrid decisioning frameworks integrating:Rule based strategiesTraditional scorecardsMachine learning and AI modelsAdaptive and self-learning componentsDrive in house decision engine build initiatives or oversee vendor platforms (e.g., OMDM, TRIAD, SMG, Probe, or equivalent).Ensure decision engines are scalable, resilient, API enabled, and capable of real-time decisioning.
Advanced Analytics, AI & Model Integration
Oversee integration of predictive models into decision workflows, including:Application scorecardsBehavioral and transaction-based modelsFraud and identity risk indicatorsPropensity and affordability modelsEnable the use of alternative and big data sources, including credit bureaus, open banking data, and internal behavioral data.Establish challenger and test and learn frameworks to continuously improve decision outcomes.Ensure explainability (XAI), fairness, and bias mitigation in all AI driven decisions.
Decision Governance & Model Risk Management
Establish robust governance frameworks for credit decisioning, including:Decision logic approval and change managementModel validation, monitoring, and lifecycle managementPolicy interpretation and implementation controlsEnsure compliance with regulatory and internal requirements related to:Model Risk ManagementAuditability and traceability of decisionsFair lending and customer treatment principlesMaintain full documentation of decision strategies, rules, models, overrides, and exceptions.Support internal audit, external audit, and regulatory reviews related to credit decisioning systems.
Business Enablement & Partnership
Partner with Product, Digital, Sales, Marketing, and Operations teams to:Enable fast and accurate credit decisions across branch and digital channelsSupport acquisition, cross-sell, and offer personalization strategiesImprove customer experience through reduced turnaround times and straight-through processingAdvise senior stakeholders on trade-offs between risk, growth, customer experience, and profitability.Support launch of new products and channels through rapid configuration of decision strategies.
Performance Management & Optimization
Define and monitor key decisioning KPIs, including:Approval rates and decline reasonsRisk-adjusted returnsModel performance and stability metricsAutomation and straight-through processing ratesContinuously optimize decision thresholds, cut-offs, and strategies to maximize risk-adjusted value.Drive decisioning improvements across the full credit lifecycle, from acquisition to early delinquency prevention.
Knowledge & Skill Requirements
The ideal candidate will demonstrate deep expertise in:
Credit decision science and automated underwriting.AI and machine learning applications in credit decisioning.Decision engine architecture and strategy orchestration.Rule-based and model-driven decision frameworks.Real-time and API-based decision platforms.Explainable AI (XAI), fairness, and bias management.Challenger frameworks, A/B testing, and test-and-learn methodologies.Integration of fraud, credit, and affordability decision layers.Regulatory expectations related to automated credi

data.title: Design Director
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Mace combines construction expertise with consultancy to unlock potential in every person or project and redefine the boundaries of ambition. Our values shape the way we consult and define the people we want to join us on our journey.

The Project

This portfolio comprises projects either directly managed by the client team or delivered by leading third-party developers under the client’s leadership. It includes a diverse range of projects, spanning new construction, master planning schemes, and executive fit-out works.

The design director will be integrated into the client’s team, providing senior strategic design leadership across the client’s portfolio. The role will act as a trusted advisor to the client, shaping design quality, governance, and delivery outcomes while ensuring alignment with Abu Dhabi’s broader development vision, standards, and long-term objectives.

At the portfolio level, the design director will be responsible for setting the overall design direction, drafting comprehensive design briefs, and critically reviewing submissions from signature architects to ensure alignment with the client’s objectives. The role will regularly present design concepts and progress to senior management, lead multidisciplinary teams and external consultants, and maintain oversight of design performance across multiple projects. Additionally, the design director will ensure that all design solutions are not only of the highest quality and buildable but also sustainable and capable of delivering enduring value.

You'll Be Responsible For

Provide portfolio-wide strategic design leadership, setting clear design vision, principles, and quality benchmarks aligned with the client requirements.Act as the design authority across projects, ensuring consistency, excellence, and alignment with client standards and governance requirements.Influence early-stage decision-making to ensure design outcomes support programme, cost, operational, and long-term asset value objectives.Monitor design progress, performance, and key milestones, escalating challenges and recommending corrective actions where required.Support the client in managing interfaces between projects, programmes, developers, and stakeholders.Review, challenge, and critique design submissions at all stages, providing expert guidance on functionality, aesthetics, compliance, and buildability.Ensure effective integration of design with construction, procurement, and operational requirements.Lead, mentor, and develop high-performing teams, fostering collaboration, professional development, and design excellence.Act as a senior interface with the client’s leadership, government stakeholders, developers, and delivery partners.

You’ll Need To Have

Significant experience operating at a design director or equivalent senior leadership level on complex, high-value portfolios.A degree qualification in architecture with relevant professional accreditation or membership.Possesses an architectural background with experience collaborating alongside renowned architects.Proven track record of providing strategic design leadership within client-side, PMC, or signature architect environments.Deep understanding of design governance, design management systems, statutory approval processes, and multidisciplinary coordination.Strong capability in design review, risk management, and decision-making across all stages of the project lifecycle.Experience working with senior stakeholders, with confidence and credibility to influence outcomes.Demonstrated commitment to quality, safety, well-being, sustainability, and long-term asset value.Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to lead, mentor, and inspire teams within a secondment environment.

Our values

Safety first - Going home safe and well: We champion a safe, diverse and inclusive working environment, understanding the importance of wellbeing in every team.

Client focus - Deliver on our promise: We own the quality of deliverables, strategic outcomes and build long-term relationships with our clients.

Integrity - Always do the right thing: We influence positive outcomes within our industry, while always aligning with our compliance obligations.

Create opportunity - For our people to excel: We champion a continuous improvement culture throughout all activities, inspiring our people and teams to develop.

Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfill all the criteria, please apply, as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation.

We are also open to discussing part-time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable within the role.

data.title: Director - Managed Schools - ADNOC Schools
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Job Description

Aldar Education is currently seeking a Director - Managed Schools (ADNOC) to support our growing family of managed schools in the UAE, commencing immediately.

The Director – Managed Schools (ADNOC) will be fully responsible for effectively leading all school improvement strategies, marketing, and enrolment activities of the managed schools and have commercial and education line management of all managed schools. The incumbent will continuously strive to exceed internal & external customer expectations, capitalizing the economic profit and return on capital investment, while embracing the Aldar Education Core Values.

Aldar Education is the leading education group in Abu Dhabi, we provide inclusive and innovative learning that inspires and empowers communities, Aldar Education aims to be your employer of choice. We educate over 34,000 students across our group of owned and managed schools in Abu Dhabi and Al Ain and as we grow so does our need for highly skilled, inspirational, and dedicated school leaders and employees across the group.

Working collaboratively, our purpose is to empower the next generation to shape our future, directly supporting the educational goals of Abu Dhabi’s government. Today, Aldar Education’s efforts are guided by the Abu Dhabi Economic Vision 2030 plan, which aims to build a sustainable knowledge-based economy through premium education.

We are a “Great Place To Work” and currently employ over 1,400 teachers to deliver the English National Curriculum, the highly regarded American Massachusetts State Curriculum, and the prestigious International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme all with an international dimension.

This is an exciting opportunity to join the highly successful Aldar family of schools and to help shape the future of Abu Dhabi’s leading school group.

Responsibilities

Main Duties 

 Provide robust and intellectual leadership, vision, and strategic direction to achieve the highest levels of performance and ensure a culture of continuous improvement across all key areas.  Lead and support managed schools in order to build capacity and capability towards meeting current and future challenges in line with company priorities.  Foster highly effective relationships with other members of the team responsible for central functions, (such as HR; Finance; Marketing; Enrolments and IT, etc) in meeting identified key objectives.  Work alongside the Director of Education and other members of the Executive Leadership Team in the delivery of the company’s stated outcomes against a clearly defined set of performance indicators and standards.  Work alongside the Director of Education and the Executive Leadership Team to deliver the education strategy, mission and vision for managed schools. Through direct reports, ensure schools are aligned and articulate the identified strategic direction and build a shared sense of vision and purpose for all schools and their communities.  To support and advise HQ departments and School Principals on efficient and effective school operation processes, strategy and development and any other duties as required by the Director of Education 


Educational Leadership

 Inspire and support direct reports in the delivery of high standards of student achievement, increased student progress, school inspection increases using available data to drive improvement.  Support Leadership and other senior staff to shape the strategic direction, guiding the delivery of specified educational goals and directives for managed schools.  Provide quality assurance of all strategic documents such as schools strategic improvement/development plans and self-evaluations, taking appropriate action as required.  Lead in the effective implementation (and ongoing review) of learning and assessment frameworks that use benchmarking and value-add data, to monitor and report student progress and achievement.  Quality assure curriculum offers and champion new educational thinking and educational initiatives in conjunction with the Senior Executive team and schools.  Support Principals in the most appropriate educational strategies ensuring equity of educational outcomes to enrich the school as a learning environment for its students, families and the wider community.  To provide effective, innovative and professional leadership and direction for Principals and other staff coaching, mentoring and talent managment to achieve the best outcomes for children.  Provide support and guidance in relation to inspection planning, preparation and quality assuring the SEF. (School self evaluation form) 


Relationship Management

 Promote and represent the company effectively and positively to all internal and external stakeholders and seek opportunities to expand its role across the UAE and globally.  Represent the company at school functions, interacting with parents in a professional and collaborative manner to build trust in the company and strong community relations.  Devel

data.title: Head of Business Development
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Head of Business Development Climate-Tech & Geospatial SaaS Abu Dhabi (full relocation support)
I am currently partnering with an exceptionally well-funded Climate-Tech startup based in the UAE. They are a "moonshot" venture within a multi-billion dollar R&D ecosystem, focused on using Geospatial AI and Robotics to restore global ecosystems at scale.
After successfully deploying their tech to plant millions of trees regionally, they are now hiring a Head of Business Development to lead their international commercial expansion.
This is a high-autonomy, founding-level commercial leadership role. Reporting to the Head of Strategy, you will build the global sales playbook, hire a commercial team, and secure high-value partnerships with governments and enterprise land managers.
Who we are looking for:10+ years of business development leadership within Geospatial SaaS, Agri-tech, or Climate-tech.You have a proven track record of scaling commercial teams and taking a technical product from an early-stage pilot to a scalable, international commercial operation.You are comfortable managing long, high-stakes sales cycles involving government ministries, sovereign wealth funds, and C-suite stakeholders.You can translate complex data (from satellite imagery to ecological modelling) into a clear commercial value proposition for non-technical buyers.
The OpportunityA rare chance to lead the commercial rollout of technology reversing climate change at a planetary scale.Work within a multi-billion dollar R&D ecosystem alongside world-class scientists.Highly competitive tax-free compensation with a performance-based bonus.Full family relocation support including business class flights, luxury temporary housing, and schooling assistance.
Please note: This role is based on-site in Abu Dhabi (with flexible WFH days) and requires a high-level security clearance as part of the onboarding process.

data.title: Global Head of Talent
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Why This Role Matters

If you’re driven by the challenge of shaping the talent strategy of a truly global organisation—and you thrive at the intersection of business performance, leadership development, and cultural transformation—this is a rare, high‑impact opportunity.

As ERM continues its rapid growth, the Global Head of Talent will be the architect of our future leadership pipeline, ensuring we have the capability, agility, and diversity required to deliver on our long‑term strategy.

This is a role for a visionary senior talent leader who can influence at scale, anticipate what’s coming next, and build a world‑class talent ecosystem across 40+ countries.

What Your Impact Is

In this critical global leadership role, you will:

Define and deliver ERM’s enterprise‑wide talent strategy, directly strengthening business performance and leadership bench strength.Build an integrated, data‑driven talent system that identifies, develops, and accelerates critical talent across every region.Shape a culture of career opportunity, ensuring ERM continues to fulfil its career promise for all ERMers.Partner at the highest levels of the organisation—including the Executive Committee, Global HR Leadership Team, and regional people leaders—to drive succession, capability development, and internal mobility.Lead and grow a high‑performing team of global talent professionals committed to unlocking potential and enabling organisational success.

Required

What You’ll Bring

L4 education or equivalent experience in business, HR, management, or related fields.Proven experience leading global People or Talent functions within complex, multinational organisations (minimum 5 years).Strong commercial acumen and the ability to translate business strategy into actionable talent priorities.Deep understanding of global talent and succession best practices, including leadership assessment, development, and mobility.Demonstrated success influencing senior stakeholders and driving change across geographically dispersed teams.Expertise in talent analytics, succession planning, and enterprise‑wide capability development.Strong commitment to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Belonging.Exceptional communication, negotiation, and stakeholder‑management capabilities.Ability to thrive in a fast‑paced environment and manage competing priorities.

Preferred

Experience shaping global career frameworks or talent operating models.Executive coaching experience.Experience leading talent transformations during periods of growth or organisational change.Prior experience in professional services, consulting, or similarly people‑intensive industries.Demonstrated ability to innovate and introduce leading talent practices at scale.

Key Responsibilities

Translate strategic direction and governance requirements set by the Project Management Manager into executable, domain‑specific project plans and delivery strategies. Ensure portfolio‑level integration between project delivery, long‑term maintenance planning, and asset lifecycle objectives Lead all Project Management activities related to the initiation, planning, execution, and closeout of all HVAC and chiller projects. Ensure project initiation, planning, scheduling, cost control, risk management, and reporting activities are executed in compliance with corporate standards, regulatory requirements, and approved project governance frameworks. Lead key interface with other organizational functions on all related Project Management activities. Lead the management of key project stakeholder expectations and provide periodic communication to all relevant personnel as part of the project portfolio. Lead the development and ensure the accuracy and quality of all required project documentation for submission to Project Review Boards. Lead proactive identification and mitigation of technical, operational, supply chain, and execution risks, particularly for works performed in live plant environments. Support the Project Management Manager in portfolio‑level reporting and decision‑making through accurate domain insights. Provide leadership and coordination for HVAC SME and Chiller SME roles within the organization. Ensure technical standards, design philosophies, equipment selection criteria, and maintainability requirements are consistently applied across all HVAC and chiller projects.
