Job Purpose The Investment Banking & Markets (“IB&M”) Compliance team is responsible of providing compliance advisory services and support to all Investment Banking and Global Markets business areas. The team ensures that that regulatory risks for these business areas are identified, managed, escalated, and reported through FAB’s Compliance Program, encompassing regulatory change and control management, undertaking compliance risk assessments, management and business reporting, assurance activities and training delivered to business staff. The team ensure the business areas receive ongoing oversight for meeting compliance with UAE Federal Laws, SCA and CBUAE Regulations, as well as FAB’s internal Compliance policies and procedures. The role of AVP Compliance Advisory is responsible to support in providing Second Line of Defense compliance advisory support. The primary focus of the role will be to support the Investment Banking Coverage teams and Global Corporate Finance. Key Accountabilities Strategic Contribution Support implementation of the Bank’s Compliance strategy within the IB&M teams. Advisory and Administration Providing Compliance advice and guidance to relevant stakeholders. Support the Head of Compliance – Global Markets, GCF and Head of Surveillance to deliver the wider Compliance strategy, including Annual Compliance Plan, Compliance Risk Assessment, regulatory developments, regulatory engagement, and issue management. Lead Compliance involvement in the product review process, new business initiatives, cross border activities and financial promotions. Timely and diligent manage
data.jd_text: Job Purpose The Investment Banking & Markets (“IB&M”) Compliance team is responsible of providing compliance advisory services and support to all Investment Banking and Global Markets business areas. The team ensures that that regulatory risks for these business areas are identified, managed, escalated, and reported through FAB’s Compliance Program, encompassing regulatory change and control management, undertaking compliance risk assessments, management and business reporting, assurance activities and training delivered to business staff. The team ensure the business areas receive ongoing oversight for meeting compliance with UAE Federal Laws, SCA and CBUAE Regulations, as well as FAB’s internal Compliance policies and procedures. The role of AVP Compliance Advisory is responsible to support in providing Second Line of Defense compliance advisory support. The primary focus of the role will be to support the Investment Banking Coverage teams and Global Corporate Finance. Key Accountabilities Strategic Contribution Support implementation of the Bank’s Compliance strategy within the IB&M teams. Advisory and Administration Providing Compliance advice and guidance to relevant stakeholders. Support the Head of Compliance – Global Markets, GCF and Head of Surveillance to deliver the wider Compliance strategy, including Annual Compliance Plan, Compliance Risk Assessment, regulatory developments, regulatory engagement, and issue management. Lead Compliance involvement in the product review process, new business initiatives, cross border activities and financial promotions. Timely and diligent management of requests and queries received by the business. Coordinate with Regulatory Compliance (RC) and Financial Crime Compliance (FCC) functions to provide recommendations on compliance risks as and when needed. Support in the administration and response of external requests received from clients and counterparties. Developing and strengthening relationships to promote best practice and ensure a good understanding of Compliance requirements across IB&M. Deliver effective training and provide updates to IB&M businesses on compliance requirements, best practices, market news, international regulations, hot topics and regulatory changes. Provide support for Compliance surveillance activities where required. Continuous Improvement Proactively identify opportunities for continuous improvements related to the systems, controls, processes and practices based on risks and control deficiencies identified. Undertake training as directed and required. Reporting Prepare MI related to the business areas for internal and, where required, external reports. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or higher in a field with demonstrable transferability to the role. Certification in Compliance preferred. Minimum Experience Minimum of five years’ experience with a bank or financial institution. Experience in a Compliance Advisory role, preferably in providing

data.title: Head of IT Solutions and Quality - Michael Page
data.jd_text:
Lead the IT Solutions and Quality departmentContribute to the growth of the organisation through Technology


About Our Client

Our client is a well-established Financial Services organisation in Jeddah.

Job Description

The Head of IT Solutions and Quality will be responsible for:

Managing budgets for the IT Solutions and Quality departmentTranslating business needs into system requirements and developing/delivering/implementing solutions successfullyReviewing upgrade plans to ensure alignment with current and future business goalsEnsuring solution delivery is on time and within budgetOverseeing vendor activities during system implementation and deliveryLeading negotiations for software licenses, services and maintenance contractsLeading and developing department staff through training, performance tracking and mentorshipCoordinating with other department heads to keep change initiatives on track and within scopeEnsuring compliance with SDLC best practices across development teamsSystems Quality Assurance and UATMaximise system availability and performance through proactive managementOverseeing end-to-end system upgrades, product setups and production readiness


The Successful Applicant

The Head of IT Solutions and Quality will be/have:

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related fields of study8+ years of experience in solutions development and quality assurance within the Banking/Financial Services sectorExcellent leadership skills; previously led solutions development teamsStrong project management skillsKnowledge of crisis management and disaster recoveryAbility to work cross-functionally, and interact with various technical/non-technical stakeholdersBased in/willing to relocate to JeddahA Saudi national (strongly preferred)


What's On Offer

The salary for the Head of IT Solutions and Quality role will be based on experience.

Contact

Aniket Deo

Quote job ref

JN-072025-6789677

data.title: Cybersecurity BU Head
data.jd_text:
Make an impact with NTT DATA

Join a company that is pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We are renowned for our technical excellence and leading innovations, and for making a difference to our clients and society. Our workplace embraces diversity and inclusion – it’s a place where you can grow, belong and thrive.

Your day at NTT DATA

As a Cybersecurity BU Head at NTT DATA, you will be responsible for driving the growth and strategic direction of NTT DATA’s cybersecurity business across the region. This role combines advanced subject matter expertise with direct sales accountability, overseeing pipeline development, client engagement, and team leadership.

The BU Head works closely with Client Managers, pre-sales architects, and technology partners to pursue qualified leads, develop tailored solutions, and close complex deals. The role also involves expanding market presence, building strategic vendor relationships, and mentoring a team of cybersecurity sales specialists.

You'll play a vital role in developing and maintaining strong relationships with new and existing clients, conducting workshops, and delivering impactful presentations. Your expertise in security technology will enable you to support the closure of sales, address conceptual challenges, and maintain a comprehensive knowledge of our product and service offerings.

You'll be responsible for creating clear account plans, identifying client risks, and using sales methodologies to drive the sales process. Your ability to adapt quickly to new missions and deadlines, along with your client-centric approach to finding the best-fit solutions, will significantly impact achieving our sales goals.

To thrive in this role, you need to have:

An advanced understanding of security principles, concepts, and technologies, including NIST CSF, ISO 27001, and best practices in securing DATA and IT infrastructure.An excellent understanding of the technical concepts of security solutions and the ability to provide technical consultation to customers.Proven success in solution-based selling and achieving/exceeding sales targetsStrong client relationship skills up to the C-level, with the ability to deliver engaging sales presentations.The ability to maintain up-to-date, accurate sales forecasts and close plans.Proficiency in team-selling approaches and collaboration with internal teams.Knowledge of competitors and the ability to apply successful sales strategies.Flexibility to adapt swiftly to new missions, urgent deadlines, and changing client needs.A bachelor's degree or equivalent in a technical or sales field is preferred.Industry/Vendor sales certifications required.

Workplace type: 

Hybrid Working

About NTT DATA

NTT DATA is a $30+ billion trusted global innovator of business and technology services. We serve 75% of the Fortune Global 100 and are committed to helping clients innovate, optimize and transform for long-term success. We invest over $3.6 billion each year in R&D to help organizations and society move confidently and sustainably into the digital future. As a Global Top Employer, we have diverse experts in more than 50 countries and a robust partner ecosystem of established and start-up companies. Our services include business and technology consulting, data and artificial intelligence, industry solutions, as well as the development, implementation and management of applications, infrastructure, and connectivity. We are also one of the leading providers of digital and AI infrastructure in the world. NTT DATA is part of NTT Group and headquartered in Tokyo.

Equal Opportunity Employer

NTT DATA is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer with a global culture that embraces diversity. We are committed to providing an environment free of unfair discrimination and harassment. We do not discriminate based on age, race, colour, gender, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, disability, pregnancy, marital status, veteran status, or any other protected category. Join our growing global team and accelerate your career with us. Apply today.

Third parties fraudulently posing as NTT DATA recruiters

NTT DATA recruiters will never ask job seekers or candidates for payment or banking information during the recruitment process, for any reason. Please remain vigilant of third parties who may attempt to impersonate NTT DATA recruiters—whether in writing or by phone—in order to deceptively obtain personal data or money from you. All email communications from an NTT DATA recruiter will come from an @nttdata.com email address. If you suspect any fraudulent activity, please contact us.

data.title: Head of IP Strategy (Intellectual Property), Technology Transfer & Innovation Office
data.company: KAUST (King Abdullah University of Science and Technology)
data.jd_text:
Head of (Intellectual Property) IP Strategy, Technology Transfer & Innovation Office
Research Translation Hub, Technology Transfer & Innovation Office (TTIO) 
Position SummaryThe Head of IP Strategy provides expert leadership in the management and strategic oversight of KAUST’s IP portfolio and team. As a core member of the TTIO Leadership Team, the role contributes to the development and execution of TTIO strategy, drives team performance and development, and cultivates strong internal and external stakeholder relationships to advance the University’s IPobjectives and reputation.
Major Responsibilities
Expert IP ManagementExecute the role of IP Principal, maintaining focus on both the operational and strategic dimensions of the function, and providing expert technical leadership in the management of KAUST’s intellectual property portfolio, commercialization activities, and highly complex IP matters.TTIO LeadershipServe as a core member of the TTIO Leadership Team, actively contributing to meetings and the development and execution of TTIO strategy as directed by the TTIO Director.Oversee the health and performance of the TTIO pipeline, including prioritization, team workload and capability development, budget oversight, risk management, and process optimization.Provide regular reporting, insights, and analysis to inform strategic decision-making.Monitor and advance IP Strategy Team and TTIO KPIs, maintaining accountability for theeffective management and performance of KAUST’s IP portfolio.Contribute to the continuous improvement of TTIO strategy, operating model, efficiency, and effectiveness, and identify/implement innovative opportunities to enhance TTIO initiatives.Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including senior leadership, partners, service providers, and attorneys.Develop and maintain a strong external profile that enhances the reputation of TTIO and KAUST, including representation at external meetings, workshops, and conferences.Lead the development and periodic review of KAUST’s institutional IP strategy to ensurealignment with research, commercialization, and national innovation priorities.
Team Talent DevelopmentEngage with IP Strategy Team members to assess skills, capabilities, and development needs.Develop individual and team development plans to address capability gaps, cultivate talent, and support succession planning aligned with TTIO and KAUST business priorities.Conduct regular one-on-one meetings with team members to support professional development.
Team Performance ManagementActively manage and maintain accountability for the performance of the IP Strategy Team and the IP sprint process, including workload, timeliness, quality, responsiveness, and follow-through.Address performance issues promptly to maintain high standards.
Being Part of the TTIODemonstrate and model the values and professional standards of TTIO and KAUST.Perform other related duties as directed by the TTIO Director.
Personal Requirements
CompetenciesExpert understanding of IP laws and practice in Saudi Arabia and the United States.Strong delegation skills to manage workload effectively while maintaining leadership focus and supporting team development.Demonstrated initiative, reliability, and credibility as a senior leader.Understanding of IP laws and practice in jurisdictions beyond Saudi Arabia and the US is desirable.Demonstrates an efficient, effective, and transparent approach to workProactive and responsive in engagement with colleagues and stakeholders.Collaborative leader who inspires confidence and trust.Applies sound, commercially focused judgment and supports data-driven decision-making.Committed to continual improvement and professional excellence.Exceptional verbal and written communication skills; communicate clearly, concisely, respectfully, and professionally.
QualificationsBachelor's degree in law, Science, Engineering, or a related field (advanced degree preferred).Professional certification or qualification as an IP Manager, Specialist, or Professional.
ExperienceA minimum of 12 years of relevant post-qualification experience in intellectual property.Extensive experience advising on and managing a broad range of IP issues and disputes, including inventions, trade secrets, copyright, software, and designs.Demonstrated experience in leading an IP portfolio and advising on IP aspects of commercial transactions.Proven experience in leading and developing an IP team, contributing to IP strategy, and developing organizational IP policies.

data.title: Project Director
data.jd_text:
The Project Director is responsible for the overall leadership and delivery of multiple engineering or high-volume projects, ensuring alignment with organizational objectives, technical standards, budget, and timeline. This role provides strategic direction, manages all aspects of planning, scheduling, resource allocation, cost control, and contractor performance, while ensuring technical excellence, compliance with engineering standards, and outstanding client service. The position requires translating strategic goals into actionable plans, overseeing technical execution, and delivering measurable project performance.
Key Responsibilities
Lead the planning, coordination, and control of engineering projects to ensure technical, functional, and financial viability.Develop cost-effective strategies and processes across all project phases, including engineering design, procurement, construction, and commissioning.Oversee quality assurance and quality control procedures to meet engineering standards and client expectations.Manage project schedules using advanced planning tools, tracking variations, disruptions, and associated impacts.Conduct regular coordination meetings and inspections to ensure timely completion of deliverables and approvals.Provide technical guidance and leadership across engineering disciplines, ensuring compliance with drawings, specifications, and industry best practices.Maintain overall cost control by evaluating alternatives and implementing cost-efficient strategies.Establish and monitor cost allocation schedules and provide regular reporting to management and clients.Define procurement targets and manage all expenditures, including subcontractor payments, bonding, and cash flow.Implement strategies to optimize project earnings and cost efficiency.Lead risk identification, assessment, mitigation, and elimination for all project phases.Ensure strict adherence to HSE policies, safety initiatives, and incident resolution in line with company and IMS standards.Oversee all regulatory, contractual, and engineering compliance requirements.Lead, mentor, and manage multidisciplinary engineering teams, including personnel planning, performance evaluations, and dispute management.Drive team development, high performance, and accountability.Promote effective communication, collaboration, and coordination across departments and project stakeholders.Lead commercial planning, value engineering, contract administration, and cost estimations.Manage project-related claims and reconciliations with clients and contractors.Participate in tender processes and strategic decision-making for engineering projects.
Job Knowledge & SkillsStrong knowledge of engineering design, specifications, and construction standards.Expertise in project management, technical planning, and execution of complex engineering projects.Proven leadership and team development capabilities in engineering and construction environments.Excellent stakeholder, client, and supplier relationship management skills.Proficiency in ERP systems, preferably SAP, for project and financial management.In-depth understanding of HSE, QA/QC, and site logistics best practices.ExperienceMinimum 15 years of professional experience in engineering projects, with at least 10 years in senior management or leadership roles.GCC experience is a plus (minimum 5 years preferred).Demonstrated success in managing large-scale engineering projects across multiple disciplines.EducationBachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering Professional qualification in engineering, project management, or construction management is highly preferred.

AEW was established in 2007, as a subsidiary of Al Jomaih Holding Company, a leading Saudi conglomerate with a legacy spanning over 80 years, focused on enabling sustainable, social, and economic progress in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and beyond. At AEW, our mission is to tackle new technologies, solve complex problems, and leverage our expertise to unlock opportunities in the Power, Renewables & Water Sectors, while driving impact for our stakeholders across the value chain. Duration: Approx. 4 years Job Overview To lead and oversee all construction activities for the Water Treatment Plant. Ensuring the timely, on-budget, and quality delivery of the asset, meeting contractual requirements, and design specifications for reliable and sustainable operation over 25 years. Implementing the HSSE program and supervision in the Project Company and Owner's team, fostering a culture of health, safety, security, and environment in alignment with Project Company and Stakeholder targets. Monitoring, coordinating, and reporting on multiple Contractors' activities at the site, challenging and negotiating with the EPC contractor when necessary to protect the Project Company and Aljomaih Energy Water Company's interests. Directing the Owner's Engineer team at the site. Facilitating seamless communication and information exchanges among stakeholders, including project partners, local communities, and external parties. Manage HSSE implementation onsite and offsite, maintaining a strong HSSE culture. Coordinate activities of the Owner's Engineer, local contractors, local authorities, interface partners, and Contractors. Monitor and enforce EPC Contractor's compliance with Saudi Aramco IKTVA and Saudization targets. Act as the primary link between the off-taker, Owner's Engineer, EPC Contractor, third party, the Phase 1 Operator, --etc to align battery limits and tie-ins. Align technical and schedule interfaces between different contractors, ensuring contract conformity and project needs. Monitor Engineering & Procurement, ensure the EPC Contractor issuing the engineering packages and deliver procurement items ahead of construction needs. Organize and lead construction and progress management meetings with all stakeholders. Identify, assess, and escalate technical deviations, construction risks, and non-conformities to the Project Company CEO, proposing effective mitigation and recovery measures. Manage contractual, commercial, and technical matters arising under the EPC Contract and WTA, in coordination with legal and commercial teams. Review, evaluate, and provide recommendations on change orders, variations, and claims submitted by contractors and service providers. Lead resolution meetings for technical and construction issues encountered on-site and off-site during execution. Lead QA/QC oversight by managing the Owner's Engineer site engineering team to witness, verify, and document key inspections and tests in accordance with the approved Inspection and Test Plans (ITPs) and project requirements. Lead risk assessment and risk identification workshops with the EPC Contractor and Owner's Engineer, ensuring all project risks are identified, formally recorded in the risk reg

data.title: Director of Mining – Major Programs
data.jd_text:
Job Description

WSP Middle East is seeking an experienced and dynamic Director of Mining based in Riyadh, KSA, to establish and lead our Major Programs Mining & Minerals business line in the region. This strategic leadership role will combine business development, project delivery oversight, commercial and financial management, and client relationship excellence to drive growth and successful delivery in one of our key sectors. As a leader within the Middle East region, with global connectivity, this role will require a blend of technical expertise, project leadership, business development acumen, and strategic client engagement.

You will be responsible for shaping the direction of the mining business line, building strong teams, and delivering complex, major mining programs that align with WSP’s global standards and regional ambitions

Responsibilities

Business Development & Strategy:

Serve as the founding leader for WSP’s Mining & Minerals major programs business in the Middle East, establishing market presence and credibility.Navigate regional regulatory frameworks, cultural dynamics, and client expectations to build a sustainable business footprint.Define and execute the growth strategy for the Mining & Minerals business line across the Middle East.Identify and pursue new market opportunities, capitalize on your existing relationships with government entities, mining operators, and strategic investors.Lead bid preparation and proposal development, including pricing, risk assessments, and contract terms.Develop and maintain strong relationships with new and existing clients, and effectively represent WSP in meetings, workshops, and site-based engagements.Prepare proposals, technical reports, and client-facing documentation aligned with project and business objectives.Lead the setup and execution of PMC frameworks for large-scale mining programs.Develop delivery models tailored to regional client needs, including owner’s team services and integrated project controls.


Strategic Oversight of Advisory & Design Streams:

Provide strategic leadership for the Mining Advisory and Design service lines.Ensure alignment with global technical teams and leverage international expertise to support and grow regional delivery.Drive integration across service lines to create a unified client offering – from pit to port, from exploration to closure.


Project Delivery & Operations:

Provide leadership and oversight for the successful end-to-end delivery of major mining projects including feasibility studies, mine planning, delivery, and closure phases.Implement and enforce project delivery governance, quality standards, safety protocols, and risk management frameworks.Collaborate closely with multi-disciplinary teams to ensure seamless project execution and client satisfaction.


Client Relationship & Stakeholder Management:

Develop and maintain trusted advisor relationships with key clients, government agencies, regulators, and industry partners.Represent WSP at industry forums, conferences, and stakeholder meetings to enhance brand positioning and business opportunities.Be proactive in identifying opportunities to improve client outcomes and exceed expectations.


Commercial & Financial Leadership:

Take full P&L accountability for the Mining & Minerals major programs business line, including budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting.Manage commercial agreements, contracts, claims, and scope changes to protect company interests and maximize profitability.Drive cost control initiatives and optimize resource allocation.


Leadership & Team Development:

Recruit, mentor, and lead a high-performing team of mining professionals across technical, commercial, and operational disciplines.Foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, safety, and continuous improvement.


Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in mining engineering, Mineral Processing, Geology, or related field; master’s degree or MBA preferred.Minimum 15–20 years of experience in the mining sector, with exposure to consulting and operator-side roles in the Middle East region.Familiarity with regional mining regulations, standards, and market dynamics is highly desirable.Proven track record in business development and establishing new business lines or service offerings.Strong commercial acumen with experience managing P&L, contracts, and financial performance.Demonstrated success in leading large-scale mining projects or programs in the Middle East or similar regions.Excellent leadership, communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment and drive change effectively.


Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all.

Join our close-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the worl

data.title: Associate Director Project Control
data.jd_text:
Mace combines construction expertise with consultancy to unlock potential in every person or project and redefine the boundaries of ambition. Our values shape the way we consult and define the people we want to join us on our journey.

The Project

This is an opportunity to work on one of the largest programmes in Saudi Arabia. This unique project consists of major mixed-use development including sports facilities, entertainment, and arts along with all associated infrastructure.

Mace is one of the delivery partners requiring high-calibre and experienced professionals to lead integrated teams throughout each phase of the project.

You’ll Be Responsible For

Lead PMO and project controls performance, designing frameworks and strategies to deliver excellence.Act as trusted advisor, building strong client relationships and providing subject matter expertise.Direct project teams in cost, schedule, change, risk, and reporting to achieve key objectives.Manage baselines, monitor delivery performance, and oversee quantitative risk analysis for cost and time.Drive structured governance, reporting, and compliance with Mace Control Centre and industry standards.Mentor and develop junior team members, fostering capability while championing sustainability and net zero carbon goals.

You’ll Need To Have

Degree in construction, engineering, or related discipline; chartered membership preferred.Significant PMO and project controls leadership on major programmes.Expert knowledge of tools, techniques, and software including Primavera, Power BI, and risk systems.Proven ability to design frameworks, manage uncertainty, and deliver assurance strategies.

Our values

Safety first - Going home safe and well: We champion a safe, diverse and inclusive working environment, understanding the importance of wellbeing in every team.

Client focus - Deliver on our promise: We own the quality of deliverables, strategic outcomes and build long-term relationships with our clients.

Integrity - Always do the right thing: We influence positive outcomes within our industry, while always aligning with our compliance obligations.

Create opportunity - For our people to excel: We champion a continuous improvement culture throughout all activities, inspiring our people and teams to develop.

Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfil all the criteria, please apply as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation.

We are also open to discussing part-time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable within the role.

data.title: Director of Communication
data.jd_text:
Saudi Arabia

Director of Communication

Organization

UN Development Programme

Posted 18 Mar 2026 Closing date 30 Mar 2026

1 - Position Information

Organization: UNDP Saudi Arabia, through Stars Orbit Consultants and Management Development (SOC).VA No. VA26/40Office/Unit/Project: KSA/Program/ 01004920Position Title: Director of CommunicationDuty Station: Riyadh, Saudi ArabiaOffice-based: On-site (UNDP and PSU)National or International Consultancy: International / NationalDuration: 12 MonthsNumber of Required Positions: 1Vacancy Date of Issue: 17 March 2026Vacancy Closure Date: 30 March 2025Expected Starting Date: As soon as possible

This vacancy is announced by UNDP Saudi Arabia through Stars Orbit Consultants and Management Development (SOC). SOC is a sub-contractor for UNDP Saudi Arabia, assisting the programme in implementing their activities through HR and contract management of consultants working in their support.

To review the full job description, please click on the following link:

https://drive.google.com/file/d/19OsO9J1MkqNQMtfDBPw2cTR4w8IUpJWc/view?usp=sharing

2 - Project Title

Establishment of and Support to Public Diplomacy Think Tank, Prince Sultan University (PSU), (Project Number: 01004920).

3 - Project Description

Through the partnership between the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) and Prince Sultan University (PSU), this project supports the establishment and operationalisation of the Public Diplomacy Think Tank in Saudi Arabia. The initiative represents Phase II of a multi-phase cooperation, building on the successful completion of Phase I, which defined the strategic foundation and institutional design of the Think Tank.

The Think Tank will strengthen Saudi Arabia’s capacity for public diplomacy by advancing research, innovation, and capacity development aligned with Vision 2030. It will serve as a centre of excellence to promote thought leadership, knowledge exchange, and cross-sector partnerships, positioning PSU as a national and regional leader in public diplomacy.

UNDP provides technical and operational support to ensure the Think Tank’s establishment adheres to international standards, integrates sustainable governance systems, and strengthens PSU’s institutional capacity. PSU will lead in developing research, training, and outreach programmes to enhance Saudi Arabia’s influence, promote global dialogue, and develop local expertise in digital diplomacy and soft power strategies.

The project contributes to the UNSDCF Outcome 4: fostering peaceful, just, and inclusive societies and mobilizing means required for the implementation of Agenda 2030. It aligns with UNDP Strategic Plan Output 3.1 (economic diversification through research, innovation, and digital transformation) and CPD Output 4.3 (strengthening institutional and technical capacities of civil society and academic partners).

4- Scope of Work

The Director of Communications’ scope includes, but is not limited to, the following:

 Communication Strategy and MessagingDevelop and maintain the overall communication strategy aligned with public diplomacy objectives.Define messaging priorities, tone, and positioning across audiences.Develop and approve key messages for different target audiences.Ensure consistency of narrative across all outputs and platforms (Arabic and English). Content Oversight and Research CommunicationProvide editorial oversight and final approval for:Research-related communication outputsMedia-oriented contentWebsite and digital contentEnsure clarity, credibility, and policy relevance of published materials.Work with research and programme teams to translate technical outputs into accessible, communication-ready content.Guide the framing of publications for public, policy, and media audiences. Media, Digital Presence, and Public EngagementOversee media engagement and communication materials (press-style summaries, statements, media briefs).Ensure availability of media-ready content aligned with institutional messaging.Guide content direction across digital channels, including the website and other communication platforms.Ensure alignment between digital presence and institutional communication priorities.Oversee communication related to events, dialogues, and public engagements.Support the design and rollout of communication campaigns when required. Planning, Coordination, and GovernancePrepare periodic communication plans (annual / quarterly).Monitor overall effectiveness and coherence of communication efforts (high-level, non-technical).Act as the central coordination point for communication across management, research teams, and relevant partners.Ensure communication outputs adhere to institutional and governance standards.

4 - Performance indicators

Communication Strategy

Communication strategy is clear, coherent, and effectively aligns with public diplomacy objectives.Key messages are consistently reflected across all channels and audiences.
Content Oversight

Research and

Project Director- Highrise WSP in the Middle East الرياض الرياض السعودية قبل١٨ دقيقة أكثر من ٢٠٠ متقدم معرفة من قامت شركة WSP in the Middle East بتعيينه التقدم انضم أو سجل الدخول للعثور على وظيفتك التالية انضم للتقدم إلى وظيفة ⁦ ⁩‏Project Director- Highrise‏⁦ ⁩ في ⁦ ⁩‏WSP in the Middle East‏ البريد الإلكتروني أو رقم الهاتف كلمة المرور عرض هل نسيت كلمة المرور؟ تسجيل الدخول تسجيل الدخول باستخدام البريد الإلكتروني أو عضو جديد على LinkedIn؟ انضم الآن بالنقر على الاستمرار للانضمام أو تسجيل الدخول، فأنت توافق على اتفاقية المستخدم و اتفاقية الخصوصية و سياسة ملفات تعريف الارتباط على LinkedIn. حفظ الإبلاغ عن هذه الوظيفة Job Description WSP Middle East is seeking a high-caliber Senior Project Director to lead the successful delivery of a landmark high-rise tower in Riyadh,KSA. The successful candidate will have an unwavering commitment to quality across every aspect of delivery — from the service we provide and the client relationships we build, to the teams we lead and the construction works we supervise. You will be responsible for overseeing all phases of project delivery, ensuring alignment with client expectations, budget, schedule, and WSP’s commitment to excellence. Responsibilities RESPONSIBILITIES Lead the delivery of a high-rise / supertall tower project, ensuring the highest quality standards are upheld at every stage. Foster a strong client relationship by consistently delivering exceptional service and acting as the primary point of contact. Build and manage high-performing multidisciplinary teams, ensuring collaboration and accountability. Ensure assigned projects are completed within budget and schedule, while meeting client requirements and business objectives. Oversee all aspects of project execution, including design management, construction supervision, cost control, and stakeholder engagement. Provide strategic leadership and guidance throughout the full project lifecycle. Drive innovation, sustainability, and digital best practices in project delivery. Represent WSP at senior-level meetings and within broader industry forums as required. Qualifications/Experience QUALIFICATIONS A degree in a discipline suited to the work to be delivered, with the ability to converse in technical matters across all disciplines associated with our Construction Supervision projects. 20+ years’ experience in leading construction supervision of multi-disciplinary projects. Working for an international consultancy for 20+ years and over 5 years of experience in the Middle East. Exceptional leadership and communication skills with a strong client interface presence. Proven experience in managing large-scale tunnelling and hospitality projects, with a deep understanding of the technical aspects involved. PMP, RICS, or similar project management certification is an advantage. Pre-contract design management experience under the RIBA framework (e.g., coordination during RIBA Stages 2–4, oversight of multidisciplinary design teams, value engineering, and design governance). High-rise project leadership experience, clearly noting the scale (GFA, height, no. of floors) and complexity (mixed-use, hospitality, residential, or commercial) of the developments. Early Contractor Involvement (ECI) delivery strategy exposure, such as constructability reviews, logistics planning, sequencing, and value engineering input at early design stages. Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all. Work on landmark projects around the world and embrace opportunities to make an even bigger impact in the communities you care about. What if you could do the best work of you

data.title: Commercial Director
data.jd_text:
Our client is a premium lifestyle quick‑commerce platform entering the Saudi market. They are seeking a Commercial Director to own the commercial strategy and lead the end‑to‑end commercial performance across all categories in KSA.
This role is responsible for maximizing revenue, margin, supplier value, and category growth while shaping the product assortment and building strong supplier partnerships. It requires a commanding commercial leader with deep supplier negotiation experience and the ability to drive high‑impact commercial outcomes.
Key Responsibilities
Own and drive commercial performance, P&L, revenue, and margin KPIsBuild category strategies across grocery, beauty, home, gifting, and curated premium linesLead supplier acquisition, negotiations, and trading terms to secure top‑tier discounts, promos, and rebatesDevelop pricing architecture, promo calendars, and margin‑protection strategiesOversee forecasting, demand planning, and replenishment alignmentPartner with Marketing on 360° brand activation and co‑marketing budgetsLead new category launches, exclusive lines, gifting concepts, and private‑label initiativesManage and mentor the commercial team across category management, buying, planning, and analyticsCollaborate cross‑functionally with Operations, Supply Chain, Finance, Marketing, and TechBuild strong commercial governance, vendor scorecards, and negotiation playbooks
Requirements Experience in FMCG, retail, e‑commerce, or quick‑commerce commercial leadershipProven ability to secure aggressive supplier terms, rebates, exclusives, and promo fundingStrong commercial instincts with exceptional negotiation and influencing capabilityDeep experience in pricing, promotions, category profitability, and inventory implicationsStrong analytical, data‑driven commercial decision‑makingLeadership experience managing cross‑functional commercial teamsBachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Commerce, or related fieldFluent in English; Arabic is an advantage
** Due to the high volume of applications, please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. If you do not hear from us within two weeks, kindly consider your application unsuccessful at this time. Thank you for your understanding. **

data.title: Director, Navy Portfolio (TS/SCI)
data.jd_text:
Position Title:  Director, Navy Portfolio

 Departmental Function(s):  Growth Leader (BD Executive), Senior Program Manager

Location: Remote - US; travel to Pax River, MD / Indian Head, MD and the Pentagon as Required

Clearance: DoD/DoW TS/SCI Required; will consider Top Secret on case by case basis

Do you thrive on creating unique and impactful value for Federal clients? Are you passionate about partnering with clients and collaborating to create solutions? Do you enjoy the opportunity to drive change that impacts our nation? We are looking for smart, curious, driven individuals to join us in partnering with our client to help them solve some of the nation’s most challenging problems. ASI Government serves as a trusted adviser to government leaders seeking improved performance, enhanced organization effectiveness, and strategic change.

Company Overview

ASI Government provides government leaders with the expertise they need to navigate acquisition and organizational challenges so they can make an impact and achieve their goals. For over 30 years, ASI has transformed the way government does business, impacting over 70 government agencies across the civilian, defense, and intelligence communities.

Job Description

We are seeking a battle‑tested Navy executive leader to own, grow, and scale our Navy portfolio. This role is designed for a senior professional with deep Navy experience, trusted relationships across the Navy ecosystem, and a proven ability to turn access into growth.

This leader will sit at the intersection of strategy, mission delivery, and business expansion / development serving as:

Director of Navy portfolio growth Business Development / Growth Leadership Program Manager for a flagship Navy Portfolio Acquisition Executives (PAE) initiative

This is a high‑impact, high‑visibility leadership role with direct influence on company growth, client trust, and mission outcomes. This is not a caretaker position. This is a build-and-lead role for someone who wants to shape strategy, drive growth, and leave a lasting impact on Navy mission success. You will have executive visibility, real authority, and the opportunity to scale a portfolio with momentum.

Key Responsibilities

Navy Portfolio Leadership & Growth

Own the overall Navy portfolio strategy, growth plan, and execution.  Ability to be the BD / Account Executive for the Navy Portfolio with your vast Navy Network; leaning on your previous connections and leadership experience from past engagements. Leverage established Navy relationships (uniformed, civilian, and industry) to expand footprint, influence, and revenue. Serve as a trusted executive partner to Navy clients, anticipating needs and positioning the company ahead of demand. Identify, shape, and support new business opportunities, including capture strategy, client engagement, and solution framing. Represent the company with credibility and authority across Navy commands and partner organizations. 

Program Management & Delivery

 Serve as Program Manager for a high-visibility Navy PAE initiative  Ensure excellence across performance, schedule, budget, and delivery.  Lead senior program teams and ensure outcomes align with Navy mission priorities.  Mentor, guide and advise Engagement Leads, Project Managers and supporting staff to delivery world class delivery with the support of ASI’s Executive Team. 

Minimum Requirements

 Current and Active DOD/DOW TS/SCI, willing to consider Top Secret clearances on case by case basis. “SCI eligibility” required. Prior Navy experience (uniformed or civilian) with deep understanding of Navy culture, decision-making, and mission execution. Strong, current Navy relationships with the credibility to operate at senior officers and executives. Ability to gain trust immediately with your knowledge and reputation gained throughout your civilian and/or military career as trusted leader. Prior experience as a Business Development or Account Executive within the Navy, NAVIR community. Foundational – Expert knowledge of the Navy Acquisition and Procurement process both traditional (FAR based) and non-traditional (OT / OTA’s) mechanisms. High business acumen and understanding of the complexities the comes with the Warfighter (i.e. Navy Munitions or related) procurement and R&D process. Natural passion and drive to deliver real value to the Navy Warfighter programs and brings a new, inspiring energy to help facilitate and modernize the federal acquisition process via the PAE. Proven experience leading large, complex Navy programs as a Program Manager or senior executive,Interest in close engagement and collaboration with our AI and Data Practice teams.  Track record of driving growth within Navy or DoD environments; High Win-RateActive Secret clearanceWillingness to travel to Pax River, MD

Preferred Qualifications

 Former senior Navy officer (O-5/6 levels) or Senior Navy civilian (Growth Focused).  Located in or around Pentagon, Indian Head, MD, or Pax R

data.title: Innovation Director
data.jd_text:
The Innovation Director leads the company’s innovation and transformation agenda, driving the development and execution of high-impact initiatives aligned with strategic objectives. This role focuses on fostering a culture of innovation, adopting emerging technologies, and enabling sustainable transformation, in line with Beyond Company’s mission to enhance institutional performance across public and private sectors.
Key Responsibilities:
Develop and lead the organization’s innovation strategy aligned with business goals.Design and implement innovation and transformation initiatives, leveraging modern technologies.Oversee the full lifecycle of innovation initiatives from ideation to execution and evaluation.Establish innovation frameworks, methodologies, and performance measurement tools to ensure effectiveness and scalability.Foster a culture of creativity, experimentation, and continuous improvement across the organization.Collaborate with government entities and strategic partners to develop innovative solutions aligned with Saudi Vision 2030.Monitor innovation performance using KPIs and provide regular insights and reports to executive leadership.Identify emerging trends, technologies, and opportunities that can drive business growth and transformation.Represent the company in innovation-related events, forums, and industry engagements.
Qualifications and Experience:
Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field; Master’s degree preferred.10–12 years of experience in innovation, transformation, or strategic program leadership.Experience working with government entities or leading consulting firms is highly preferred.Strong knowledge of innovation management, digital transformation, and emerging technologies.Professional certifications such as Design Thinking or Innovation Management are preferred.Exceptional leadership, analytical, and communication skills with the ability to influence and engage stakeholders at all levels.

data.title: Director Transaction Advisory
data.jd_text:
Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional

From the projects you will work on to the clients we partner with. From career‑defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where everyone contributes to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it will be anything but ordinary.

Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional. 

From the projects you will work on to the clients we work with. From career-defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where we all contribute to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it’ll be anything but ordinary.

Your next role

Buro Happold is supporting the transformation of cities and public services across the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the wider GCC, aligned with Vision 2030, national transformation programmes, and public‑sector reform agendas. As Director – Transaction Advisory within our Municipal Services team, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping, structuring, and delivering complex public‑sector transactions across the region.

Based in KSA, you will lead Transaction Advisory services across priority municipal sectors including urban services, mobility, utilities, public realm, mixed-use development, sport and social infrastructure. You will work closely with senior public‑sector stakeholders and delivery entities.

This role offers a rare opportunity to combine deep KSA market understanding, strong knowledge of the local NCP PPP lifecycle framework, and global best practice to help public authorities structure bankable, resilient, and outcome‑driven transactions that deliver long‑term value for citizens.

Your key duties

As a Director, you will provide senior leadership to our Transaction Advisory and PPP offering within Municipal Services, driving growth, credibility, and excellence in delivery across KSA and the GCC.

You will:

Lead the Transaction Advisory strategy in KSA and the GCC for public‑sector projects, aligned with Buro Happold’s regional growth.Originate and lead PPP, privatization, and transaction advisory engagements, including project screening, feasibility studies, business cases, value‑for‑money assessments, and procurement strategies.Advise public‑sector clients across the full PPP lifecycle, including project identification, structuring, market sounding, procurement, bid evaluation, and support through financial close.Act as a trusted advisor to senior government, municipal, and delivery authority leadership, supporting decisions on service delivery models, private‑sector participation, and long‑term operating arrangements.Provide expert advice on commercial, contractual, and risk‑allocation structures, ensuring transactions are bankable, compliant with KSA frameworks, and aligned with policy and affordability objectives.Work closely with multidisciplinary teams (engineering, planning, operations, digital, economics) to deliver integrated, technically robust, and commercially sound transaction solutions.Lead high‑level client engagements, workshops, negotiations, and market‑facing interactions with government entities, investors, developers, operators, and lenders.Drive business development across KSA and the GCC, including strategic client relationships, opportunity origination, bid leadership, and framework participation.Represent Buro Happold with NCP, municipalities, ministries, and key industry stakeholders, strengthening our position as a trusted advisor in PPP and public‑sector transactions.Mentor and develop regional advisory talent, building a strong, locally grounded Transaction Advisory capability within the Municipal Services team.Oversee portfolio performance, ensuring quality assurance, commercial success, and effective risk management across advisory commissions.


Your Skills And Experience

You will bring a combination of regional credibility, transaction expertise, and senior‑level leadership experience.

You will have:

A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Engineering, or a related discipline; a postgraduate qualification is highly desirable.Significant experience in Transaction Advisory, PPP, or Commercial Advisory, gained within a leading consultancy, advisory firm, or financial institution.Strong, demonstrable experience in the KSA market, including a clear understanding of government structures, procurement regulations, and the NCP PPP process/Framework.A proven track record delivering PPP, privatization, or investment transactions across municipal, infrastructure, or public‑service sectors in KSA and/or the GCC.Deep understanding of project delivery models, operating models, funding and financing structures, and risk allocation in public‑sector transactions.Established relationships with government entities, NCP, municipalities, investors, developers, and operators in the region.Strong leadership and people‑development skills,

data.title: Principal (Director) Data Science Consultant Practice Lead
data.jd_text:
About usEPAM Systems is a leading global provider of digital platform engineering and software development services. Founded in 1993 and headquartered in Newtown, Pennsylvania, EPAM has been helping clients navigate digital transformation for over 30 years, delivering innovative solutions to stay competitive in constantly evolving markets.As consultants, designers, architects, engineers and trainers, at EPAM, we specialize in building long-term partnerships, enabling businesses to reimagine themselves through a digital lens. In Saudi Arabia, EPAM operates in Riyadh and Al Khobar, offering cutting-edge engineering, strategy, design, consulting, and innovation services across industries.  EPAM is driving digital transformation across the Middle East and we’re expanding our Data Practice in Saudi Arabia to meet the rising demand for cutting-edge Generative AI (Gen AI) and advanced data solutions.
We are looking for an experienced Principal (Director) Data Science Consultant Practice Lead with a strong focus on Gen AI applications across industries. In this role, you will design, deploy and optimize AI-driven solutions, helping organizations unlock new opportunities and navigate the evolving AI landscape.
If you are passionate about leveraging AI to solve complex business challenges and create real impact, we invite you to join us in shaping the future of AI-driven innovation
The role offers exciting opportunities to play a highly visible role in partnership with the wider European data leadership teams to drive growth in the region.
The deadline for submission is 31/03/2026. ResponsibilitiesProvide technical leadership and strategic direction expertise with clients across industries to define and execute Gen AI strategies that align with their business goals Design and implement innovative AI-driven products, including customer personalization tools, demand forecasting systems, dynamic pricing solutions and operational automation Lead the development, deployment and monitoring of Gen AI/ML models with a strong focus on MLOps, operational scalability and model lifecycle management Leverage Gen AI technologies, including Large Language Models (LLMs) and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), to solve industry-specific challenges such as supply chain optimization, risk assessment, predictive maintenance and enhanced customer engagement Communicate AI strategies, opportunities and risks effectively with senior executives and cross-functional teams Ensure AI solutions are fair, explainable and aligned with responsible AI practices Work closely with legal and compliance teams to navigate industry regulations Stay ahead of industry trends, emerging Gen AI technologies and evolving regulatory landscapes Contribute to pre-sales activities, including client presentations, product demos and responses to RFPs/RFIs  RequirementsBachelor's or Master's or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics or a related field or extensive relevant work experience Extensive experience in data science leadership roles with demonstrable impact across various industries. Experience in IT consulting is highly desirable Able to act as a diving force in developing and expanding the Middle East Practice footprint in partnership with the wider Data leadership teams Expertise in Gen AI, including Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and advanced AI applications Strong understanding of AI/ML tools and platforms such as Azure, AWS, GCP, Databricks, MLFlow and Streamlit Proficiency in Python and experience with frameworks like Airflow, Plotly Dash or similar tools Proven track record of implementing responsible AI practices, addressing bias and ensuring model explainability Strong communication skills with the ability to translate complex AI concepts into actionable insights for senior business leaders Familiarity with AI regulatory frameworks Experience in pre-sales or supporting sales teams We offerGeneral Organization for Social Insurance (GOSI)Private healthcare and life insuranceEnd of service gratuityAnnual air travel allowance for expatriatesCorporate Programs including Employee Referral Program with rewardsLearning and development opportunities including in-house training and coaching, professional certifications, over 22,000 courses on LinkedIn Learning Solutions and much more Nature of Work and Working Hours The usual working hours for full-time roles at EPAM in Saudi Arabia are from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, including a one-hour unpaid lunch break. Working hours may vary based on client requirements and flexibility is accommodated whenever possible.Our Locations in Saudi ArabiaRiyadh - Moon Tower, Mezzanine Floor, King Fahed Branch Road, AR Rahmaniyah, Riyadh, Saudi ArabiaAl Khobar - 21st Floor, Khobar Gate Tower, Al Bandariyah District, King Fahd Road, Al Khobar, Saudi ArabiaHow to Apply, Recruitment Process and Deadline If you meet the requirements and are eager to join EPAM, simply submit your applicatio

data.title: Senior Manager/Director - Data Lead
data.jd_text:
Please note we are looking to speak to individuals already based in the Riyadh/Dubai as a first priority.

Capco, a Wipro company, is a global technology and management consultancy specializing in driving digital transformation in the financial services industry. With a growing client portfolio comprising of over 100 global organizations, Capco operates at the intersection of business and technology by combining innovative thinking with unrivalled industry knowledge to deliver end-to-end data-driven solutions and fast-track digital initiatives for banking and payments, capital markets, wealth and asset management, insurance, and the energy sector. Capco’s cutting-edge ingenuity is brought to life through its Innovation Labs and award-winning Be Yourself At Work culture and diverse talent.

Location: Riyadh/Dubai (On-site) | Practice Area: Data & AI | Type: Permanent

The Role

As a Managing Principal / Director – Data Lead, you’ll play a pivotal role in growing Capco’s Data & Analytics capability in Riyadh. This is a senior, hands-on leadership position that blends delivery ownership with market development. You’ll lead high-impact data programmes for major clients while actively contributing to sales activity, account growth, and the expansion of Capco’s footprint in the region.

What You’ll Do

Lead end-to-end delivery of complex data and analytics engagements, remaining hands-on enough to guide technical direction and outcomesAct as a trusted senior advisor to client stakeholders, translating business objectives into scalable data solutionsDrive business development activity, supporting proposals, shaping client opportunities, and building long-term client relationshipsBuild and grow Capco’s Data & Analytics presence in Riyadh, contributing to capability development and local market strategyMentor and develop teams, fostering an inclusive, high-performing environment aligned to Capco values

What We’re Looking For

Experience in leading Consulting firms10+ years’ practical experience in data, analytics or AI roles with significant, demonstrable exposure to the financial services industrySenior leadership experience delivering large-scale data and analytics programmes within consulting or complex client environmentsStrong technical grounding across data platforms, architecture, and analytics, with the ability to engage credibly in delivery discussionsExcellent stakeholder management skills, with confidence working at executive levelDemonstrable consulting background and experience in delivery or sales, account growth and client development.

Bonus Points For

Experience working in or across Middle Eastern marketsBackground in financial services or adjacent regulated industriesExperience shaping new market offerings or growing regional practicesNative Arabic speaker

Why Join Capco

Deliver high-impact data and analytics solutions for leading organisationsPlay a key role in growing Capco’s Data & AI in the Middle EastWork in a collaborative, flat, and entrepreneurial consulting culture

Inclusion at Capco

We’re committed to making our recruitment process accessible and straightforward for everyone. If you need any adjustments at any stage, just let us know – we’ll be happy to help. We value each person’s unique perspective and contribution. At Capco, we believe that being yourself is your greatest strength. Our #BeYourselfAtWork culture encourages individuality and collaboration – a mindset that shapes how we work with clients and each other every day.

data.title: Director of Partnerships

Partnerships Director / ManagerLocation: Riyadh, Saudi Arabia (On-site) Employment Type: Full-Time 
We are seeking a high-caliber Partnerships Manager/Director to join our client in Riyadh. This is a pivotal role within an organization renowned for delivering high-impact global summits and strategic initiatives.
As a key driver of growth, you will be responsible for identifying, securing, and managing strategic partnerships that elevate large-scale events and provide unparalleled value to global stakeholders. This role requires a sophisticated communicator who understands the nuances of the Middle Eastern corporate landscape and possesses a proven track record in high-level sponsorship and commercial negotiations.

data.title: Subsurface Director
data.jd_text:
Job PurposeThe Subsurface Director leads all subsurface activities of the company. This includes geology, geophysics, reservoir engineering, petrophysics, geomechanics, and subsurface data. The role covers the full lifecycle—from exploration to field development and production—and ensures safe, efficient, and maximum recovery of resources while supporting the company’s long-term energy strategy.
Experience & Qualifications10+ years of subsurface experience in the energy sector (oil & gas, geothermal, CCS, hydrogen storage, or integrated energy).Strong experience in exploration, appraisal, field development, and reservoir management.Proven track record in reserves growth and production optimisation.Experience leading large technical teams.Proficient in industry software (Petrel, Eclipse/CMG, DecisionSpace, Geolog, OFM, etc.).Master’s degree in a relevant technical field (or equivalent professional certifications).
Key Responsibilities1. Strategy & Technical LeadershipDevelop and implement the company’s subsurface strategy.Ensure high-quality technical evaluations of prospects, reservoirs, and field development plans.Set and control subsurface technical standards and workflows.2. Resource & Reserves ManagementLead resources from early concepts through to reserves.Oversee reserves estimation and classification (PRMS or equivalent).Approve reserves booking, production forecasts, and depletion plans.3. Geology & GeophysicsOversee seismic acquisition, processing, and interpretation.Ensure accurate geological and reservoir models.Support exploration, appraisal, and development drilling decisions.4. Reservoir Engineering & Field DevelopmentLead reservoir studies, simulation, and production forecasting.Approve Field Development Plans (FDPs), well locations, and recovery strategies.Ensure continuous monitoring and optimization of reservoirs.5. Production OptimizationWork closely with Operations and Drilling teams to improve production.Support problem-solving for underperforming wells.Ensure long-term reservoir health and integrity.6. Technology & Data ManagementPromote use of digital tools, automation, and AI/ML technologies.Ensure proper management and quality of subsurface data.Encourage innovation to improve efficiency and accuracy.7. Collaboration & Stakeholder ManagementWork with drilling, operations, HSE, commercial, and project teams.Liaise with joint venture partners, regulators, and consultants.Provide subsurface input for business development, M&A, and due diligence.8. Leadership & Team DevelopmentLead and develop a multidisciplinary subsurface team.Build team capability through coaching and succession planning.Promote a strong culture of safety, collaboration, and technical excellence.Key Skills & CompetenciesStrong strategic and analytical thinking.Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.Ability to make decisions under uncertainty.Proven leadership in multicultural and multidisciplinary environments.High integrity and safety-focused mindset.Strong problem-solving abilities.

data.title: Associate Director, WHOOP Labs Doha
data.jd_text:
At WHOOP, we're on a mission to unlock human performance and healthspan. WHOOP empowers its members to perform at a higher level by providing a deep understanding of the interactions between their unique physiology and chosen behaviors.

We’re looking for a talented leader to help open WHOOP Labs Doha, our first international product research facility. The Doha lab will partner with the WHOOP Labs Boston team to conduct mixed-methods research to support physiology, user experience, and consumer studies. To serve the company’s diverse testing and validation needs, WHOOP Labs completes tens of thousands of data collections a year and creates best in class experience for our participants. WHOOP Labs Doha will set the bar for product innovation research in the GCC, setting strategy for participant experience and contributing to advancing WHOOP technology.

We’re looking for someone with a proven track record collaborating internationally and leading a cross-functional team, who is excited to work with our members and isn’t afraid to roll up their sleeves and get sweaty. This position is in-person in Doha, Qatar with potential travel to Boston, MA 1-2 times per year.

This role is based in the Doha, Qatar. We will only consider candidates who currently live in Doha, Qatar.

Responsibilities

Own the development and execution of the WHOOP Labs Doha opening strategy, including team hiring, participant compensation structure, and setting operating hoursChampion research integrity, including ensuring that data collection tools and team practices align with local regulations for ethical human subjects researchDefine and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure the effectiveness of program initiatives and track progress towards goalsEnsure clear communication between the Boston and Doha labs, developing ways of working that support multi-facility research execution Represent Doha culture and unique participant experience factors in broader strategic conversations with the rest of the WHOOP human subjects research teamBuild the WHOOP Labs Doha brand and represent WHOOP in the GCC region Reports to the Director of Human Subjects Research

Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree in a field related to human subjects research such as psychology, neuroscience, exercise physiology, or consumer science5+ years of full-time professional experience in a related area2+ years of success leading a diversely skilled teamExperience working on a distributed international team preferredDeep knowledge of local culture and ability to thoughtfully communicate localization considerations for research at WHOOP Labs DohaUnderstanding of best practices regarding human subjects research, including how to obtain IRB approval for new research projects; research ethics, data privacy, and consent Track record of successful project management, including resource allocation, timeline management, and risk mitigationExcellent verbal and written communication skills. Strong organizational skills, ability to plan ahead while still being flexible and able to work around challengesStrong networking and collaboration skills, with experience in building partnerships and engaging with external stakeholdersAbility to present findings to stakeholders and effectively communicate with company leadersExcitement to work in a high-energy, fast-paced startup environmentUnderstanding of branding and marketing as it applies to research in industry spacesFluency in verbal and written English, WHOOP’s working language

This role is based in the Doha, Qatar. We will only consider candidates who currently live in Doha, Qatar.

Interested in the role, but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to still apply! At WHOOP, we believe there is much more to a candidate than what is written on paper, and we value character as much as experience. As we continue to build a diverse and inclusive environment, we encourage anyone who is interested in this role to apply.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-verify to determine employment eligibility. It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

data.title: EOI - Project Director (Offshore)
data.jd_text:
Job Description

Wood is accepting expressions of interest for a Project Director (Offshore) to join our team in Qatar. This role create delivery excellence across assigned project/program/portfolio of projects, provide certainty and confidence to the customer/project sponsor, and robust control of project scope, schedule, cost and risk.

Specifies and directs the implementation and completion of one or more major projects or programs, including responsibility for managing safety, scope, cost, schedule and risk, and ensuring appropriate reporting and governance is in place. Responsible for delivery of project(s)/program(s)/portfolio to agreed scope, specifications, schedule and budget, for managing change and customer relationships. Delivers project(s)/program(s)/portfolio which often involves a high level of complexity and risk with multiple workstreams and high financial risk. Oversight of multiple project managers.

Responsibilities

P & L responsibility with associated commercial and financial accountability including revenue delivery, margin and overhead management, budget and forecast determination and attainment, risk and opportunity reviews, cost controls, accounts payable/billing and cash collectionContributes to and reviews the project proposal and plan to establish timeframe, procedures for accomplishing the project, resource requirements, and allotment of available resources to various phases of the project. Ensures adherence to corporate policy and best practice, and sets project evaluation parameters.Negotiates changes to project scope with the customer. Follows Change Management process to identify and approve modifications to scope, cost, schedule and qualityPursues and develop opportunities for innovation, such as digitalisation and technology use, work share and high value engineering centres, to bring value to Wood and customerApproves project procedures and project execution plan including the scope of work, deliverables list, master schedule, budget, org charts, risk register etcEnsures competence of project team members to carry out assigned workOversees proposals and call-offs (may be single projects) within delegation of authority, ensuring compliance with contracting policy and standards of ethical conduct.Monitors adherence to schedule, cost and quality and proactively intervenes to avoid slippageEnsures that the required project management software systems are established for the project and the supporting training is completedEnsure the project assurance protocol and schedule are establishedInvolved in establishing terms and conditions, and wider scope and larger size of projects may require more visibility in the community as well as activity in attracting project staffResponsible for the project HSSE leadership, ensures a program of initiatives and auditing schedule are established, and that HSSE and quality management issues are proactively addressedDrives good practices, including corporate initiatives, across projects and programsResolves complex issues which are escalated by project teamParticipate in assurance activities and peer reviewsThe complexity, potential impact and political sensitivity of projects/programmes of this size (particularly in an international context), frequently require the project director to be involved in closing the deal, attracting specialized staff from outside the organization, and managing relationships with local officials and community groups as well as the customer and contractors, all based on their credibility and proven track record 

Qualifications

University degree (bachelors or masters) in EngineeringProject Management Professional (PMP), Associate in Project Management (APM) or equivalent certification is preferredTypically requires more than 20 years of broad and extensive pertinent experience, including minimum 10 years project management experience, mastering their area and knowledgeable about impacts on other areas; of which substantial experience successfully managing major/complex international or remote location projects.Requires strong technical skill as relevant to the project scope(s)Good level of experience in the related field, industry, and locations of the workHas thorough knowledge of project success and benefits managementExpert in stakeholder management with extensive experience in managing challenging stakeholders and able to coach and advise others in this areaThorough understanding of requirements management from creation to monitoring to verification of complex project requirements and their linkage to business benefits.Knowledge of work flow management, established policies for engineering ownership, globalization of projects and request for tender processExperienced in assembling and managing multi-phase project budgets, including risk contingenciesExpert Practitioner in Risk Management processes, well versed in how risks are transposed into cost, time and contingency estimates and forecastsAdvanced knowledge

We are seeking an experienced media and entertainment professional to lead the identification, sourcing, and development of global brand integration and product placement opportunities across films, OTT content, television, music, and digital content.
