data.title: Associate Principal, Business Development - Middle East
data.jd_text:
Who We Are

Welcome to TELUS Digital - where innovation drives impact at a global scale. As an award-winning digital product consultancy and the digital division of TELUS, one of Canada’s largest telecommunications providers, we design and deliver transformative customer experiences through cutting-edge technology, agile thinking, and a people-first culture.

With a global team across North America, South America, Central America, Europe, and APAC, we offer end-to-end expertise across eight core service areas: Digital Product Consulting, Digital Marketing Services, Data & AI, Strategy Consulting, Business Operations Modernization, Enterprise Applications, Cloud Engineering, and QA & Test Engineering.

From mobile apps and websites to voice UI, chatbots, AI, customer service, and in-store solutions, TELUS Digital enables seamless, trusted, and digitally powered experiences that meet customers wherever they are — all backed by the secure infrastructure and scale of our multi-billion-dollar parent company.

Location and Flexibility

The Associate Principal, Business Development is an integral part of our Business Development team at TELUS Digital. This is a hybrid role, located in Dubai, UAE.

The Opportunity 

The Associate Principal will focus on building partnerships and solutions for prospective clients. You’ll interact with a wide variety of businesses, many partners within those businesses, and ever-changing business and technology challenges. We employ a solutions-led strategy and are seeking consultative business development executives, rather than transactional or sales-led business development professionals.

In partnership with our Head of Middle Eastern Operations, the Associate Principal will champion the expansion of our digital services to new client logos, while building and growing our partnerships within the GCC region.

Responsibilities

Act as a core founding team member of the TELUS Digital operation in the Middle East, working closely with the Managing Director to hunt for new logos and develop business Introduce TELUS Digital’s business, proposition, people, and capabilities to new client prospects as we expand within the GCC regionConsult with potential and existing clients to define digital strategy and digital product experiences and develop creative solutions -- help frame business/technical needs, project scopeSource, analyze, prioritize and respond to RFPs, bringing together the best of TELUS Digital practice areas Collaborate closely with different TELUS Digital practices, Solutions Architects and delivery teams, to propose appropriate team/resourcing plans for client briefs and develop pricing proposalsLeverage the talents of the UX Strategy and Design teams to ideate on behalf of clients, develop concepts to pitch, and develop pitch materialsDrive the overall process to combine technical plans and creative concepts into written proposals (RFP, etc) for submission and presentations for pitches, working hand in hand with design teams Identify key accounts and build and maintain relationships with clients to expand partnerships and extend services on a client-by-client basisAssist with developing strategies and plans for developing and deepening accounts Some travel required for client visits, pitches, and conferences

Competencies 

Minimum of 5 years in a top tier management consulting firm with experience in digital or technology environment, delivering large scale technology transformationExperience working with, and presenting to, C-level professionals of Fortune 500 companiesProven success in project leadership roles with ability to translate technical concepts into business objectivesSkilled problem solver and value driver for clients. Ability to move clients toward valuable recommendations and high-impact solutionsA deep sense of ownership and genuine enthusiasm for coming up with solutions for brands/clientsEngineering or computer science degree and/or background preferredProficiency in both English (required) and Modern Standard Arabic (preferred)Hustle, hunger, and drive to build and grow a business Hands-on and willing to roll up your sleeves and take end to end ownership of activities with minimal supervision Able to see the big picture and get in the weeds as needed Collaborative and able to work across multiple work zones and wear multiple hats

Equal Opportunity Employer

At TELUS Digital, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. All aspects of employment, including the decision to hire and promote, are based on applicants’ qualifications, merits, competence and performance without regard to any characteristic related to diversity.

We will only use the information you provide to process your application and to produce tracking statistics. Since we do not request personal data deemed sensitive, we ask you to abstain from sharing that information with us.

For more information on how we use your

data.title: Steel Structure QC Inspector (UAE National)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Job Description

We are currently seeking a Steel Structure QC Inspector, reporting directly to the Head of the Department to join our team based in UAE, Abu Dhabi.

About us:

Technip Energies is a global technology and engineering powerhouse. With leadership positions in LNG, hydrogen, ethylene, sustainable chemistry, and CO2 management, we are contributing to the development of critical markets such as energy, energy derivatives, decarbonization, and circularity. Our complementary business segments, Technology, Products and Services (TPS) and Project Delivery, turn innovation into scalable and industrial reality.

Through collaboration and excellence in execution, our 17,000+ employees across 34 countries are fully committed to bridging prosperity with sustainability for a world designed to last.

About the mission we offer you:

Location: Ruwais, UAE

Experience: 6 months – 1 year

Nationality: UAE Nationals only

Employment Type: Full-time – Site-based

Key Responsibilities

Assist in conducting quality inspections for steel structure fabrication and installation activities.Verify that steel works comply with approved drawings, project specifications, and international standards.Monitor welding activities, fit-up, alignment, bolting, and material traceability on site.Coordinate with contractors, subcontractors, and QC teams to resolve quality-related issues.Support inspection activities such as dimensional checks, visual welding inspections, and bolt torque verification.Prepare and maintain daily QC reports, inspection records, and non-conformance documentation.Report deviations, defects, or non-compliance to the QC Engineer/Supervisor promptly.Ensure adherence to HSE and quality procedures while promoting safe working practices.


Qualifications & Requirements

UAE National (Emiratisation role).6 months to 1 year of experience in steel structure QC, welding inspection, or related field.Diploma or bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Civil Engineering, or a related discipline (preferred).Basic understanding of steel structure drawings, welding processes, and inspection techniques.Knowledge of QA/QC procedures and documentation practices.Strong communication and teamwork skills.Willingness to work in Ruwais on a site-based schedule.


Skills & Competencies

Basic knowledge of steel structure fabrication and erection.Attention to detail and strong observation skills.Ability to work in a fast-paced, construction-driven environment.Commitment to safety, quality, and compliance standards.


Starting Date: ASAP

Your career with us 

Working at Technip Energies is an inspiring journey, filled with groundbreaking projects and dynamic collaborations. Surrounded by diverse and talented individuals, you will feel welcomed, respected, and engaged. Enjoy a safe, caring environment where you can spark new ideas, reimagine the future, and lead change. As your career grows, you will benefit from learning opportunities at T. EN University, such as The Future Ready Program, Graduate Program, and from the support of your manager through check-in moments like the Mid-Year Development Review, fostering continuous growth and development.

What’s Next?

Once receiving your system application, our recruiting team will screen and match your skills, experience, and potential team fit against the role requirements. We ask for your patience as the team completes the volume of applications with a reasonable timeframe. Check your application progress periodically via personal account from created candidate profile during your application.

We invite you to get to know more about our company by visiting www.ten.com and follow us on LinkedIn , Instagram , Facebook , X and YouTube for company updates.

data.title: Senior Principal Engineer - Satellite Platform
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
FADA is seeking a highly skilled and experienced Satellite Platform Engineer to join our dynamic team. In this role, the candidate will be responsible for leading the design, integration, and validation of satellite bus systems for advanced space missions. He will work closely with cross-functional engineering teams to ensure the successful development of the satellites.

The successful candidate will be responsible for developing high-performance platform accommodating advanced payloads for satellite systems, localising technologies and knowhow, and managing project timelines, budgets, resources, and risk, and will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to ensure successful project delivery.

The Satellite Platform Engineer will report to the VP of Engineering and Capability Development and will work closely with the System Engineer and the Payload Specialists.

Responsibilities:

 Lead end-to-end system engineering activities for satellite platforms, including requirements definition, architecture design, subsystem integration, and performance analysis.  Oversee technical coordination across propulsion, power, thermal, avionics, and structural subsystems to ensure platform-level compatibility and mission success.  Manage interface control documents (ICDs), system budgets (mass, power, data), and verification plans in alignment with mission objectives and customer requirements.  Conduct trade studies, risk assessments, and design reviews (SRR, PDR, CDR) to support decision-making and ensure compliance with space standards.  Collaborate with cross-functional teams including payload engineers, mission planners, and ground segment specialists to ensure seamless system integration.  Work closely with subject matter experts for payloads to ensure platform design supports payload requirements and operational constraints.  Support launch and early orbit operations (LEOP), anomaly resolution, and post-launch performance evaluations.  Mentor junior engineers and contribute to continuous improvement of engineering processes and tools.  Support business development activities by providing technical expertise during customer engagements and strategic planning.  Contribute to technical proposals, including system architecture descriptions, compliance matrices, and cost estimates.  Contribute to and support R&D activities related to satellite platform technologies, including advanced materials, subsystem innovations, and new mission concepts. 

Experience:

 10+ years of experience in satellite system engineering, preferably with flight-proven platforms.  Proven track record of leading successful satellite projects from concept to deployment.  Extensive experience with satellite design, integration, and testing.  Strong background in systems engineering principles and practices.  Familiarity with industry standards and regulatory requirements for satellite systems. 

Qualifications

 Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Aerospace Engineering, Systems Engineering, or related field.  Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.  Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.  Proficiency in relevant software tools and programming languages.  Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.  Strong leadership and project management skills. 

#FADA

data.title: Senior Solution Architect (Databricks & Energy Sector Architecture)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Talan is a global consulting group specialising in innovation and transformation through technology. For over 20 years, we have partnered with companies and public organisations to drive meaningful change.

We are purpose driven, working across diverse sectors to tackle social and environmental challenges, simplify and improve market operations, leverage digital transformation, and ensure that data is managed responsibly and ethically.

Our expertise spans consultancy and outsourcing services, including Technology, Data & AI and Consulting

Senior Solution Architect (Databricks & Energy Sector Architecture)

Job Description

Role Overview

The Senior Solution Architect will lead the design, assessment, and governance of data and analytics platforms — with a strong focus on Databricks — for energy utilities operating within the GCC and UAE. The role requires deep technical and data leadership, domain expertise in electricity and water utilities, and the ability to shape enterprise-scale data architectures aligned with regulatory, cybersecurity, and operational requirements unique to the UAE energy landscape.

This position supports strategy, architecture assessment, creation or improvement of data platforms, vendor engagement, and end‑to‑end solution assurance across large‑scale digital and data transformation programmes.

Key Responsibilities

1. Architecture Leadership & Design


Define and own end‑to‑end data and analytics architecture using Databricks, Delta Lake, ML/AI workloads, and real‑time data processing pipelines. Lead the integration of modern cloud-native platforms with legacy OT/IT systemsDesign architectural blueprints for data use cases such as demand and production forecast, AI based data analysis, grid analytics, power network balance, - experience of similar frameworks applied for energy & utility companies within the GCC for digital transformation programmes would be a significant advantageEnsure cross-platform interoperability across cloud solutions, data platforms, integration middleware, smart metering, net metering or IoT platforms, and cybersecurity baselines (e.g., NESA, TRA, energy regulators etc). 


2. Solution Architecture Assessments


Conduct architecture maturity assessments, platform capability reviews, and gap analysis for data, integration, and operational systems. Evaluate vendor architectures, technical documentation, and compliance with GCC & UAE energy standards. Prepare architecture assurance reports, risks & mitigation strategies, and recommendations for platform consolidation. 


3. Data Platform Engineering & Governance


Oversee design patterns for ingestion, ETL/ELT, orchestration, model training, governance, and data quality frameworks on Databricks. Establish data governance, RACI models, and minimum-security baselines for data and IT/OT systems — consistent with global industry best practices. Ensure compliance with local cybersecurity frameworks, local data protection laws, and critical infrastructure requirements. 


4. Stakeholder, Vendor & Regulatory Engagement


Lead Participate in technical workshops with various stakeholders (internal, vendors, regulators) - echoing responsibilities seen across energy & utilities consultancy roles. Translate business objectives into data-driven architectural strategies for multi‑utility environments (electricity/water/cooling). Support RFP/RFQ evaluations, vendor scorecards, integration assessments, and architecture due diligence. 


5. Delivery & Technical Assurance


Participate in delivery teams, ensuring architectural and data-oriented decisions are implemented consistently. Manage PoCs, pilots, cloud deployments, and scaling of enterprise data platforms. Provide and implement technical advice for migration from legacy environments into unified data analytics ecosystems. 


 





Qualifications

Required Technical Skills & Experience

Databricks & Cloud Technologies


5–10 years experience in designing and implementing data platformsProven experience in data management with Databricks (Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, SQL/ML workloads) and similar platforms. Expert in distributed compute, Spark optimisation, workflow orchestration (Jobs, Delta Live Tables). Strong hands-on experience with Azure (Data Factory, Synapse, ADLS, Key Vault, DevOps, networking) and AWS


Energy Sector Data Architecture


Demonstrated experience designing or assessing data management systems for:Smart metering ecosystems (MDMS, HES, concentrators, IoT)Grid analytics, balance management, forecasting, outage management, customer systems

Understanding of GCC & UAE regulatory frameworks energy-market constraints. 


Integration & Enterprise Architecture


Strong knowledge of system integration patterns, API management, middleware, and enterprise platform interoperability. Experience modernising legacy systems


Cybersecurity & Governance


Familiarity with UAE NESA standards, OT/IT security approaches, risk-based governance, and minimum-security b

data.title: Senior Vice President POM Product Manager Middle East
Co-Founder & Chief Technology Officer
data.jd_text:
We are partnering with a newly established, high-potential marketplace startup in the UAE to build their founding team.
At Uplift, we work closely with our clients not only to fill roles but to shape the core teams that will define their success. These positions are part of the initial leadership layer, offering a rare opportunity to join at an early stage and directly influence how the business, product, and customer experience are built from the ground up.
If you enjoy fast-paced environments, high ownership, and turning ideas into reality, this could be a very exciting opportunity to be part of something early and impactful.
A DIFC-incorporated, UAE-based consumer marketplace is looking for a technologist — someone who thinks like a designer, builds like an engineer, and obsesses over experience and users. You will be the third co-founder, the first engineer, and the person who decides how the platform feels as much as how it works. The platform is prelaunch, well-structured, and targeting a 2026 go-live. 
What Will You Own
1. Platform Architecture & Technical Ownership Own the full technical architecture of the marketplace: mobile apps (iOS/Android), web platform, backend services, and data infrastructure Define and enforce engineering standards, security protocols, and scalability practices from day one Lead all technical decisions: stack selection, third-party integrations (payments, logistics APIs, AI/ML), and infrastructure choices Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines, QA frameworks, and deployment processes 
2. Experience-led Product & Engineering & Delivery Ability to create a technical road map working backwards from the company visionTranslate product requirements into clean, scalable technical solutions - working closely with the founding team on requirements, user stories, and sprint execution Own the MVP build end-to-end: core transaction flows, payments and escrow integration, buyer protection, seller dashboards, and admin tooling Drive engineering velocity and quality: sprint cadence, unblocking decisions, managing delivery against roadmap milestones
3. AI First Product and Experience Strategy  AI is not a feature for our platform — it is the foundation that makes the experience joyful, fast, and cost-effective at scale. We want a CTO who can leverage the full potential of AI to turn what is typically friction-heavy (listing, pricing, discovery, curation) into moments that feel almost effortless. Embed AI-first thinking into the product: smart listing assistance, pricing recommendations, visual search, and personalisation Build the data foundation: event tracking, analytics pipelines, A/B testing infrastructure, and reporting dashboards Define the long-term AI/ML roadmap in collaboration with co-founders 
4. Team Building & Technical Leadership Hire, manage, and grow the engineering team as the business scales — from pre-launch contractors to a full in-house team post-funding Establish engineering culture: accountability, documentation, code review standards, and fast iteration Act as the technical voice in investor conversations, partnerships, and product strategy discussions Ensure the platform is built for the UAE first — local payments infrastructure, Arabic language readiness, regional logistics integration — and can scale across MENA 
What We Are Looking For5-10 years of engineering experience; up to 5 years in a CTO, VP, or Head of Engineering capacityMarketplace experience is essential — direct experience building or scaling a C2C or B2C marketplaceAI/ML integration experience that is practical and shipped — not theoretical; you have improved a product meaningfully through AI Experience with social platform mechanics: feeds, notifications, recommendation systems, and content distribution at scaleStrong full-stack capability with a backend-first mindset; comfortable with mobile (React Native or equivalent), cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP), and API design Proven ability to ship product fast in an early-stage environment — not just manage, but build Comfortable with ambiguity, high ownership, and the pace of a 0-to-1 build Strong strategic thinker with equally strong executional instincts — holds the vision and ships the sprint Understands that the feel of an app is built in engineering decisions: latency, hierarchy, animation, and the moment-to-moment texture of the experience Motivated by building something culturally significant, not just technically functionalComfortable as a co-founder: ownership mindset, high tolerance for ambiguity, genuine excitement about the early stageUAE/MENA market experience preferred; willingness to travel to Dubai regularly is requiredMotivated by equity and long-term upside — this is a co-founder role, not a corporate hire
What We OfferSalary of 20,000 per month.Meaningful co-founder equity stake with standard vesting schedule. Details shared directly with shortlisted candidatesDubai-based or remote with regular travel to Dubai. Flexibility on base locati

data.title: Chief Strategy Officer - Madison Pearl
data.jd_text:
Our client is a leading financial services Group in the GCC seeking to appoint a Chief Strategy Officer. This is a strictly confidential mandate for a senior executive who will shape Group wide strategy, enterprise alignment, and long-term value creation.

This role carries direct exposure to the CEO and Board, with responsibility for defining strategic direction, evaluating growth opportunities, and ensuring disciplined execution across a diversified financial platform.

The successful candidate will combine macro level strategic vision with structured execution capability, ensuring the Group remains competitive, resilient, and growth focused across regional and international markets.

Key Responsibilities: 

Define, refine, and oversee execution of the Group wide corporate strategy aligned with long term growth and shareholder objectivesAdvise the CEO and Board on strategic priorities, market positioning, and enterprise transformation initiativesLead the evaluation, vetting, and development of strategic initiatives including expansion, partnerships, and corporate development opportunitiesEstablish and maintain robust strategy management frameworks, policies, and performance monitoring mechanismsDrive cross functional alignment across business units to ensure cohesive and disciplined execution of strategic objectivesOversee business intelligence activities including market analysis, competitive benchmarking, and identification of emerging opportunities across regulated and non regulated segmentsSupport Board level reporting on strategic progress, risk considerations, and forward-looking initiatives


Key Qualifications: 

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline. MBA or equivalent postgraduate qualification preferred15+ years of progressive experience within financial services, diversified financial groups, sovereign related entities, or leading strategy consulting firmsDemonstrated experience in enterprise strategy formulation and execution within complex financial institutionsStrong exposure to corporate development, transformation initiatives, and performance management frameworksProven ability to influence and operate effectively at CEO and Board levelDeep understanding of GCC financial services landscapes and regulatory environmentsExceptional analytical capability, stakeholder management skills, and executive presence


This appointment is being managed on a confidential basis. Further information will be shared with shortlisted candidates following initial discussion.

If interested, please apply directly or send an email to daniel.brocklebank@madisonpearl.com for a discreet conversation.

 Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted *

data.title: Vice President of Product Engineering, 2 Hour Learning (Remote) - $400,000/year USD
data.jd_text:
Are you a technical product leader with deep engineering expertise who is ready to spearhead the reinvention of education through artificial intelligence? 2 Hour Learning seeks a technical architect to drive the modernization of our AI-powered learning ecosystem. This position requires someone with the technical discipline to function as a CTO, the product vision to lead transformational initiatives, and the commitment to apply these capabilities toward unlocking student potential at scale.

Traditional education companies add AI to existing models as an add-on. 2 Hour Learning has taken a fundamentally different approach: we have reconstructed the school model from first principles, leveraging AI and learning science to eliminate the limitations of the conventional classroom. Our students do not simply improve — they excel, earning multiple 4s and 5s on AP exams, demonstrating multi-year academic gains within a single year, and routinely exceeding their own projections. We have validated that when AI serves as the foundational operating system for learning, student performance surges.

This is not a conventional product management position centered on customer feedback cycles or incremental feature releases. This is an executive-level technical product ownership role, where you will own the architecture, technical strategy, and AI integration across our entire learning platform. You will be accountable for making critical technical tradeoffs that keep our products lean, scalable, and unwaveringly focused on advancing student outcomes. You will think with the rigor of a CTO and execute with the agility of a product entrepreneur, combining engineering excellence with learning science principles to drive continuous innovation.

If you are prepared to lead the technical transformation of education and possess the executional discipline to support that vision, we invite you to apply. This is your chance to shape the future of learning.

What You Will Be Doing

Building and maintaining deep expertise in education products to inform strategic directionCreating technical specifications that establish key technical decisions and emphasize simplicity, efficiency, and sustainabilityDeploying generative AI technologies to automate and optimize engineering workflows, in alignment with the strategic objective of minimizing manual effort

What You Won’t Be Doing

Exclusively managing people; your technical and business judgment will be central to this roleSpending time on meetings, committees, and administrative overhead; we have automated management processes to allow you to concentrate on delivering business value through direct contributionProposing high-level strategies without actionable implementation paths; your guidance must be grounded in product-level understanding

Vice President Of Product Engineering Key Responsibilities

Serve as the CTO for one or more education products, owning core capabilities and generating business value through flawless execution and strategic leadership

Basic Requirements

7+ years of experience making critical architecture and design decisions on software projects, including the application of architecture design patterns or major open source technologies4+ years of experience in roles with complete technical and delivery ownership of a revenue-generating productExperience leveraging AI agents to design, implement, and test software products or features

About 2 Hour Learning

Education is broken, but 2 Hour Learning is proving it doesn’t have to be. They’re tearing down the outdated one-size-fits-all model and replacing it with AI-driven personalized learning that helps kids master academics in just two hours a day.

With students consistently ranking in the top 1-2% nationally and the top 20% achieving an astonishing 6.5x growth, they’re proving that smarter learning is possible. At 2 Hour Learning, it’s talent and performance that matter. 

They offer a dynamic, on-campus and remote-friendly environment where innovators, educators, and AI specialists can be a part of fixing a broken school system.

2 Hour Learning is reprogramming learning for the AI era.

Here’s How They’re Fixing It.

There is so much to cover for this exciting role, and space here is limited. Hit the Apply button if you found this interesting and want to learn more. We look forward to meeting you!

Working with us

This is a full-time (40 hours per week), long-term position. The position is immediately available and requires entering into an independent contractor agreement with Crossover as a Contractor of Record. The compensation level for this role is $200 USD/hour, which equates to $400,000 USD/year assuming 40 hours per week and 50 weeks per year. The payment period is weekly. Consult www.crossover.com/help-and-faqs for more details on this topic.

You are comfortable pivoting from high-level financial strategy to granular discussions on international employment law. You prioritize automated, scalable workflows over manual processes to ensure efficiency as the company grows. Requirements 7+ years of senior leadership experience (COO, VP Ops, or similar) in a high-growth technology environment. Direct experience in the Web3/Crypto industry is highly preferred. Exceptional communication skills with the ability to lead a distributed, global team. data.jd_text: We are a fast-growing decentralized infrastructure provider sitting at the heart of the Web3 evolution. As we transition from a lean engineering-heavy team to a global powerhouse, we need a Chief Operating Officer to build the engine that powers our growth. You won't just be managing—you will be architecting. You will oversee the "Holy Trinity" of back-office excellence: Operations, Finance, and Legal , ensuring our internal systems are as robust and scalable as the decentralized networks we build. Your Core Responsibilities Design and implement the operational frameworks required to transition the company through significant growth phases and support a rapidly expanding global team. Oversee financial planning, budgeting, and Web3-specific treasury management, balancing fiat and digital asset stability. Navigate the complex global regulatory landscape of decentralized infrastructure, managing external counsel to ensure long-term institutional stability. Build a world-class HR function that attracts top-tier Web3 talent while maintaining a high-performance, remote-first culture. Translate the CEO’s vision into actionable operational roadmaps, ensuring key milestones are met across all non-engineering departments. Who You Are You have a proven track record of leading a startup through high-growth periods and maturing the organizational structure. You understand the unique operational and legal hurdles of the decentralized world, from DAO structures to node provider requirements. You are comfortable pivoting from high-level financial strategy to granular discussions on international employment law. You prioritize automated, scalable workflows over manual processes to ensure efficiency as the company grows. Requirements 7+ years of senior leadership experience (COO, VP Ops, or similar) in a high-growth technology environment. Direct experience in the Web3/Crypto industry is highly preferred. Proven success in managing diverse functions across Finance, Legal, and HR. Experience with international entity structuring and global remote hiring. Exceptional communication skills with the ability to lead a distributed, global team.

data.title: Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text:

About the Company:Our client is a high-growth, diversified automotive and mobility group with a strong international footprint across the Middle East, Africa, Asia, Europe, and South America.Having achieved rapid expansion across multiple business lines – including automobile trading, mobility solutions, and adjacent sectors – the Group is now entering a phase of structured, professional scale-up, with a clear ambition to significantly grow and achieve IPO readiness over the medium term.
The Role:We are seeking a dynamic, hands-on CFO to lead the finance function through its next stage of evolution.This is a transformational role, combining operational leadership with strategic influence – focused on building robust financial systems, enhancing governance, and supporting accelerated growth across geographies.
Key Responsibilities:Lead the end-to-end finance function, including FP&A, controllership, treasury, and taxConduct a comprehensive review of existing systems and processes, and implement scalable structures and controlsDrive the selection and implementation of a Group-wide ERP platformEstablish strong financial governance, compliance, and reporting frameworksPartner with senior leadership to support strategic planning, performance management, and expansion initiativesPrepare and present Board-level financial reports and insightsLead and develop the finance team, while overseeing Shared Services operations overseas

data.title: Compliance Senior Manager - Digital Assets
data.jd_text:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

Wise is looking for an experienced and forward-thinking Compliance Senior Manager to join our team in Dubai. This role will be responsible for building, leading, and managing the compliance framework for our new stablecoin and virtual asset services, licensed by Dubai's Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA). This is a unique opportunity to shape our compliance strategy from the ground up under a new regulatory paradigm.

As the Compliance Senior Manager, you will be the primary point of contact with VARA, ensuring that our operations not only meet but exceed regulatory expectations. You will work closely with a global team of specialists across Product, Legal, Operations, and Engineering to launch and scale innovative products safely and compliantly, helping us achieve our mission of making money instant, convenient, and transparent for everyone.

Key Responsibilities

 Regulatory Oversight & LicensingAct as the designated Virtual Asset Compliance Officer (VACO) as required by Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA), holding ultimate responsibility for the integrity and effectiveness of the compliance framework.Own the relationship with VARA, serving as the point of contact for all licensing, reporting, examinations, and strategic engagement.Build and maintain a robust, scalable compliance program tailored to the VARA framework.Oversee the maintenance of our VARA license, monitor, interpret, and advise the business on all regulatory developments from VARA and other relevant UAE authorities. Governance & AdvisoryLocalise and implement Wise’s global compliance policies to ensure they are fit-for-purpose and compliant with VARA rules.Develop and deliver targeted training to the Board and senior management on the VARA framework and the specific risks associated with virtual assets.Report directly to the Board and senior leadership on the health of the compliance program, providing clear insights into emerging risks and the effectiveness of controls.Act as the key strategic advisor to the business, providing clear and practical compliance guidance on new product development and market expansion. Financial Crime ComplianceDesign and manage the financial crime risk management framework, addressing risks specific to virtual assets such as blockchain analytics, the "Travel Rule", and counter-party VASP due diligence.Own the Suspicious Activity Reporting (SAR/STR) process, managing interactions with the UAE’s Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU).Establish risk-based procedures for customer due diligence (CDD) and enhanced due diligence (EDD), and act as the point of escalation for high-risk customer approvals.Regularly assess the money laundering, terrorist financing, and sanctions risks associated with our virtual asset products and services.Implement systems and key risk indicators (KRIs) to track and monitor our regulatory and financial crime risks effectively.

This Role Will Give You The Opportunity To

Build a compliance function from the ground up in the pioneering regulatory landscape of virtual assets in Dubai.Leverage your subject matter expertise to shape and influence the application of the VARA framework, setting a new standard for compliance in the virtual assets sector.Collaborate with brilliant teams across Wise, embedding compliance in our product innovation and growth strategy.

Qualifications & Experience

Minimum of 8 years’ experience in a regulatory or financial crime compliance role, OR 5 years of experience combined with a relevant professional certification (e.g., Certified Cryptoasset Financial Crime Compliance (CCFCC), Chainalysis Reactor Certification (CRC)).Demonstrable, direct, hands-on experience and in-depth knowledge of the virtual assets/cryptocurrency sector, including an understanding of key risks and industry best practices.Profound knowledge of UAE AML/CFT regulations and the FATF Recommendations for Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs). Experience with the VARA framework is a significant advantage.Proven ability to build and manage a compliance program, with experience in policy writing, risk assessment, and regulatory engagement.A "growth mindset" – you are passionate about expanding Wise's mission in a compliant, pragmatic, and solution-oriented manner.Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence and build trust with internal and external stakeholders.Ability to work autonomously in a fast-

data.title: Financial Partnership Development Senior Manager
data.jd_text:
About Thunes

Thunes is the Smart Superhighway for money movement around the world. Thunes’ proprietary Direct Global Network allows Members to make payments in real-time in over 130 countries and more than 80 currencies.

Thunes’ Network connects directly to over 7 billion mobile wallets and bank accounts worldwide, via more than 350 different payment methods, such as GCash, M-Pesa, Airtel, MTN, Orange, JazzCash, Easypaisa, AliPay, WeChat Pay and many more.

Members of Thunes’ Direct Global Network include gig economy giants like Uber and Deliveroo, super-apps like Grab and WeChat, MTOs, fintechs, PSPs and banks. Thunes’ Direct Global Network differentiates itself through its worldwide reach, in-house Smart Treasury Management Platform and Fortress Compliance Infrastructure, ensuring Members of the Network receive unrivalled speed, control, visibility, protection and cost efficiencies when making real-time payments globally.

Headquartered in Singapore, Thunes has offices in 12 locations, including Barcelona, Beijing, Dubai, London, Manila, Nairobi, Paris, Riyadh, San Francisco, Sao Paulo and Shanghai. For more information, visit: https://www.thunes.com/

Context of the role

Thunes is looking for a dynamic, high impact leader to drive the growth, health, and innovation of our Direct Global Network across MENA. The Network Development Manager is technically and financially savvy, creative, with strong personal vision, extremely strong domain expertise, and great people skills.

Key Responsibilities

As a key member of the Network team, you will be asked to wear many hats, including developing country and regional go-to-market strategies, cultivating network partners at all levels of the payments and financial services ecosystem, and developing a well-functioning payments network in MENA that drives volume across our global network, including:

Strategic partnership management & execution: Strategically plan, execute, and manage end-to-end relationships with critical banking and network partners (FIs). This includes strategic road map development in the region and managing key accountsCommercial growth & network competitiveness: Continually drive revenue through cross and up-sell opportunities, in close collaboration with Go-To-Market and Account Management teams. Ensure the Thunes network is the most commercially attractive in the market, from transaction cost to FX, and work with FI partners to drive payment flows through joint go-to-market initiatives.Operational excellence & stakeholder coordination: Act as an owner to ensure all aspects of partnerships are working seamlessly. Work with internal and external stakeholders (including Risk, Compliance, Product, Engineering, and Treasury) to ensure the Thunes network is operationally excellent, coordinate on partner strategy, and advance a holistic network strategy.Market knowledge: Understand and clearly articulate the payment-related nuances among the different markets in-scope (e.g. UAE vs Jordan etc.


Professional Experience/Qualifications

Must have a minimum of 3+ years in a payments scheme, Network Management, or related roleMust be able to ‘talk shop’ with experienced payments professionals and build high-integrity relationships with experts in the fieldMust have a strong network across the payments industry across different markets in MENAMust have proven talent ‘closing’ relationships and growing a businessMust have proven innovation experience including bringing new-to-industry products to marketMust be both a curious student and a patient teacherMust have experience in a fast growing small organizationMust have great communication skillsLanguage fluency in an Arabic is requiredMust be based in Dubai, UAE


Package And Benefits

Great compensation package, including performance‑based commissionMedical insurance for you and your familyLife insuranceMobile allowanceEmployee wellbeing & support programLearning & Development budgetWork‑life balance (flexibility and hybrid working policy)Our team is global: you will work with 65+ different nationalities spread across 5 continents


Sounds like you?

data.title: Chief Information Security Officer - Hedge Fund - $530k (base)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) - Elite Investment Platform - Abu Dhabi - Up to $530k Base (Tax-Free)
Durlston Partners is working with a premier, global investment platform to find a hands-on Chief Information Security Officer. Based in Abu Dhabi, this foundational role requires you to build and scale the firm's core information security capabilities from the ground up as the platform evolves to support institutional clients.
What You’ll DoStrategy & Governance: Define the global cybersecurity and operational resilience strategy, establishing frameworks aligned with NIST, ISO 27001, and DORA across US, EU, Middle East, and APAC operations.Cloud & AI Security: Embed secure-by-design practices into cloud infrastructure, APIs, and AI/ML systems, partnering directly with engineering to integrate security into the SDLC.Risk & Operations: Lead threat modelling, incident response, and BC/DR planning. Build out core capabilities like IAM, vulnerability management, and vendor risk management.Leadership: Act as a trusted advisor to executive leadership on cyber risk, while building and leading a high-impact global security team.
What They’re Looking For10+ years of cybersecurity experience with a proven track record of building and maturing security programs in cloud-native, application-driven environments.Strong leadership background within fintech, investment platforms, or heavily regulated financial environments.Deep technical expertise in cloud security (AWS, Azure, GCP), modern application security, data protection, and AI governance frameworks.Experience navigating multi-jurisdictional compliance. Professional certifications (CISSP, CISM, CISA) are highly preferred.
What They OfferTop-Tier Compensation: Up to $530,000 USD Base Salary + Discretionary Bonus (100% tax-free).Relocation & Visa: Full visa sponsorship with all associated costs covered by the company.Premium Benefits: Comprehensive family medical/life insurance, 30 days holiday (+ UAE public holidays), and an annual education allowance for up to three dependent children.
If you are interested, please apply! Alternatively, feel free to reach out to david@durlstonpartners.com for a confidential discussion.

data.title: Founding CTO (Web2)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text:
We are supporting a venture-backed, early-stage technology company building a white-label consumer engagement and rewards platform for global brands. The platform enables large-scale fan and customer experiences (e.g. predictions, quests, digital rewards, ticketing and commerce) while incorporating tokenised settlement and rewards infrastructure.

This role represents a true greenfield build. The Founding CTO will define the overall system architecture, technical standards, and engineering culture from day one, while delivering a production-grade platform designed to scale to millions of users.

As Founding CTO, you will take end-to-end ownership of the technology organisation, from initial architecture and hands-on development through to team build-out and long-term technical strategy.

You will work closely with founders and commercial stakeholders, translating product vision into scalable systems and ensuring the platform can support multiple partners, brands, and regions.

Responsibilities

Platform & architectureDefine and build the core platform architecture supporting engagement mechanics, rewards, commerce, and analyticsEstablish system design principles covering scalability, security, and performanceMake key build-vs-buy decisions and select vendors, tooling, and infrastructure partnersBackend & infrastructureArchitect and implement backend services supporting real-time event ingestion, points/wallet systems, identity, and compliance controlsDesign robust data pipelines, storage, and API layersOwn production readiness, including monitoring, alerting, SLOs, and incident response processesConsumer productsOversee development of mobile (iOS/Android) and web applications delivered under partner brandingEnsure high-quality user experience across consumer touchpointsToken & rewards infrastructureLead design and integration of tokenised reward systems, including smart contracts and distribution logicManage wallet integrations and fiat on/off-ramps in collaboration with external providersIntegrationsBuild and maintain APIs connecting to external data feeds, ticketing systems, commerce platforms, and sponsor toolsLeadership & teamHire, mentor, and scale a high-performing engineering teamEstablish engineering standards, documentation practices, and development workflowsAct as a hands-on technical leader while progressively transitioning to strategic oversight
Qualifications

8+ years of experience building consumer-facing technology productsProven experience delivering at least one 0-to-1 platformTrack record of shipping and operating products at scale (100K+ users)Strong backend engineering background (distributed systems, real-time data, API design)Experience designing production-grade systems with high reliability requirementsComfortable working across frontend, backend, and infrastructure layersStrong written and verbal communication skills (architecture docs, technical strategy, roadmap planning)

Strongly Preferred

Experience with loyalty, gaming, or marketplace-style mechanics (e.g. rewards, auctions, prediction-style systems)Familiarity with blockchain or tokenised systems (smart contracts, wallets, settlement flows)Background in security-sensitive or compliance-aware systemsExperience building white-label or multi-tenant platforms

Technical environment (current / planned)

Languages: TypeScript/JavaScript, Node.js, Python; Go or Rust exposure is a plusMobile: React Native or Flutter; native iOS (Swift) / Android (Kotlin) experience advantageousInfrastructure: Cloud-native (GCP), Docker/Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelinesData: PostgreSQL, Redis, event streaming (Kafka or equivalent)Web3 exposure: Solidity fundamentals, wallet integrations, token mechanics

data.title: Field CTO
data.jd_text:
Introduction

At IBM Global Sales, we bring together innovation, collaboration, and expertise to help clients solve their most complex business challenges. Working across industries and geographies, you’ll partner with colleagues, clients, and partners to co-create solutions that drive digital transformation and lasting impact.Success in Global Sales is built on curiosity, empathy, and collaboration. You’ll connect technical understanding with strong people skills, building trusted relationships and shaping solutions that improve business and society. With world-class onboarding, continuous learning, and a supportive culture, IBM offers the tools and opportunities to grow your career. Join us and be part of a global team that’s passionate about driving innovation and making a difference.

Your Role And Responsibilities

The Field CTO role is a high-impact position passionate about executive-level engagement, field enablement, and strategic product feedback. As a Field CTO, you act as an extension of the CTO and CEO to strategically enable and support sales teams by building pipelines, closing opportunities, and maintaining long-term relationships with customer leadership teams. You are a trusted advisor to our customers, providing business, technical, and organizational guidance and thought leadership to drive customer outcomes. You work closely with regional sales leadership to focus on top-priority accounts within the theater with significant pipeline impact. As the technical leader and executive sponsor for key accounts, you deeply understand the customer's technical landscape and can articulate how HashiCorp, an IBM company's (HashiCorp) products drive their business success. You also broadly influence the strategic direction for customer messaging through collaboration with the Strategy team, R&D, the broader field, and industry events. You are a trusted advisor to our customers, providing business, technical, and thought leadership to drive adoption and success with our products.

Illustrating the impact of the HashiCorp product suite in delivering customer outcomes is your strength, and you showcase your thought leadership and technical expertise through participation in webinars, speaking events, round tables, white papers, marketing events, advisory boards, and executive briefings. Dynamic communication and presentation skills amongst varied audiences of both business and technology leaders are essential for success in this role, and you should be as adept at engaging with the customer’s C-level executives as you are working closely with internal product and engineering teams.

As a Field CTO, you contribute to public and internal messaging of the HashiCorp principles and tech vision to existing and potential customer executives and decision-makers and enable internal teams and partners to elevate the product narrative. As a field CTO, you are regarded as a business and technical thought leader at HashiCorp and a well-rounded technologist in the IT/application operations landscape.

In This Role You Can Expect To

Work closely with field marketing teams to spread awareness and support pipeline building via customer touch points (round tables, workshops, etc), conferences, strategic events, blogs, webinars, and whitepapers, among other opportunities. Aggregate and synthesize customer feedback on product messaging, pricing, and capabilities and communicate those findings to the relevant internal organizations (sales, marketing, R&D, enablement, services).Coordinate with the regional sales team to establish relevant relationships and act as a technical lead and trusted advisor to accelerate the pipeline for strategic accounts.Understand the customer's technical, people, and process needs, and articulate how HashiCorp's products can meet those needs for the enterprise.Work closely with the field teams to identify opportunities for product adoption, expansion, and extension within their accounts, to communicate a unified vision, and to collaborate through thought leadership.Collaborate with product and marketing teams to provide feedback on customer needs and struggles to ensure our products' proper future direction and success.Drive customer CISO/CxO engagements with the field teams.Validate and contribute to technical materials, standard methodologies, etc., based on field experience to continuously up-level and enable our internal partners.Lead executive briefings and advisory boards with customers, partners, and prospects.Educate, mentor, and promote upskilling within the sales and customer success teams on HashiCorp's products through internal enablement efforts.

Preferred Education

Bachelor's Degree

Required Technical And Professional Expertise

You must have an Engineering/CS/MIS Bachelor’s Degree or strong relevant industry experience;Have a history of executive engagement or served at an executive level;Enjoy speaking in front of crowds, from small to very large, virtually;Are a well-rounded

data.title: Vice President of Global Expansion
data.jd_text:
UnlockLand is an AI-driven PropTech company recognized as one of the top innovators in the “Future of Lifestyle” PropTech Accelerator, led by the National Information Technology Development Program (NITDP) and the Real Estate General Authority (REGA) under Saudi Vision 2030.
 Our mission is to transform how developers, investors, and governments plan, evaluate, and optimize real-estate assets. 
UnlockLand’s AI Operating System integrates geospatial data, financial modelling, and regulatory intelligence to deliver instant feasibility studies and predictive analytics — helping cities and investors build smarter, more sustainable communities.
As we scale across North America, the Middle East, and Asia, UnlockLand is seeking a Vice President of Finance to lead our financial strategy, operational excellence, and investment planning. This executive leader will ensure the company’s financial structure supports rapid global expansion, enterprise partnerships, and large-scale real estate intelligence programs.
As we expand across North America, the Middle East, and Asia, UnlockLand is seeking a Vice President of Operations & Global Expansion to architect scalable systems, optimize cross-functional execution, and drive operational excellence across continents. This leader will ensure UnlockLand’s operational backbone supports rapid adoption, enterprise partnerships, and national-level PropTech initiatives.
This is a high-impact role for an operations executive with deep experience in fast-growth SaaS/AI environments, international expansion, and real estate or smart-city ecosystems.

Vice President MMS META BD Riyadh Region قبل ٢٣ساعة أكثر من ٢٠٠ متقدم معرفة من قامت شركة BD بتعيينه التقدم انضم أو سجل الدخول للعثور على وظيفتك التالية انضم للتقدم إلى وظيفة ⁦ ⁩‏Vice President MMS META‏⁦ ⁩ في ⁦ ⁩‏BD‏ البريد الإلكتروني أو رقم الهاتف كلمة المرور عرض هل نسيت كلمة المرور؟ تسجيل الدخول تسجيل الدخول باستخدام البريد الإلكتروني أو عضو جديد على LinkedIn؟ انضم الآن بالنقر على الاستمرار للانضمام أو تسجيل الدخول، فأنت توافق على اتفاقية المستخدم و اتفاقية الخصوصية و سياسة ملفات تعريف الارتباط على LinkedIn. حفظ الإبلاغ عن هذه الوظيفة Job Description Summary We are the makers of possible BD is one of the largest global medical technology companies in the world. Advancing the world of health™ is our Purpose, and it’s no small feat. It takes the imagination and passion of all of us—from design and engineering to the manufacturing and marketing of our billions of MedTech products per year—to look at the impossible and find transformative solutions that turn dreams into possibilities. Why join us? A career at BD means learning and working alongside inspirational leaders and colleagues who are equally passionate and committed to fostering an inclusive, growth-centered, and rewarding culture. You will have the opportunity to help shape the trajectory of BD while leaving a legacy at the same time. To find purpose in the possibilities, we need people who can see the bigger picture, who understand the human story that underpins everything we do. We welcome people with the imagination and drive to help us reinvent the future of health. At BD, you’ll discover a culture in which you can learn, grow and thrive. And find satisfaction in doing your part to make the world a better place. Become a maker of possible with us! Our vision for Medication Management Solutions at BD A key and growing part of our business is Medication Management Solutions (MMS), which offers integrated solutions that touch all stages of the patient journey, with the goal of reducing errors and inefficiencies and increasing patient safety. Whether you join from a clinical, technical or innovation background, a career with us will help deliver a new healthcare environment and give a better experience for patients and those who provide them care. data.title: Transition Portfolio Manager, Vice President
data.jd_text:
Who We Are Looking For

We are seeking candidates with 10+ years of experience to join our Transition Management (TM) team in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. In this role, you’ll work within State Street Investment Management’s global TM business, delivering transition and interim exposure management solutions to institutional investors worldwide. You’ll support and manage complex transitions across multiple asset classes, including equities, fixed income, FX, and exchange‑traded derivatives, serving clients domiciled in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia while contributing to the firm’s broader global transition management platform, including interim exposure management and reporting and analytics.

This role can be performed in a hybrid model, requiring at least 4 days of work in the office.

Why this role is important to us

State Street Investment Management (SSIM) is a global asset manager partnering with some of the world’s most sophisticated investors. Backed by State Street’s scale and stability, SSIM offers employees the opportunity to work on complex, high‑impact investment solutions in a collaborative, forward‑looking environment—where expertise, partnership, and long‑term thinking truly matter

This role will enable local delivery of our industry-leading TM service to clients domiciled in the KSA, and enhance our global coverage model by adding TM analysts in the Middle East. Expanding our global presence aligns with our continued commitment to providing the highest level of service to our clients.

What You Will Be Responsible For

As a Transition Portfolio Manager, you will:

Utilize your in-depth understanding of the transition life cycle (pre-trade, implementation, and post-trade aspects) to lead portfolio restructures for KSA-domiciled clients including complex multi-manager, multi-asset class events.Support KSA-domiciled clients and our global transition desk with pre- and post-trade analysis and execution reporting; planning and project management including communication across parties (clients, custodians, record-keepers, investment managers and consultants); portfolio management and order generation; collaboration with trading desk on trade strategy and instructions; and monitoring of daily middle office support functions (trade communications, reconciliations, corporate actions).Cover trade reconciliation process between internal systems for accuracy and completeness as needed prior to sending to middle office and custodians to ensure timely settlement of trades.Represent transition and interim exposure management products as a subject matter expert on multi-asset agency trading, exposure management, and hedging strategies.Apply your expertise on benchmarking and measurement of transition and trade execution performance through Transaction Cost Analysis (TCA)Support sales and business development initiatives and contribute to thought leadership on transition, exposure management and trading topicsAssess internal business operations (trading strategies, performance, technology, data management, marketing, research) and develop solutions to enhance product positioningLead and contribute to strategic projects in partnership with other groups / businesses within State Street as well as external vendorsAssist in evolving and industrializing our suite of trading, hedging and exposure analysis tools – including ongoing enhancement of pre- and post-trade analytics and interim exposure management platform – working with our Exposure Solutions team, reporting and analytics specialists, and external vendors


What We Value

The following skills will help you succeed in this role:

Portfolio management, trading and/or operational experience, preferably across multiple asset classesFamiliarity with Charles River Development, Axioma, or other portfolio and order management systems / toolsClient-facing experience leading complex transitions or portfolio implementations; strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skillsTechnical proficiency, with experience using Bloomberg and other market-data sources for analytical purposesSelf-starter with ability to work and problem solve independently under tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment, all while maintaining a critical focus on detailsStrong project management and organizational skills; pro-active in seeking out the next project to help the team


Education & Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, mathematics, economics, finance or similar10+ years of experience in a portfolio management, trading, consulting, analytical or operational roleMBA, graduate degree in a quantitative discipline, or CFA designation preferred


Additional Requirements

CMA registration requiredWillingness to travel up to 10% of the timeExperience with TCA, AI, programming languages, modeling in Excel / VBA and Python, data visualization software, and database infrastructures a plus


About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to

data.title: Require a Chief Financial Officer (CFO) in Jeddah
data.jd_text:
This role will oversee financial strategy, governance, budgeting, and performance management, while supporting business expansion and operational excellence. 
 Develop and execute financial strategies that support business expansion and operational efficiency
  Lead annual budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning processes
  Monitor financial performance and deliver actionable insights to executive leadership
  Ensure robust financial governance, compliance, and risk management practices
  Partner with cross-functional teams to optimize costs and improve profitability
  Provide strategic financial analysis to support key business decisions and initiatives
  Drive continuous improvement in financial reporting and performance management systems
 


Requirements

The ideal candidate brings strong leadership experience within food or FMCG manufacturing, with a proven track record in driving profitability, optimizing costs, and partnering with executive teams to deliver sustainable growth. 
 Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field; CPA or CFA preferred
  3+ years of progressive financial leadership experience in food or FMCG manufacturing
  Proven track record in driving profitability, cost optimization, and financial performance
  Strong expertise in budgeting, forecasting, financial modeling, and performance reporting
  Demonstrated ability to partner effectively with executive teams and influence strategic outcomes
  Advanced proficiency in financial systems and data analytics tools
  Excellent communication, leadership, and problem-solving skills
  Experience supporting business growth initiatives and operational transformation projects

The platform is built, live, and ready to scale. We're looking for a commercially driven leader to join as our Founding CRO. Someone who doesn't just talk about growth, but engineers it from the ground up. Why this isn't a typical early-stage pitch: This is not a slide deck looking for a builder. The platform is fully developed with 12 professional tools, AI-powered evaluation, PMI-standard project health monitoring, digital contracts, and automated invoicing. What we need now is a commercial co-pilot who can turn a strong product into a market leader. There are real leads in the pipeline. There is a clearly defined go-to-market strategy. There is a real market gap validated through direct engagement with project-owning enterprises and execution firms across Saudi Arabia. The Role: You will own the full commercial engine: revenue, partnerships, client acquisition, and market expansion. This is not a support function. This is a founding seat at the table with direct ownership of outcomes. Your core mandate: ▸ Build and execute the commercial strategy from first client to sustainable revenue ▸ Develop strategic partnerships with enterprises, government-linked entities, and key players in the Saudi B2B ecosystem ▸ Design and own the full sales cycle: outreach, qualification, negotiation, and closing ▸ Establish SmartBrick's positioning as the default infrastructure for B2B project execution in the Kingdom ▸ Collaborate directly with the founder on pricing, packaging, and market entry sequencing ▸ Translate market feedback into product priorities that accelerate product-market fit What You Bring: → Deep, hands-on experience in the Saudi market. You understand the business culture, decision-making dynamics, and institutional landscape. → A strong track record in business development, partnerships, or commercial leadership, ideally in SaaS, platforms, or B2B services. → Prior experience in building businesses from early stage. You've been in the room when there was no playbook, and you wrote one. → The ability to open doors, build trust, and sustain long-term relationships with enterprise clients and institutional partners. → Fluency in both Arabic and English. This is non-negotiable given the nature of the market and the partnerships involved. → Comfort operating in a lean, high-ownership environment where your decisions have direct and visible impact. What We Offer: → A meaningful equity stake in a platform with real infrastructure, not a prototype. Structured with a standard 4-year vesting schedule and a 1-year cliff. → Clear, milestone-driven equity terms. Your ownership is tied to measurable outcomes, not promises. → A founding role with full strategic authority over the commercial function. → A platform already built, already tested, and positioned in a high-demand segment of the Saudi market, aligned with Vision 2030's push toward digital transformation and private sector enablement. → A working relationship with a technical founder who built the entire platform independently. Your counterpart is not looking for someone to figure things out; the architecture is done, and the growth seat is yours to own. This role is for someone who: ✓ Wants to build something meaningful in Saudi Arabia's B2B landscape ✓ Thrives in founding environments where ambiguity is the starting point, not the obstacle ✓ Sees equity not as a consolation prize, but as the highest-conviction bet they can make on themselves ✓ Is ready to move. Not in six months, but now. If this reads like your next chapter, we'd like to hear from you. Apply directly or reach out with your

data.jd_text: Who We Are At Inverto in Riyadh, we’re defining what’s next in procurement and supply chain—driven by ambition, precision, and impact. With the strength of BCG behind us and a sharp entrepreneurial mindset at our core, we partner with leading global organizations to deliver strategies that create lasting value. Our people are central to that mission. As experts who move with intent and momentum, we continually invest in developing our capabilities, expanding our reach, and scaling our impact. Growth at Inverto is shared growth-opening new opportunities for our clients, our business, and our teams. What You'll Do The Business & Operations Senior Manager drives the effective operationalization of Inverto’s regional growth agenda by leading key commercial, people, financial, and operational processes for the Middle East. Acting as a strategic partner to Inverto leadership and EMESA teams, the role ensures transparency in business development activities, enables strong go-to-market execution, and supports high-quality decision-making through robust performance and financial management. It plays a central role in shaping and sustaining talent and team development, driving a cohesive and high-performing culture, and ensuring optimal staffing and resource allocation. The position also safeguards operational excellence by strengthening cross-functional collaboration with BCG Core and Inverto EMESA, ensuring reliable data flow, and securing compliance with governance, legal, and internal standards. Additionally, it oversees local office operations and orchestrates shared services and marketing initiatives to enable seamless delivery and reinforce Inverto’s brand presence in the region. A key aspect of the role is ensuring alignment between BCG core and Inverto-specific requirements, in accordance with Inverto’s global guardrails, and business model. Overall, the role is critical in enabling the Middle East business to operate effectively, grow sustainably, and achieve its strategic objectives. Key Responsibilities Lead Management and GTM support Partner with Inverto Managing Directors, Principals, and Inverto EMESA to operationalize the Inverto growth plan and operating model by translating strategic priorities into actionable initiatives and effective execution Liaise with the Inverto Business Excellence team and local Inverto leadership to ensure full transparency in the BD pipeline, including tool management / data accuracy, process alignment, and effective governance of local Inverto leads Enable commercial success by creating visibility into local platforms (e.g., local BCG lead calls), synthesizing insights, and supporting senior stakeholders with actionable steering information People & Talent Management Drive optimal team deployment and resource allocation in close collaboration with the Inverto EMESA team, ensuring sustained delivery capacity and alignment with business needs Shape the Middle East hiring strategy jointly with Inverto EMES

data.title: Traditional Trade Channel Head
data.jd_text:
About the Role:

Ferrero is seeking a dynamic Traditional Trade Channel Head to lead our strategy and growth in the traditional trade sector; covering independent retailers, wholesalers, and neighborhood shops. In this role, you will be responsible for building strong customer relationships, driving perfect store execution, expanding numeric distribution, and delivering commercial excellence across this critical channel.

Main Responsibilities:

As a Traditional Trade Channel Manager, you will develop and execute the Traditional Trade strategy to drive market share and penetration. You will manage relationships with key partners, distributors, and wholesalers to ensure alignment and deliver high service levels throughout the channel. Your responsibilities will include leading field execution plans in general trade outlets to secure product visibility, availability, and pricing compliance. You will also oversee promotional planning and trade investments, optimizing return on investment and maximizing in-market impact.

Collaboration with marketing, category management, and supply chain teams is crucial to support product launches and guarantee consistent activation in the market. Additionally, you will analyze channel performance, customer data, and market trends to recommend and drive growth initiatives. Key performance indicators for this position include volume and revenue growth in traditional trade, numeric distribution and customer expansion, execution compliance for visibility, availability, and pricing, distributor performance and stock availability, as well as the return on investment for trade investments and promotions.

You will lead your team by setting objectives, managing performance, and facilitating training. You are responsible for compliance, efficient resource management, conflict resolution, and fostering a positive, collaborative culture to support productivity and employee growth.

About You:

The ideal candidate will have a bachelor’s degree in business, Sales, or Marketing and at least 10 to 12 years of experience in FMCG sales management, with a particular focus on traditional or general trade.

You have a strong understanding of distributor networks, local market dynamics, and channel execution is required. Experience in sweet snacks, confectionery, or impulse categories is an advantage.

Proficiency in sales analytics, Excel, PowerPoint, and trade spend tools is important for success in this role.

We seek someone with strong interpersonal and negotiation skills, who is commercially driven with a consumer-first mindset. To be successful in this role, you will demonstrate excellent planning, execution, and follow-up abilities. Resilience, adaptability, and proactive work habits are key.

You excel at managing and developing people. Your ability to nurture talent and inspire confidence helps create an environment where individuals thrive and teams achieve remarkable goals.

Our Benefits & Perks:

Careers with caring built in - discover our benefits here.

About Ferrero:

Ferrero began its journey in the small town of Alba in Piedmont, Italy, in 1946. Today, it is one of the world’s largest sweet-packaged food companies, with many iconic brands sold in countries all over the world. Find out more about Ferrero at ferrero.com.

DE&I at Ferrero:

Ferrero is committed to building a diverse and inclusive culture in which all employees feel welcomed and appreciated and have the same opportunities. We believe all our people are equally talented in their own way. In nurturing the curiosity and natural abilities of our employees, we provide them, generation after generation, the means to succeed personally and professionally, enabling them to craft their journey at Ferrero. The diversity of our talents is what makes our work environment multicultural, innovative, and highly rewarding.

Find out more here.
